mvp_scope.rationale: Rollback and webhooks are critical for safe monolith migration

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 on all internal traffic, Keycloak OIDC with namespace-scoped roles, all config values encrypted at rest with Azure Key Vault, audit log immutable and append-only
security_baseline.rationale: Baseline aligned with internal confidential data policy

frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; operators interact via API clients or existing internal tooling

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI, Pydantic v2 for request/response validation
backend_stack.rationale: Team expertise in Python; FastAPI async performance suitable for concurrent config reads

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for versioned config storage; Redis 7 for resolved config caching per service+environment key
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL MVCC fits versioned rows; Redis cache reduces DB read amplification for hot service startups

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps in private VNet, East US 2
hosting_target.rationale: Existing Azure landing zone; Container Apps managed scaling without Kubernetes overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Config version history retained indefinitely for rollback; audit logs retained 2 years; deleted namespace data purged after 90-day grace period
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long retention needed for compliance audit trails and emergency rollback

future_scope.value: Config schema validation with JSON Schema per namespace; diff-based promotion between environments; per-value secret resolution via Azure Key Vault references
future_scope.rationale: Post-migration enhancements

constraints.value: Must migrate all 47 monolith config files within 3-month transition window; must coexist with legacy file-based configs during migration; API P99 read latency under 50ms
constraints.rationale: Migration timeline fixed; SLA required for service startup reliability

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for API latency and error rate metrics; structured JSON logs to Azure Monitor; alerts on rollback events and webhook delivery failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Existing platform observability stack

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with milestone delivery at migration completion checkpoints
execution_preference.rationale: Aligns with monolith migration project cadence

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II controls: change management logging, least-privilege access, encryption at rest and in transit; internal change management policy requires audit trail for all config mutations
compliance_context.rationale: High risk and confidential data sensitivity trigger compliance requirements

project.goal: Versioned config API to replace file-based config distribution in the monolith

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC client credentials and authorization code flows
security.encryption: Azure Key Vault for value encryption at rest

data.cache: Redis 7 for resolved config hot cache

open_questions.schema_validation: Defer JSON Schema validation per namespace to phase 2

confirmed_decisions.config_inheritance: namespace hierarchy with environment override precedence

reasoner_reviews.product: The namespace hierarchy inheritance model correctly addresses the monolith migration case which uses environment-level overrides on top of service defaults. Webhook fanout is essential for warm reloads without service restarts, directly addressing the current deployment pain. Rollback to any prior version must be atomic - partial rollbacks would introduce inconsistency across namespace children.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async request handling will comfortably meet the 50ms P99 read latency target with Redis caching. The versioning model using an append-only config_versions table avoids UPDATE contention and naturally supports point-in-time rollback. Webhook delivery should use an outbox pattern to decouple write commits from fanout latency and ensure at-least-once delivery.
reasoner_reviews.security: All config values must be encrypted at rest using Azure Key Vault envelope encryption. Keycloak namespace-scoped roles must be validated per request to prevent cross-namespace config leakage. The audit log must be INSERT-only with a PostgreSQL trigger policy blocking UPDATE and DELETE even for admin roles.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 50ms P99 read target is achievable with Redis write-through caching but requires cache invalidation to be synchronous on write. The 3-month migration window requires the import job to accept legacy config files from day one of deployment.

backend.service_design: Four FastAPI routers: ConfigReader (GET /{service}/{env}/{namespace}), ConfigWriter (PUT /{service}/{env}/{namespace}), VersionManager (GET/POST versions, POST rollback), WebhookManager (CRUD for webhook registrations). Namespace inheritance resolver implemented as recursive merge with environment precedence.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1. Versioned under /api/v1. Idempotent PUT for config writes with ETag optimistic concurrency. Webhook delivery via outbox table polled by background worker with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue.
backend.failure_handling: Circuit breaker on Redis cache reads with fallback to PostgreSQL. Webhook delivery failures retried up to 5 times; dead-lettered to failed_webhooks table for manual replay. All mutations in PostgreSQL SERIALIZABLE transactions for version integrity.
backend.background_jobs: Webhook outbox processor polls config_change_events every 500ms. Migration import job reads legacy config files from blob storage and creates initial version records.

security.auth_design: Keycloak client credentials grant for service-to-service reads; OIDC authorization code for human operator mutations. Access tokens validated at request middleware with namespace claim extraction.
security.authorization_model: Namespace-scoped roles: config:read for services, config:write for operators, config:admin for namespace creation and webhook management. No role permits cross-namespace reads.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable config_audit_log with INSERT-only trigger policy. Log entries capture actor identity, action type, namespace path, previous version ID, new version ID, timestamp, source IP. Retained 2 years.
security.secrets_management: Config values encrypted with Azure Key Vault AES-256 before INSERT; Key Vault key rotation triggers background re-encryption. API returns plaintext only to authenticated callers.

data.entities: config_namespaces (id UUID PK, service_id VARCHAR, environment ENUM production/staging/dev, namespace_path TEXT, parent_namespace_id UUID FK nullable, created_at). config_entries (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, key VARCHAR, value_encrypted BYTEA, kv_version INT). config_versions (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, snapshot_encrypted BYTEA, version_number INT, created_by, created_at). config_change_events (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, version_id FK, created_at, processed_at nullable). webhook_registrations (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, target_url TEXT, secret_hmac_hash VARCHAR, active BOOL). config_audit_log (id UUID PK, actor_id, action ENUM read/write/rollback, namespace_path, prev_version_id, new_version_id, source_ip INET, ts TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for all config state with append-only versioning. Redis 7 cache keyed by service+environment+namespace with TTL and write-through invalidation. Azure Blob Storage for legacy config file imports.
data.retention_and_deletion: Config version history indefinite retention. Audit logs 2 years. Namespace grace period 90 days before purge.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps East US 2 private VNet. API revision: min 2/max 10 replicas. Webhook-worker revision: min 1/max 3 replicas. PostgreSQL Flexible Server zone-redundant HA. Azure Cache for Redis zone-redundant.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipelines: PR runs pytest with 85% coverage gate and Pydantic schema tests. Main deploys to staging via Container Apps revision swap. Production requires manual approval gate. Alembic migrations as one-off Container Apps jobs before revision swap.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus on /metrics: config_read_latency_seconds histogram, webhook_delivery_failures_total counter, rollback_events_total. Grafana dashboards for namespace read throughput, cache hit rate, webhook fanout lag. Alerts: P99 read >50ms, webhook failure rate >5%, rollback burst >3 in 10min.
devops.rollback_strategy: Container Apps revision traffic split for instant API rollback. PostgreSQL schema migrations forward-only with backward-compatible columns during migration window.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a versioned config API to safely migrate a large monolith off file-based configs. Key decisions: append-only PostgreSQL versioning with SERIALIZABLE transactions for atomic version writes, Redis write-through cache for the 50ms P99 read latency SLA, Keycloak namespace-scoped roles to prevent cross-namespace config leakage, and outbox pattern for decoupled webhook fanout. Migration coexistence requires legacy import job from day one.
target_output.title: Environment Configuration API Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A FastAPI service on Azure Container Apps providing versioned configuration management for internal services migrating away from file-based configs. Supports namespace hierarchy inheritance, atomic versioned writes with rollback, and webhook-driven change notifications. PostgreSQL stores append-only version history; Redis caches resolved configs for sub-50ms P99 reads. Keycloak enforces namespace-scoped access control with immutable audit logging for SOC 2 compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Layered API with FastAPI routers for config read, write, version management, and webhook management. Namespace hierarchy resolver executes recursive merge of parent and child namespace entries with environment precedence, caching the resolved result in Redis. Config writes use PostgreSQL SERIALIZABLE transactions to atomically append a new config_versions snapshot and insert a config_change_event for the outbox processor. Webhook-worker polls the outbox and fans out signed HTTP POST notifications. All config values encrypted with Azure Key Vault before PostgreSQL storage, decrypted only on authenticated reads.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, FastAPI with Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg. PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server with SERIALIZABLE isolation. Redis 7 Azure Cache. Keycloak 23 OIDC. Azure Container Apps, Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault, Azure Blob Storage. Azure DevOps CI/CD. Grafana, Prometheus, Azure Monitor.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Config Read with Namespace Inheritance: services query GET /api/v1/config/{service}/{env}/{namespace} and receive merged key-value map resolving parent defaults with environment overrides. Versioned Config Write: operators PUT new key-value sets creating atomic config_versions snapshots with ETag optimistic locking. Rollback: POST /api/v1/config/{namespace}/rollback?to_version={N} atomically restores specified snapshot and triggers change webhooks. Webhook Management: CRUD for webhook endpoint registrations with HMAC-SHA256 signed payloads. Audit Log: append-only query endpoint. Legacy Import: background job ingests legacy files from blob storage.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps (api revision: FastAPI workers min 2/max 10; webhook-worker revision: outbox processor min 1/max 3). PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (config_namespaces, config_entries, config_versions, config_change_events, webhook_registrations, config_audit_log). Azure Cache for Redis 7 (resolved config hot cache). Azure Key Vault (envelope encryption for config values). Keycloak 23. Azure Blob Storage (legacy config staging). Azure Container Registry.
target_output.workflows: Config Read with Cache: service sends GET /api/v1/config/payment-svc/production/database with Bearer token; Redis cache miss triggers PostgreSQL inheritance resolution query; resolved map cached with 5-minute TTL and returned in under 15ms. Versioned Config Write and Webhook Fanout: operator PUTs updated value; SERIALIZABLE transaction appends new config_entries row, snapshots namespace to config_versions, inserts config_change_event; Redis invalidated synchronously; webhook-worker polls within 500ms, POSTs signed notification to all registered subscribers. Emergency Rollback: SRE calls rollback endpoint; API restores snapshot atomically, inserts audit entry, triggers webhooks; consumers receive and reload config within seconds.
target_output.data_model: config_namespaces (id UUID PK, service_id VARCHAR, environment ENUM production/staging/dev, namespace_path TEXT UNIQUE per service+env, parent_namespace_id UUID FK nullable, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). config_entries (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, key VARCHAR, value_encrypted BYTEA, kv_version INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). config_versions (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, snapshot_encrypted BYTEA, version_number INT NOT NULL, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, rollback_from_version_id UUID nullable). config_change_events (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, version_id FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, processed_at nullable). webhook_registrations (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, target_url TEXT, secret_hmac_hash VARCHAR, active BOOL). config_audit_log (id UUID PK, actor_id VARCHAR, action ENUM read/write/rollback, namespace_path TEXT, prev_version_id UUID nullable, new_version_id UUID nullable, source_ip INET, ts TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful under /api/v1. GET /config/{service}/{env}/{namespace} - resolved config read with Redis cache. PUT /config/{service}/{env}/{namespace} - atomic versioned write with ETag. GET /config/{service}/{env}/{namespace}/versions - version history. POST /config/{service}/{env}/{namespace}/rollback?to_version={N} - atomic restore. GET /config/{service}/{env}/{namespace}/audit - audit log with actor/date filters. GET /webhooks?namespace={ns}. POST /webhooks. DELETE /webhooks/{id}. POST /migrations/import - trigger legacy file import. All require Keycloak Bearer token with namespace-scoped role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC: services use client credentials grant with config:read; operators use authorization code with config:write or config:admin namespace roles. Azure Key Vault AES-256 envelope encryption for all config values at rest; ciphertext only in PostgreSQL. Redis stores resolved plaintext ephemerally; evicted on write. Webhook payloads signed with HMAC-SHA256 per-endpoint secret. config_audit_log INSERT-only via PostgreSQL trigger policy blocking UPDATE/DELETE. SOC 2: change management trail, namespace RBAC, TLS 1.3 in transit, Key Vault key rotation schedule.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps East US 2 private VNet. API revision: min 2/max 10, scales on HTTP request count. Webhook-worker: min 1/max 3, scales on config_change_events queue depth. PostgreSQL Flexible Server zone-redundant HA, 6-hour automated backups. Azure Cache for Redis zone-redundant. Azure DevOps CI: pytest 85% coverage gate, Pydantic schema contract tests, Bandit security scan. CD: staging via Container Apps revision swap; production requires manual approval with release notes. Alembic migrations as one-off Container Apps jobs before revision swap. Secrets via Azure Key Vault references on Container Apps managed identity.
target_output.observability: Prometheus /metrics: config_read_latency_seconds histogram (labels: service, environment, cache_hit), webhook_delivery_failures_total (label: namespace), rollback_events_total, config_write_latency_seconds. Grafana: namespace read throughput, cache hit rate per service, webhook fanout lag, rollback frequency. Alerts: P99 read >50ms, webhook failure rate >5% in 5min, rollback burst >3 in 10min, PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion. Azure Monitor Log Analytics for SOC 2 audit trail queries.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $400/month for Container Apps baseline (2 api + 1 worker), PostgreSQL Flexible Server Burstable, and Azure Cache for Redis Basic. Redis absorbs 90%+ of config read volume during normal service startups. Webhook-worker scales to 3 replicas only during high-volume config push events. Migration import job billed per execution second as a transient one-off.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Core API and Migration Foundation (5 weeks): config namespace CRUD, versioned read/write with Redis caching, Keycloak auth, audit log, legacy config file import job, Azure DevOps CI/CD, staging environment. Phase 2 - Rollback and Webhooks (3 weeks): atomic rollback endpoint, webhook registration and HMAC-signed delivery, outbox processor, webhook dead-letter replay API, Grafana dashboards. Phase 3 - Migration Completion (4 weeks): onboard all 47 monolith config namespaces, validate P99 SLA under production load, remove legacy file mounts from monolith, SOC 2 evidence package.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 strict type annotations enforced by mypy in CI. Pydantic v2 models for all request/response shapes. SQLAlchemy 2.0 async sessions; no raw SQL outside migration scripts. All PostgreSQL version writes use SERIALIZABLE isolation explicitly. Redis cache keys follow config:{service}:{env}:{namespace} pattern with mandatory TTL. No secrets in code; all credentials via Azure Key Vault references. pytest with 85% coverage gate; contract tests validate OpenAPI spec against live FastAPI app.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis cache stampede during service mass-restart after config change webhook; mitigated by staggered webhook delivery with jitter and cache pre-warming before fanout. Risk: PostgreSQL version table growth under high-frequency writes; mitigated by background compaction archiving versions older than 6 months to blob storage. Tradeoff: SERIALIZABLE isolation for version writes vs throughput; acceptable given config write frequency is low compared to reads. Risk: Key Vault latency spike on cache miss; mitigated by encrypting at write time, never in the hot Redis read path.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Redis write-through invalidation over TTL-only to ensure rollback changes immediately visible to restarting services. Chose append-only config_versions over UPDATE-in-place to preserve full rollback history. Confirmed SERIALIZABLE isolation after testing showed no throughput degradation at expected write frequency. Legacy import uses blob storage staging to decouple from monolith deploy schedule.

metadata.notes: Config API migration from file-based legacy system with atomic versioning, rollback, and webhook fanout on Azure.

profile.domain: Engineering Operations / Release Management

project_goal.value: Mobile-first deployment tracking app replacing a spreadsheet-based internal release board, enabling engineering managers and QA leads to monitor live deployment status, approve staging promotions, and view rollback history across all environments from iOS and Android
project_goal.rationale: Spreadsheet release board creates coordination friction and lacks real-time status visibility during incidents

target_users.value: Engineering managers, QA leads, and release engineers who need real-time deployment visibility and approval authority on mobile devices
target_users.rationale: These roles are often away from desks during deployment windows and incident response

project_class.rationale: Flutter app for iOS and Android with a Go backend API integrating with existing CD tooling

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Flutter UI, Go Gin API, PostgreSQL, Firebase Auth, CD system integrations, and real-time push for deployment events

complexity_level.rationale: CD system integration and real-time push notifications add moderate complexity; core data model is straightforward

risk_level.rationale: Approval authority over production deployments from mobile carries operational risk; approval actions must be audited

data_sensitivity.rationale: Deployment metadata and artifact links are internal operational data; not customer PII

external_exposure.rationale: Firebase Auth required; API accessible only to authenticated employees

access_model.value: Firebase Auth with role claims: release_engineer (read + deployment event post), qa_lead (staging promotion approval), engineering_manager (all approvals + rollback initiation)
access_model.rationale: Approval authority scoped by role prevents unauthorized production promotions

feature_scope.value: Deployment event feed, environment stage view, staging-to-production promotion approval with linked test reports, rollback initiation for health-check failures, push notifications for deployment events and approval requests, deployment history and audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Full deployment lifecycle visibility from event receipt through approval to rollback

mvp_scope.value: Deployment event push notifications, environment stage cards, promotion approval with comment and audit log, rollback initiation, Firebase Auth with role enforcement
mvp_scope.rationale: Core approval and rollback workflows replace the critical spreadsheet processes for the pilot team

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT on every API request, role-based authorization for approval and rollback endpoints, all approval and rollback actions written to immutable audit log
security_baseline.rationale: Approval authority for production deployments requires strong auth and audit trail

frontend_stack.rationale: Single codebase for iOS and Android; strong state management with Riverpod; native push notification support

backend_stack.rationale: Existing CD integration scripts are in Go; team expertise and fast API response times

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL on GCP aligns with existing GCP infrastructure

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for Go API; FCM for push notifications; Flutter app distributed via TestFlight and Google Play Internal Testing
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run for serverless scale; FCM as the authoritative push delivery channel for both iOS and Android

privacy_retention_policy.value: Deployment records retained 2 years for audit purposes; rollback events retained indefinitely; push notification tokens rotated on each app launch
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 2-year deployment audit trail supports post-incident reviews and compliance queries

future_scope.value: Slack integration for approval requests, deployment SLO tracking with trend charts, multi-CD system support beyond the current tooling
future_scope.rationale: Slack integration is the most requested feature from release engineers

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing CD tooling via webhook; must not introduce a new approval bottleneck longer than 2 minutes for on-call scenarios
constraints.rationale: Webhook integration reuses existing CD event infrastructure; 2-minute approval SLO prevents deployment delays

observability_baseline.value: Grafana plus Prometheus for API latency and push notification delivery rate; structured logging for promotion approval and rollback events
observability_baseline.rationale: Push notification delivery rate is the critical reliability metric for this app

execution_preference.value: Parallel mobile and backend development with weekly integration milestones; TestFlight beta for pilot team from week 4
execution_preference.rationale: Early TestFlight access catches mobile UX issues before the pilot team depends on the app

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: promotion approval and rollback initiation events logged in immutable audit trail; role-based access controls documented; internal change management policy compliance
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=medium triggers compliance context; approval authority over production deployments is in scope for change management audit

project.goal: Real-time mobile deployment tracking and approval platform replacing spreadsheet release board
project.target_users: Engineering managers, QA leads, release engineers
project.timeline: TestFlight beta in 4 weeks, MVP production in 8 weeks

backend.api: REST with JSON, Firebase JWT validation middleware

security.auth: Firebase Auth JWT validated on every API request
security.authz: Role claims from Firebase custom claims for approval and rollback authorization

data.cache: Redis 7 for deployment event state cache

constraints.integration: Webhook receiver for existing CD tooling
constraints.approval_slo: Under 2 minutes from notification to approval action

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope correctly focuses on the three highest-friction points of the spreadsheet process: deployment visibility, promotion approval, and rollback initiation. The 2-minute approval SLO is the right forcing function for the push notification design. Rollback should be surfaced as a critical action card, not buried in a history list.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Cloud Run is the right choice for webhook receiver and API given the bursty event pattern during deployment windows. The CD webhook receiver should be a lightweight endpoint that writes to a queue or directly to Postgres to avoid blocking the CD pipeline. Firebase custom claims for role enforcement is clean; custom claims are set at user creation and checked in Gin middleware. Redis for deployment event state cache avoids redundant CD API calls during active deployment windows.
reasoner_reviews.security: Firebase Auth JWT validation in Gin middleware is standard and well-tested. Role claims for approval and rollback must be validated server-side, never trusted from the mobile client. The audit log for approvals and rollbacks should be append-only with timestamps and approver identity. Push notification tokens must not persist beyond a session since they can be used to send arbitrary notifications.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: FCM delivery is best-effort; a fallback polling endpoint is needed for cases where push delivery fails. The 2-minute approval SLO is achievable with FCM high-priority messages and a single-tap approval action in the notification payload. Webhook integration from existing CD tooling needs to handle retry on API 5xx to avoid losing deployment events.

backend.service_design: Gin app on Cloud Run with modules: webhook_receiver (CD event ingestion), deployments (deployment record CRUD and status), promotions (staging-to-production approval workflow), rollbacks (rollback initiation and status), notifications (FCM dispatch), auth (Firebase JWT validation middleware). Separate Cloud Run service or Cloud Tasks queue for async FCM dispatch to avoid blocking the webhook receiver response.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/webhooks/cd receives CD deployment events, validates HMAC signature, upserts deployment_records, queues FCM notification. GET /v1/deployments?env=&status= returns current deployment state per environment. GET /v1/deployments/:id returns deployment detail with artifact_link and test_report_url. POST /v1/promotions/:deployment_id/approve requires qa_lead or engineering_manager role. POST /v1/rollbacks/:deployment_id/initiate requires engineering_manager role. GET /v1/history?env=&from=&to= returns paginated deployment history.
backend.failure_handling: Webhook receiver returns 200 immediately on event receipt before processing to unblock the CD pipeline; event queued for async processing. FCM delivery failures retried up to 3 times via Cloud Tasks with exponential backoff. Approval endpoint idempotent on duplicate approval submission returns 409 with current status.
backend.scaling_notes: Cloud Run scales to zero between deployment windows. Min instances set to 1 for production to eliminate cold start during time-critical approval scenarios. Redis cache reduces Cloud SQL load during active deployment windows with multiple engineers querying simultaneously.

frontend.app_structure: Flutter 3.19 app with Riverpod providers for deployment state, notification handling, and auth state. Screens: deployment feed, environment stage board, deployment detail, promotion approval, rollback confirmation, history.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Deployment Feed: scrollable list of recent deployment events across all environments with status badges (deploying/success/failed/rolled_back). Environment Stage Board: kanban-style view showing current artifact version per stage (dev/staging/prod). Deployment Detail: artifact version, commit SHA, linked test reports, deploy log URL, approval status, promote or rollback action buttons gated by role. Promotion Approval: required comment field plus confirm button with role-gated visibility. Rollback Confirmation: shows current version, target rollback version, and single-tap confirm with biometric authentication.
frontend.state_management: Riverpod AsyncNotifier providers for deployment list and environment state. StreamProvider for Firebase Auth state. Local notification handler updates deployment_records in Riverpod state on FCM foreground message receipt. Background FCM handler updates local SQLite cache for offline-capable history view.

security.auth_design: Firebase Auth with Google SSO for corporate accounts. Firebase custom claims set on user creation: role ENUM release_engineer/qa_lead/engineering_manager. JWT included in every API request Authorization header. Gin middleware calls Firebase Admin SDK to validate token and extract role claims on each request.
security.authorization_model: Role enforcement in Gin middleware before handler: approval endpoints require qa_lead or engineering_manager claim, rollback endpoints require engineering_manager claim. 403 returned with no detail on role mismatch. All authorization checks server-side; Flutter UI hides buttons for unauthorized roles but never trusts client-side role state for security decisions.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: promotion_approvals table (id UUID, deployment_id FK, approver_user_id, role_at_approval, comment, approved_at) is INSERT-only. rollback_events table (id UUID, deployment_id FK, initiator_user_id, role_at_initiation, reason, initiated_at, completed_at, status) is INSERT-only. Both tables have no UPDATE grant for application DB role.

data.entities: deployment_records (id UUID PK, environment ENUM dev/staging/production, service_name VARCHAR, artifact_version VARCHAR, commit_sha VARCHAR, artifact_link VARCHAR, test_report_url VARCHAR, status ENUM deploying/success/failed/rolled_back, cd_event_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). promotion_approvals (id UUID PK, deployment_id FK, approver_user_id VARCHAR, role_at_approval VARCHAR, comment TEXT, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). rollback_events (id UUID PK, deployment_id FK, target_deployment_id FK, initiator_user_id VARCHAR, role_at_initiation VARCHAR, reason TEXT, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/completed/failed). fcm_device_tokens (id UUID PK, user_id VARCHAR, fcm_token VARCHAR NOT NULL, platform ENUM ios/android, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen TIMESTAMPTZ). release_trains (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, services TEXT[], created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 for all operational data. Redis 7 (Cloud Memorystore) for deployment_records state cache with 5-minute TTL per environment. Local Flutter SQLite via sqflite for offline-capable deployment history cache.
data.consistency_notes: Webhook receiver uses PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT(cd_event_id) DO UPDATE for idempotent event ingestion. Promotion approval uses SELECT FOR UPDATE on deployment_records to prevent double-approval race.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run services: deployment-api (main API), webhook-receiver (lightweight CD event ingestion). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL in private VPC with Cloud Run VPC connector. Cloud Memorystore Redis in same VPC. Cloud Tasks queue for async FCM dispatch. Firebase project for Auth and FCM.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go vet, golangci-lint, go test with race detector, Docker build and Artifact Registry push, Cloud Run deploy. Flutter: flutter test, flutter build iOS and Android, TestFlight upload via fastlane on tag push. GitHub Environments for staging and production approval gates.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana plus Prometheus: api_request_duration_seconds by endpoint, fcm_dispatch_success_rate, promotion_approval_latency_seconds (webhook receipt to approval action), rollback_initiation_count. Cloud Run request logs for webhook receiver. Structured JSON logs for all approval and rollback events.

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a Flutter deployment tracking app on GCP. Key decisions: Cloud Run for bursty webhook-driven workload with min_instances=1 for the production API to eliminate cold start during time-critical approval windows. Firebase Auth custom claims for role enforcement server-side in Gin middleware. Async FCM dispatch via Cloud Tasks to unblock the webhook receiver response to the CD pipeline. Redis cache for deployment state reduces Cloud SQL load during busy deployment windows with multiple engineers querying simultaneously.
target_output.title: Deployment Tracker Mobile App - Real-Time Release Board Replacement
target_output.executive_summary: A Flutter 3.19 mobile application for iOS and Android that replaces a spreadsheet-based release board with real-time deployment event tracking, environment stage visibility, and mobile-native approval workflows for staging-to-production promotions and rollback initiation. A Go 1.22 Gin API on GCP Cloud Run receives CD system webhooks, persists deployment records to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, and dispatches FCM push notifications to the Flutter app. Engineering managers and QA leads approve promotions or initiate rollbacks with role enforcement in Gin middleware backed by Firebase Auth custom claims; all approval and rollback actions are written to append-only audit tables.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven architecture with two Cloud Run services: a webhook receiver that accepts CD pipeline event POSTs, validates HMAC signatures, and enqueues FCM dispatch via Cloud Tasks; and a deployment API that serves the Flutter app with deployment state, environment boards, and approval/rollback endpoints. Both services connect to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 via VPC connector and Redis 7 (Cloud Memorystore) for deployment state caching. Firebase Auth validates JWT tokens on every API request; custom claims encode the user role for server-side authorization. FCM delivers high-priority push notifications to Flutter app instances with notification payloads containing deep link URLs to the relevant deployment detail screen. Local SQLite in the Flutter app provides an offline-capable history cache.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.19 with Dart, Riverpod for state management, firebase_messaging for FCM, firebase_auth for auth, sqflite for local cache, go_router for navigation. API: Go 1.22 with Gin, firebase-admin-go for JWT validation, pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL, go-redis for Redis, net/http for Cloud Tasks enqueue. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with VPC connector. Cache: Cloud Memorystore Redis 7. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run, Cloud Tasks for async FCM dispatch, Firebase project for Auth and FCM, Artifact Registry for container images. CI: GitHub Actions with fastlane for iOS/Android distribution.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Deployment Event Feed: real-time list of deployment events across all services and environments, updated via FCM foreground messages and background sync. Environment Stage Board: kanban-style view of current artifact version per stage (dev/staging/production) with health indicator. Deployment Detail: artifact version, commit SHA, linked pre-deployment test report, deploy log URL, time in current status, and role-gated action buttons for promotion approval and rollback. Promotion Approval: required comment field with confirm action; approved_at and approver identity logged to promotion_approvals. Rollback Initiation: current vs. target version comparison with biometric-confirmed single-tap; rollback event logged to rollback_events. Push Notifications: high-priority FCM messages for new deployments reaching staging, health check failures, and pending approval requests with direct deep link to action screen.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run webhook-receiver service: Gin handler validating CD HMAC signature, upserting deployment_records, enqueueing Cloud Tasks FCM job. Cloud Run deployment-api service with min_instances=1 in production: Gin API for Flutter app consumption. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 in private VPC with VPC connector for Cloud Run access. Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 for deployment state cache with 5-minute TTL per environment key. Cloud Tasks queue for async FCM dispatch with retry. Firebase project providing Auth user management, custom claims management, and FCM message delivery for iOS (APNs relay) and Android. Flutter app with Riverpod state, local SQLite offline cache, and biometric auth for rollback confirmation.
target_output.workflows: CD Deployment Event Ingestion: CD pipeline POSTs deployment event to webhook-receiver with HMAC-SHA256 signature; receiver validates signature against shared secret; upserts deployment_records using ON CONFLICT(cd_event_id) DO UPDATE for idempotency; enqueues Cloud Tasks FCM job with deployment_id and notification type; returns 200 immediately. FCM Dispatch: Cloud Tasks worker fetches deployment_record and relevant user FCM tokens from fcm_device_tokens; constructs high-priority FCM message with notification title, body, and data payload containing deployment_id deep link; dispatches via Firebase Admin SDK; retries up to 3 times on delivery failure; marks dispatch status. Promotion Approval: engineering manager or QA lead receives FCM notification for pending staging deployment; taps deep link to deployment detail screen; reviews linked test_report_url; taps Approve with required comment; Flutter calls POST /v1/promotions/:id/approve with Firebase JWT; Gin middleware validates JWT and checks role claim; handler uses SELECT FOR UPDATE to prevent double-approval race; inserts promotion_approvals record; updates deployment status to promotion_approved; sends FCM confirmation to release engineer. Rollback Initiation: automated health check failure triggers FCM critical alert to engineering_manager; manager opens rollback screen showing current version vs. previous stable artifact; biometric auth confirmation required; Flutter calls POST /v1/rollbacks/:id/initiate; Gin validates engineering_manager role claim; inserts rollback_events record with status pending; triggers rollback webhook to CD system; updates to completed or failed on CD callback.
target_output.data_model: deployment_records (id UUID PK, environment environment_enum NOT NULL, service_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, artifact_version VARCHAR NOT NULL, commit_sha VARCHAR, artifact_link VARCHAR, test_report_url VARCHAR, status deployment_status_enum NOT NULL DEFAULT deploying, cd_event_id VARCHAR UNIQUE NOT NULL, triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ; INDEX on environment, status for board queries). promotion_approvals (id UUID PK, deployment_id UUID FK NOT NULL, approver_user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, role_at_approval VARCHAR NOT NULL, comment TEXT NOT NULL, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL; INSERT-only app role grant). rollback_events (id UUID PK, deployment_id UUID FK NOT NULL, target_deployment_id UUID FK, initiator_user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, role_at_initiation VARCHAR NOT NULL, reason TEXT, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status rollback_status_enum NOT NULL; INSERT-only app role grant). fcm_device_tokens (id UUID PK, user_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, fcm_token VARCHAR NOT NULL UNIQUE, platform platform_enum NOT NULL, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_seen TIMESTAMPTZ). release_trains (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR NOT NULL, services TEXT[] NOT NULL, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/webhooks/cd validates HMAC signature, upserts deployment_record, returns 200 immediately; requires CD webhook shared secret in X-CD-Signature header. GET /v1/deployments returns current deployment_records grouped by environment with Redis cache (5-min TTL). GET /v1/deployments/:id returns full deployment detail including artifact_link, test_report_url, promotion approval status. POST /v1/promotions/:deployment_id/approve body={comment} requires qa_lead or engineering_manager role; idempotent on repeat call. POST /v1/rollbacks/:deployment_id/initiate body={reason} requires engineering_manager role. GET /v1/history?env=&from=&to=&service= paginated deployment history. POST /v1/devices/token registers FCM token with platform. All endpoints except webhook require Firebase JWT in Authorization header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firebase Auth: Flutter app authenticates via Google SSO; Firebase custom claims set at provisioning time encode role ENUM; Gin middleware calls firebase-admin-go VerifyIDToken on every request and extracts role claim for authorization. Role enforcement: approval endpoint checks qa_lead or engineering_manager claim; rollback endpoint checks engineering_manager claim; 403 with no role detail on mismatch. Flutter UI hides action buttons by role but server-side enforcement is authoritative. Audit trail: promotion_approvals and rollback_events tables have INSERT-only app DB role grant; Postgres triggers prevent UPDATE and DELETE; records capture user_id, role_at_time, timestamp, and action detail for SOC 2 change management audit. Webhook HMAC: CD webhooks validated with HMAC-SHA256 using shared secret from Secret Manager before any processing. Push token hygiene: fcm_device_tokens rotated on each app launch; tokens inactive for 30 days purged by scheduled Cloud Run job.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: go vet, golangci-lint, go test with race detector, Docker build, Artifact Registry push, Cloud Run deploy with traffic split for canary. Flutter pipeline: flutter test, flutter build apk and ipa, Fastlane TestFlight upload on tag push. Environments: dev (Cloud Run single region, Cloud SQL shared dev instance), staging (Cloud Run multi-region, dedicated Cloud SQL), production (Cloud Run multi-region with min_instances=1, Cloud SQL HA, Redis HA). Secrets in Secret Manager: DB password, Redis password, Firebase service account, CD webhook shared secret. Cloud Run IAM service accounts with least-privilege binding to Secret Manager and Cloud SQL.
target_output.observability: Grafana plus Prometheus via Cloud Run custom metrics: api_request_duration_seconds by endpoint and status code, fcm_dispatch_attempts counter, fcm_dispatch_success_rate gauge (target >99.5%), promotion_approval_latency_seconds histogram (time from webhook receipt to approval action; SLO <120s), rollback_initiation_count counter by service. Structured JSON logs for all approval and rollback events including approver_id, role, and deployment_id. Alerts: fcm_dispatch_success_rate < 99% for 10 minutes (PagerDuty P2), approval latency p95 > 120 seconds (Slack notification to release-eng channel), Cloud SQL connection pool exhaustion (PagerDuty P1).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run deployment-api at min_instances=1 approximately $20/month baseline plus burst. Cloud Run webhook-receiver scale-to-zero approximately $5/month. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL db.n1.standard-2 HA approximately $200/month. Cloud Memorystore Redis basic-tier approximately $40/month. Firebase Auth and FCM at this scale is included in Spark plan. Total approximately $280/month. Scale path: Cloud Run auto-scales on concurrency target 80; Cloud SQL read replica for history query load at higher team count.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): CD webhook receiver with HMAC validation, deployment event feed, environment stage board, staging promotion approval with Firebase role enforcement, rollback initiation for engineering managers, FCM push notifications for deployment events and approval requests, append-only audit tables, TestFlight and Google Play Internal Testing distribution. Phase 2 Enhancements (4 weeks): release train grouping, test report inline preview in deployment detail, deployment SLO trend charts, FCM fallback polling endpoint for missed push delivery. Phase 3 Integrations (roadmap): Slack notification integration for approval requests, multi-CD system adapter, deployment cost attribution per service per deploy.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint. Firebase JWT validation must be covered by middleware integration tests using a mock Firebase Admin SDK. Approval and rollback endpoints must have role-enforcement integration tests asserting 403 for unauthorized roles. No deployment artifact_link or test_report_url logged at INFO level to avoid internal URL leakage. Flutter: Riverpod providers unit tested for deployment state transitions. Biometric auth required for rollback confirmation in production build; bypassed only in debug mode with explicit flag. All DB migrations via golang-migrate applied as Cloud Run job pre-deploy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: FCM delivery failure during a critical production deployment leaves approvers without notification. Mitigation: fallback polling endpoint GET /v1/notifications/pending checked by Flutter app on foreground resume; Cloud Tasks retry for FCM dispatch; Apple critical alert entitlement requested for rollback alerts on iOS. Risk: Cloud Run cold start on webhook receiver during initial burst of deployment events at start of deployment window. Mitigation: min_instances=1 for production webhook-receiver during business hours via Cloud Scheduler toggle. Tradeoff: Firebase custom claims for role enforcement is simple but requires re-provisioning if role changes; acceptable given infrequent role changes. Risk: ON CONFLICT idempotency in webhook ingestion assumes cd_event_id is stable across retries; any CD tooling that regenerates event IDs on retry will create duplicate records.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Cloud Run over GKE given bursty webhook pattern and zero-maintenance preference. Confirmed Firebase Auth over Keycloak to reuse existing Firebase project and avoid additional IdP. Confirmed Cloud Tasks for async FCM over direct dispatch to keep webhook receiver p99 under 100ms. Confirmed biometric auth for rollback confirmation to reduce accidental production rollback risk on mobile.

metadata.notes: First-pass strong plan; all domain workflows specific to deployment tracking and approval; biometric rollback confirmation and FCM delivery resilience well-addressed.

profile.domain: Developer Tooling / Feature Flag Management

project_goal.value: CLI tool wrapping the internal feature flag management API, allowing developers to toggle flags, list active experiments, and override flag values per user segment from the terminal without visiting the web dashboard
project_goal.rationale: Dashboard round-trips during development slow down feature flag iteration; CLI enables scripting and automation

target_users.value: Software engineers using terminal workflows to manage feature flags during development and QA testing
target_users.rationale: CLI consumers are already in the terminal; eliminating context switches reduces friction

project_class.rationale: Standalone Go binary with subcommands, no server runtime or web UI

capabilities.rationale: CLI binary logic and feature flag API integration only; no frontend, auth service, or database

complexity_level.rationale: Single-binary CLI with a small subcommand surface; state is limited to a local config file

risk_level.rationale: CLI can toggle flags in production environments; accidental or unauthorized flag changes carry operational risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Feature flag names and segment rules are internal configuration data

external_exposure.rationale: API calls authenticated with a stored token; production operations require explicit environment flag

access_model.value: Per-environment API tokens stored in a local config file (~/.flagctl/config.yaml) with file permission 0600; production token requires explicit --env=production flag to prevent accidental prod mutations
access_model.rationale: Environment-explicit flag prevents engineers from accidentally toggling production flags while targeting staging

feature_scope.value: flag toggle, flag list with status and rollout percentage, per-user-segment override, override removal, environment profile management, JSON output mode, human-readable output mode
feature_scope.rationale: Core CRUD operations against the feature flag API with environment-aware profiles

mvp_scope.value: flagctl toggle, flagctl list, flagctl override set, flagctl override remove, flagctl env add/list, JSON and human-readable output, --env flag for environment selection
mvp_scope.rationale: Covers the three most frequent developer operations against the flag API

security_baseline.value: API tokens stored with file permission 0600; never printed to stdout or stderr; TLS 1.3 for API calls; --dry-run flag shows planned API call without executing for production safety
security_baseline.rationale: Token protection and dry-run mode are the minimum safety controls for a CLI with production write access

frontend_stack.value: none - CLI binary only, output to stdout/stderr
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; all output is terminal text or JSON to stdout

backend_stack.rationale: Go produces small static binaries; cobra for subcommand parsing; no runtime dependency

data_platform.value: none - no database; local config in ~/.flagctl/config.yaml; no persistent state beyond config
data_platform.rationale: CLI is stateless; the feature flag API is the source of truth; no local DB required

hosting_target.value: Distributed as a static Go binary via GitHub Releases; installable via Homebrew tap for macOS; manual download for Linux and Windows
hosting_target.rationale: GitHub Releases provides versioned binary hosting without a deployment infrastructure requirement

privacy_retention_policy.value: No data retention; CLI makes ephemeral API calls; local config file contains only tokens and env URLs, no flag values or segment data
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Stateless CLI has no retention obligation

future_scope.value: flagctl audit subcommand reading flag change history from API, shell completion for flag names via API autocomplete endpoint, CI mode for scripted flag operations in pipelines
future_scope.rationale: Audit and shell completion are the most frequently requested enhancements from pilot users

constraints.value: Single static binary with no runtime dependencies; config file must not be committed to source control; production operations must require explicit --env=production flag
constraints.rationale: Zero-dependency install and production safety guards are baseline requirements for developer tool adoption

observability_baseline.value: none - CLI is a local binary; API server-side observability is out of scope for this tool
observability_baseline.rationale: CLI observability is not relevant; the feature flag API has its own observability

execution_preference.value: Single implementation sprint with GitHub Releases binary distribution and Homebrew tap on completion
execution_preference.rationale: Simple scope suits a focused delivery without phased rollout

compliance_context.value: Internal security baseline: API tokens stored at 0600 permissions; production write operations require explicit environment flag; no flag values or segment data stored locally
compliance_context.rationale: risk_level=medium with internal data sensitivity triggers compliance context for token handling and production access controls

project.goal: Feature flag management CLI wrapping the internal flag API

backend.framework: cobra for subcommand parsing, viper for config
backend.output: stdout human-readable and JSON via --output=json flag

security.production_guard: --env=production explicit flag required for write operations

data.state: Local config file only; no database

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for binary build and release

constraints.binary: Static single binary no runtime deps

reasoner_reviews.product: The subcommand structure is well-targeted at the three most frequent operations. The JSON output mode is essential for scripting in CI pipelines. The --dry-run flag for production safety is a good design choice that will prevent accidental flag mutations during development testing.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Cobra plus Viper is the standard Go CLI stack and appropriate here. The config file at ~/.flagctl/config.yaml with 0600 permissions is a well-established pattern for CLI token storage. The API client should respect HTTP timeouts and return actionable error messages when the flag API is unreachable rather than hanging.
reasoner_reviews.security: Token storage at 0600 is correct. The CLI should also warn at startup if the config file has looser permissions than 0600. The --env=production guard must be enforced at the command dispatch layer, not left to the user to remember. Consider a confirmation prompt for destructive operations on production flags.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Static binary with no runtime dependencies is achievable in Go with CGO_ENABLED=0 and GOOS/GOARCH cross-compilation. Homebrew tap requires a formula file and SHA256 hash of each release binary.

backend.service_design: Single Go binary structured as: cmd/ package with cobra root command and subcommands (toggle, list, override, env), internal/api/ package for the feature flag HTTP client, internal/config/ package for ~/.flagctl/config.yaml read/write with permission check, internal/output/ package for human-readable table formatter and JSON marshaller. No server process; binary executes, calls API, prints result, exits.
backend.api_patterns: Feature flag API client calls: GET /v1/flags?env=:env lists all flags with name, enabled, rollout_pct, segment_count. PATCH /v1/flags/:name/toggle?env=:env toggles enabled state. POST /v1/flags/:name/overrides creates per-user-segment override. DELETE /v1/flags/:name/overrides/:segment removes override. All calls include Authorization: Bearer :token header from config. HTTP client timeout 10 seconds.
backend.failure_handling: HTTP 401 prints 'Invalid or expired API token for environment :env. Run flagctl env add to reconfigure.' HTTP 403 prints 'You do not have write permission for environment :env.' HTTP 404 on flag name prints 'Flag :name not found in :env. Use flagctl list to see available flags.' Network timeout prints 'Feature flag API unreachable. Check your VPN connection.' All errors exit with code 1.

security.secrets_management: Config file written with os.WriteFile using 0600 permissions. On each startup, CLI checks config file permissions with os.Stat and warns if permissions are broader than 0600. API tokens never printed to stdout, stderr, or debug logs. --dry-run flag prints the HTTP method, URL, and request body (with token redacted) without executing the call.
security.authorization_model: Production environment writes require --env=production explicit flag; CLI returns an error if the user attempts a write operation (toggle, override set/remove) without the explicit environment flag and the configured env maps to a URL containing 'production'. Destructive operations (override remove, flag disable) prompt for confirmation unless --yes flag is provided.

data.entities: PostgreSQL database with tables: feature_flags (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, environment VARCHAR, enabled BOOLEAN, rollout_pct INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ), flag_overrides (id UUID PK, flag_id FK, segment_id VARCHAR, override_value BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ), audit_log (id UUID PK, flag_id FK, action VARCHAR, actor VARCHAR, old_value JSONB, new_value JSONB, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.schema_notes: Using GORM ORM for database access. Migrations managed by golang-migrate. Database connection string stored in environment variables.
data.retention_and_deletion: Flag records soft-deleted. Audit log retained indefinitely.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions cross-compilation matrix: GOOS=darwin/linux/windows GOARCH=amd64/arm64. goreleaser for multi-platform binary packaging and GitHub Release creation with SHA256 checksums. Homebrew tap repo updated automatically by goreleaser on release tag.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go vet, golangci-lint, go test with race detector, goreleaser build and release on semver tag push. Integration tests mock the flag API using httptest.NewServer. Binary size check fails build if binary exceeds 20MB.
devops.rollback_strategy: Previous version always downloadable from GitHub Releases. Homebrew formula can be pinned to a specific version. No server-side state to roll back.

target_output.thinking_summary: CLI tool for feature flag management. Simple cobra-based binary with API client, config management, and output formatting. The stateless design with no local database is correct for this scope.
target_output.title: Feature Flag CLI Tool - Terminal Interface for Flag Management API
target_output.executive_summary: A standalone Go 1.22 CLI binary named flagctl that wraps the internal feature flag management API, exposing subcommands for toggling flags, listing active experiments with rollout percentages, and managing per-user-segment overrides across multiple environment profiles. Configuration stored in a local YAML file with 0600 permissions protects API tokens. The binary is distributed via GitHub Releases and a Homebrew tap with zero runtime dependencies.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single static Go binary with no server runtime, no database, and no web UI. The binary is structured around cobra subcommands with a shared Viper config layer reading from ~/.flagctl/config.yaml. On each invocation the binary reads the target environment from --env flag, loads the corresponding API token and base URL from config, constructs the appropriate HTTP request to the feature flag API, and prints the result to stdout in human-readable table or JSON format. Production write operations require an explicit --env=production flag and a confirmation prompt unless --yes is passed. The binary is cross-compiled for macOS arm64/amd64, Linux amd64/arm64, and Windows amd64 via goreleaser in GitHub Actions.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 with CGO_ENABLED=0 for fully static binaries. cobra v1 for subcommand routing and flag parsing. Viper for ~/.flagctl/config.yaml read/write. tablewriter for human-readable stdout tables. Standard library net/http with 10-second timeout for API client. goreleaser for cross-platform binary packaging and GitHub Release creation. Homebrew tap updated automatically on release. GitHub Actions for CI and release pipeline.
target_output.functional_feature_map: flagctl list --env=staging lists all feature flags with name, enabled state, rollout percentage, and segment override count. flagctl toggle :flag_name --env=staging enables or disables a flag. flagctl override set :flag_name --segment=:id --value=true --env=staging creates a per-segment override. flagctl override remove :flag_name --segment=:id --env=production requires confirmation. flagctl env add --name=staging --url=https://flags.internal/api --token=:token stores a named environment profile in config. flagctl env list shows configured environments with URL but masked token. All commands support --output=json for machine-readable stdout.
target_output.system_components: cmd/ package: cobra root command with persistent --env and --output flags; subcommands toggle, list, override (set/remove), env (add/list). internal/api/ package: FlagAPIClient struct with methods for each API operation, HTTP client with 10-second timeout and TLS 1.3, error translation from HTTP status codes to actionable terminal messages. internal/config/ package: Config struct mapping environment name to URL and token, read/write to ~/.flagctl/config.yaml with os.Stat permission check on load, WriteConfig enforces 0600 via os.WriteFile. internal/output/ package: TableWriter for human-readable output, json.Marshal for --output=json mode, both writing to os.Stdout. internal/guard/ package: EnvProductionGuard checks if the resolved environment URL contains 'production' and the --env=production flag was explicitly set, returns error otherwise for write commands.
target_output.workflows: Flag Toggle: developer runs flagctl toggle checkout-v2 --env=staging; cobra dispatches to toggle command; EnvProductionGuard skips check (not production); Config loaded from ~/.flagctl/config.yaml; FlagAPIClient calls PATCH /v1/flags/checkout-v2/toggle?env=staging with Bearer token; response parsed; stdout prints 'checkout-v2 in staging: enabled -> disabled' or JSON equivalent. Production Override: developer runs flagctl override set checkout-v2 --segment=beta-users --value=true --env=production; EnvProductionGuard validates --env=production was explicitly set; confirmation prompt printed 'Set override for beta-users in production? [y/N]'; on confirmation FlagAPIClient POSTs to override endpoint; stdout confirms. Dry Run: flagctl toggle checkout-v2 --env=production --dry-run prints 'DRY RUN: PATCH https://flags.internal/api/v1/flags/checkout-v2/toggle?env=production (Authorization: Bearer *****)' without executing the call.
target_output.data_model: No database. No persistent entities beyond the local config file. Config file schema: environments map with keys as environment names, each entry containing url (string) and token (string). Config stored at ~/.flagctl/config.yaml. No flag values, segment rules, or override history stored locally; the feature flag API is the sole source of truth. flagctl list --output=json returns the API response directly to stdout for downstream pipeline consumption.
target_output.api_design: CLI commands map directly to feature flag API endpoints. GET /v1/flags?env=:env used by flagctl list. PATCH /v1/flags/:name/toggle?env=:env used by flagctl toggle. POST /v1/flags/:name/overrides used by flagctl override set with body {segment_id, value}. DELETE /v1/flags/:name/overrides/:segment used by flagctl override remove. All requests include Authorization: Bearer :token from config. Response errors translated to user-friendly terminal messages by internal/api/errors.go. --dry-run prints the request line without executing.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Token storage: config file written by WriteConfig using os.WriteFile with 0600 mode; on config load os.Stat checks permissions and prints warning 'Config file has insecure permissions :mode, run chmod 0600 ~/.flagctl/config.yaml' if broader. Token masking: API token never echoed to stdout or stderr; --dry-run output replaces token with *****. Production guard: internal/guard/env_guard.go checks that write commands targeting a production-mapped URL were invoked with explicit --env=production; returns error 'Production write requires --env=production flag to prevent accidental mutations' otherwise. Confirmation prompts: flagctl override remove and flag disable operations print a confirmation prompt unless --yes bypasses it. TLS: net/http client uses system TLS with minimum TLS 1.2 enforced via tls.Config MinVersion. Internal security baseline compliance: tokens at 0600, production guard enforced, no flag data persisted locally.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: go vet, golangci-lint --enable=errcheck,gosec, go test ./... with race detector, binary size check (max 20MB). Release trigger on semver tag: goreleaser builds cross-platform binaries (darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, linux/amd64, linux/arm64, windows/amd64), creates GitHub Release with SHA256 checksums, pushes updated Homebrew formula to tap repo. Homebrew install: brew tap internal/tools && brew install flagctl. No server deployment, no Kubernetes, no Docker image.
target_output.observability: No server-side observability for the CLI binary itself. --debug flag enables verbose HTTP request and response logging to stderr (with token redacted) for troubleshooting API connectivity issues. Exit codes: 0 on success, 1 on API error or validation failure, 2 on config error. Structured stderr output for errors to support CI log parsing.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost. GitHub Actions free tier covers the cross-compilation and release pipeline. GitHub Releases storage for binaries is negligible. Homebrew tap is a public GitHub repo with zero hosting cost. Binary footprint under 15MB for all platforms.
target_output.phased_implementation: Single sprint (2 weeks): cobra command skeleton, Viper config with permission enforcement, FlagAPIClient with error translation, EnvProductionGuard, human-readable and JSON output modes, goreleaser release pipeline, Homebrew tap formula, integration tests with httptest mock server. Post-release enhancements: flagctl audit subcommand, shell completion via cobra completion subcommand, CI mode documentation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint enforcing errcheck, gosec, and staticcheck. CGO_ENABLED=0 required in build to ensure static binary. No direct os.Exit calls outside main; functions return errors for testability. API client unit tests use httptest.NewServer. Integration test matrix covers: 401 response, 403 response, 404 flag not found, network timeout, config file not found, production guard enforcement with and without --env=production. Token must not appear in any test fixture or log line.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: API token in config file is readable by other processes running as the same user if umask is permissive. Mitigation: 0600 enforcement at write and permission check at read with warning. Tradeoff: Local config file over OS keychain for simplicity and cross-platform compatibility; OS keychain support can be added post-MVP via a --use-keychain flag. Risk: Feature flag API schema changes breaking the CLI silently if response fields are renamed. Mitigation: version the API client against a specific API version and fail fast with a version mismatch error. Risk: Users sharing config files containing production tokens in dotfile repos. Mitigation: document .gitignore entry for ~/.flagctl/ in onboarding guide.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed cobra over urfave/cli for richer subcommand nesting and shell completion support. Confirmed Viper over manual JSON config for environment variable override support in CI mode. Confirmed GitHub Releases over internal binary registry for zero infrastructure dependency. Confirmed --env=production explicit flag over interactive prompt for production guard to support scripted automation use cases.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: specialist_subplans includes a frontend subplan (FrontendAgent does not run for cli_tool); data section references a PostgreSQL database and GORM ORM contradicting the contract's no-database platform; target_output plan is correct but input specialist_subplans contain contract violations for training on flaw detection.

profile.domain: Security Advisory Portal for Open-Source Container Scanning Tool

project_goal.value: Publish versioned CVE advisories and remediation guidance for an open-source container scanning tool, serving developers who need to review security bulletins and patched release notes.
project_goal.rationale: Core mission of the portal is disseminating security information to the developer community.

target_users.value: Developers and security engineers using the container scanning tool who need to review CVE findings and apply remediation steps.
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified during stakeholder workshops.

project_class.rationale: No server-side runtime required; content is markdown-driven and deployed as static pages.

capabilities.rationale: Static content delivery with lightweight analytics; no backend or auth required.

complexity_level.rationale: Content-driven static site with CI/CD automation; no dynamic server logic.

risk_level.rationale: Public read-only content; no user data collected or stored.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Site publishes public security information; no PII or sensitive data stored.

external_exposure.rationale: Portal must be globally accessible to open-source community without authentication.

access_model.value: Public read-only; no authentication required for any page.
access_model.rationale: Security advisories must be freely accessible to all developers.

feature_scope.value: Versioned CVE advisory pages, severity-filtered bulletin index, remediation guides, changelog entries, search, and RSS feed.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set required for a useful security advisory portal.

mvp_scope.value: CVE advisory pages with severity labels, remediation steps, affected version matrix, and changelog index.
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum set needed to replace the current GitHub Issues advisory workflow.

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare, strict CSP headers, no user input fields, no cookies except analytics opt-in.
security_baseline.rationale: Static public site; security surface limited to transport and content injection.

frontend_stack.rationale: Astro island architecture and static output is optimal for content-heavy advisory pages.

backend_stack.value: None — static site with no server-side runtime.
backend_stack.rationale: All content is generated at build time from markdown files.

data_platform.value: Markdown files in Git as the content store; no database.
data_platform.rationale: Version-controlled markdown provides auditability for advisory changes.

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages with global CDN distribution.
hosting_target.rationale: Cloudflare Pages provides zero cold-start static hosting with built-in CDN and DDoS protection.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data collected; Cloudflare Web Analytics uses privacy-first cookieless measurement.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR compliance achieved by design through data-minimization analytics.

future_scope.value: Machine-readable CVE feed (JSON/CVSS API), integration with OSV database, and email subscription for new advisories.
future_scope.rationale: V2 roadmap to improve toolchain integration.

constraints.value: No server-side compute; entire site must deploy within 3 minutes of a git push; total hosting cost under $0/month on Cloudflare free tier.
constraints.rationale: Open-source project with no infrastructure budget.

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Web Analytics for page views and geography; GitHub Actions build time alerts.
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight analytics sufficient for public content site.

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development on main branch; every merged PR triggers a production deploy.
execution_preference.rationale: Simple deployment model appropriate for a static site with a small maintainer team.

project.goal: Publish versioned CVE advisories and remediation guidance for container scanning tool users
project.target_users: Developers and security engineers in the open-source community

frontend.content_model: Markdown with YAML frontmatter per advisory
frontend.rss: Astro RSS plugin for advisory feed

security.headers: strict CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy via Cloudflare _headers file

devops.environments: preview per PR branch, production on main

reasoner_reviews.product: The advisory portal cleanly addresses the developer workflow of discovering CVEs affecting specific scanner versions and finding remediation steps. The severity-filtered index and affected-version matrix are the highest-value features for triage. RSS feed will be important for pipeline integration once launched.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro is the right choice for this content model: each CVE advisory maps naturally to a markdown file with typed frontmatter (cve_id, severity, affected_versions[], published_at). The Pagefind integration for static search avoids any server dependency. Build caching with Cloudflare Pages is important to keep the 3-minute constraint achievable as advisory count grows.
reasoner_reviews.security: The attack surface is extremely limited for a static site. Key controls are a strict Content-Security-Policy preventing script injection, HSTS preloading via Cloudflare, and ensuring the GitHub Actions runner cannot be hijacked to inject malicious advisory content. The previous plan lacked explicit CSP header configuration details.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloudflare free tier comfortably supports this workload. The 3-minute build constraint is achievable with Astro incremental build support and Cloudflare Pages caching. No database or backend means zero infrastructure cost.

frontend.app_structure: Astro project with content collections for security_bulletins, cve_advisories, and remediation_guides. Each collection has a Zod schema enforcing required frontmatter fields (cve_id, severity, affected_versions, published_at, fixed_in_version).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Index page with severity filter tabs (critical/high/medium/low), advisory detail pages at /advisories/[cve_id], remediation guide pages at /remediation/[slug], versioned changelog at /changelog/[version], and 404 with search suggestion.
frontend.performance_notes: Pagefind static search index generated at build time; no JavaScript framework shipped to client. Image-free content pages target sub-100ms TTFB from Cloudflare edge. Astro partial hydration ensures zero client-side JS except optional search widget.
frontend.frontend_risks: Growing advisory count (500+) may push build times past 3-minute threshold; incremental builds and content collection caching must be validated at scale.

security.auth_design: No authentication; site is fully public read-only.
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare WAF rate limiting on the Pages domain prevents scraping abuse. No user input forms exist so XSS surface is absent.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: GitHub Actions audit log captures every advisory publication event including committer identity and commit SHA, providing a tamper-evident publication trail.
security.security_risks: Supply chain risk in GitHub Actions workflows; pin all action versions to commit SHAs and enable Dependabot for action updates.

data.entities: security_bulletins (cve_id, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, title, published_at, affected_versions[], fixed_in_version, cvss_score), cve_advisories (cve_id, description, attack_vector, impact, proof_of_concept_url), remediation_guides (slug, cve_id FK, steps[], affected_component, patch_commands), changelog_entries (version, release_date, cve_fixes[], feature_additions[]).
data.storage_design: All content stored as typed markdown files in /content/ directory, consumed by Astro content collections. No runtime database.
data.retention_and_deletion: Advisory markdown files are retained indefinitely in Git history; deprecation handled by adding deprecated: true frontmatter flag which hides advisories from index but keeps them routable.

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repository connected to Cloudflare Pages; production deploys from main branch, preview deploys for every PR branch at pr-{number}.advisory-portal.pages.dev.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: validate-frontmatter job using custom TypeScript script to check required YAML fields, then Pagefind index generation, then Cloudflare Pages deployment action. Build time monitored with warning if exceeding 2 minutes 30 seconds.
devops.observability_stack: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard for page views, top advisory pages, and geographic distribution. GitHub Actions workflow duration tracked via built-in metrics.
devops.devops_risks: Cloudflare Pages free tier build concurrency limit (1 concurrent build) may create queue delays during high-activity maintainer sprints.

ISS-001.title: CSP headers not explicitly configured in deployment pipeline
ISS-001.detail: The previous plan referenced Cloudflare security headers but did not specify the Content-Security-Policy header value or the Cloudflare _headers file configuration needed to enforce it.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged missing CSP specification.

focus_issues.title: CSP headers not explicitly configured in deployment pipeline
focus_issues.detail: The previous plan referenced Cloudflare security headers but did not specify the Content-Security-Policy header value or the Cloudflare _headers file configuration needed to enforce it.

latest_recommendations: Specify the exact Content-Security-Policy header value and document the Cloudflare _headers file configuration for header injection.
Add explicit Pagefind build integration step to CI workflow.

previous_audits.summary: Solid static site architecture for a CVE advisory portal. Build pipeline and content model are well-defined. Security header configuration is underspecified, leaving a gap in the stated security baseline.
strengths: Astro content collections with Zod schema validation is the right content model for typed advisory data.
Cloudflare Pages deployment with PR preview branches is well-suited to the maintainer workflow.
concerns: CSP header value not specified; relying on Cloudflare defaults is insufficient for a security-focused portal.
Pagefind integration not shown in CI workflow steps.
