recommendations: Define the CSP header value (script-src, style-src, default-src directives) and document the Cloudflare _headers file configuration.
Add Pagefind index generation as an explicit step after Astro build in GitHub Actions.

issue_updates.title: CSP headers not explicitly configured in deployment pipeline
issue_updates.detail: Plan mentioned Cloudflare security headers generally but did not specify CSP directive values or configuration method.

previous_plan.title: Container Scanner CVE Advisory Portal Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: A static Astro site deployed to Cloudflare Pages that publishes CVE advisories and remediation guides for an open-source container scanning tool. Content is authored as markdown files with typed frontmatter and built into a global CDN-distributed site.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro static site generator builds advisory content from typed markdown collections into a static bundle deployed to Cloudflare Pages CDN. GitHub Actions validates frontmatter and triggers deploys on push to main.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare. Security headers applied via Cloudflare. No user data collected.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI validates frontmatter schema and deploys via Cloudflare Pages action. Preview deployments created per PR branch.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision of a static CVE advisory portal. The key issue from round 1 is the underspecified CSP header configuration. I need to provide the explicit CSP directive values and document the Cloudflare _headers file approach. Since this is a static site with Astro, the _headers file committed to the repo is the cleanest solution. Pagefind integration also needs to be added to the CI steps.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified exact CSP header directive values appropriate for an Astro static site with Pagefind: 'default-src none; script-src self; style-src self unsafe-inline; img-src self data:; font-src self; connect-src self; frame-ancestors none'. Documented the Cloudflare _headers file committed to the repository root as the enforcement mechanism. Added Pagefind index generation (npx pagefind --site dist) as an explicit post-build step in the GitHub Actions workflow.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP header is now fully specified with appropriate directives for a script-minimal Astro site, addressing the security reviewer concern and aligning with the portal security advisory branding.

target_output.title: Container Scanner CVE Advisory Portal Architecture — Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A static Astro site with TypeScript deployed to Cloudflare Pages that publishes versioned CVE advisories, remediation guides, and changelog entries for an open-source container scanning tool. Content is authored as typed markdown files with Zod-validated frontmatter, built into a Pagefind-indexed static bundle, and distributed globally via Cloudflare CDN with explicitly configured security headers committed in the repository _headers file.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro content collections serve as the typed content model, with separate collections for security_bulletins, cve_advisories, and remediation_guides each enforcing a Zod frontmatter schema. At build time, Astro renders advisory pages as static HTML and Pagefind generates a client-side search index. The static bundle is deployed to Cloudflare Pages, which applies security response headers via the committed _headers file. GitHub Actions handles frontmatter validation, Astro build, Pagefind index generation, and Cloudflare Pages deployment in a single pipeline that completes under 3 minutes.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Zod for content schema validation, Pagefind for static search, Astro RSS plugin for advisory feed. Styling: Tailwind CSS via Astro integration. Hosting: Cloudflare Pages global CDN. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Cloudflare Pages deploy action and pinned SHA action versions. Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics cookieless script.
target_output.functional_feature_map: CVE Advisory Index: filterable by severity (critical/high/medium/low) using URL query params rendered at build time. Advisory Detail Pages: full CVE description, CVSS score, affected version matrix, attack vector summary, and link to remediation guide. Remediation Guide Pages: step-by-step patch commands per affected component. Versioned Changelog: release notes grouped by scanner version listing resolved CVEs and feature additions. Static Search: Pagefind widget allowing full-text search across all advisory and remediation content. RSS Feed: /rss.xml listing new advisories for pipeline integration.
target_output.system_components: Astro build process consuming /content/advisories/, /content/remediation/, /content/changelog/ markdown directories. Zod content schemas in src/content/config.ts enforcing typed frontmatter on all collections. Pagefind post-build indexer generating /dist/pagefind/ search assets. Cloudflare Pages CDN serving static bundle with _headers file applying response headers. GitHub Actions runner executing validate-frontmatter, astro-build, pagefind-index, and cloudflare-deploy jobs.
target_output.workflows: Advisory Publication: Maintainer creates a new markdown file in /content/advisories/ with frontmatter fields (cve_id, severity, affected_versions[], fixed_in_version, cvss_score, published_at), pushes to a feature branch, opens a PR. GitHub Actions validates frontmatter schema via TypeScript script; if validation passes a Cloudflare Pages preview URL is posted to the PR. On merge to main, the full pipeline runs: Astro build, Pagefind index generation, and production deploy completing within 3 minutes. Developer Lookup: A developer landing on the portal selects severity filter 'critical' to narrow the advisory index, clicks through to a specific CVE page, reviews the affected version matrix to confirm their scanner version is impacted, follows the link to the remediation guide with patch commands, and subscribes to the RSS feed for future advisories.
target_output.data_model: security_bulletins (cve_id STRING PK e.g. CVE-2024-38365, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, title STRING, published_at DATE, affected_versions STRING[] e.g. ['1.2.0','1.2.1'], fixed_in_version STRING, cvss_score FLOAT, deprecated BOOL). cve_advisories (cve_id FK, description TEXT, attack_vector STRING, impact STRING, proof_of_concept_url STRING nullable). remediation_guides (slug STRING PK, cve_id FK, affected_component STRING, steps STRING[], patch_commands STRING[]). changelog_entries (version STRING PK e.g. '1.3.0', release_date DATE, cve_fixes STRING[], feature_additions STRING[]). All stored as typed markdown files; Astro content collections enforce schema at build time via Zod.
target_output.api_design: Static endpoints generated at build time: GET /advisories/ (severity-filtered index page), GET /advisories/[cve_id] (advisory detail, e.g. /advisories/CVE-2024-38365), GET /remediation/[slug] (remediation guide), GET /changelog/[version] (version release notes), GET /rss.xml (RSS 2.0 advisory feed with title, link, pubDate per entry), GET /pagefind/pagefind.js (static search bundle). No dynamic API routes; all routes pre-rendered at build time.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Transport: Cloudflare enforces HTTPS with HSTS (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload) applied via _headers file. Content-Security-Policy: 'default-src none; script-src self; style-src self unsafe-inline; img-src self data:; font-src self; connect-src self; frame-ancestors none' committed to _headers file at repository root and applied by Cloudflare Pages to all responses. Additional headers: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin, Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(). GitHub Actions supply chain: all third-party actions pinned to full commit SHAs, Dependabot enabled for action version updates. Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics script injected via Astro layout; cookieless by design, no GDPR consent banner required.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline (validate-frontmatter → astro-build → pagefind-index → cloudflare-deploy): Step 1 validate-frontmatter runs a TypeScript script that parses all markdown frontmatter and asserts required fields against the Zod schema, failing fast on missing cve_id, severity, or affected_versions. Step 2 astro-build runs 'astro build' with Node 20, caching node_modules and Astro build cache between runs. Step 3 pagefind-index runs 'npx pagefind --site dist' generating the search index in /dist/pagefind/. Step 4 cloudflare-deploy uses the Cloudflare Pages GitHub Action (pinned to commit SHA) to deploy the dist/ directory. PR branches deploy to preview URLs (pr-{number}.advisory-portal.pages.dev). Merges to main deploy to production. Build time target: under 3 minutes for up to 500 advisories.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard tracks page views, top advisory pages by views, visitor geography, and browser distribution — no cookies or PII collected. GitHub Actions workflow duration tracked via built-in CI metrics; Slack notification via GitHub Actions step triggered if build exceeds 2 minutes 30 seconds. Cloudflare Pages deployment status visible in repository environments page.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost on Cloudflare Pages free tier: unlimited static requests, 500 builds/month, global CDN included. Astro incremental builds keep build time under 3 minutes for up to 500 advisories; for 1000+ advisories, content collection caching and Cloudflare Pages build caching should be evaluated. RSS feed and Pagefind assets served from CDN edge, handling traffic spikes from security disclosure events without infrastructure changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Astro project scaffold with content collections and Zod schemas for security_bulletins and remediation_guides; severity-filtered advisory index; advisory detail and remediation guide pages; GitHub Actions pipeline with frontmatter validation and Cloudflare Pages deploy; _headers file with explicit CSP and security headers. Phase 2 (1 week): Pagefind search integration, RSS feed at /rss.xml, versioned changelog pages, PR preview deployments. Phase 3 (1 week): Cloudflare Web Analytics integration, Dependabot for action updates, build time monitoring alert, sitemap.xml and robots.txt.
target_output.development_guidelines: All advisory markdown files must pass Zod schema validation before merge — enforced by GitHub Actions validate-frontmatter job. Astro TypeScript strict mode enabled. Tailwind CSS only; no custom CSS files. GitHub Actions action versions pinned to commit SHAs; Dependabot configured for weekly updates. PR requires one maintainer review before merge. Advisory frontmatter uses ISO 8601 dates and CVSS 3.1 score format.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Cloudflare Pages free tier limit of 1 concurrent build causes queue delays during rapid advisory publication sprints (e.g., coordinated disclosure days). Mitigation: upgrade to paid tier if build queuing becomes a recurring issue. Risk: Pagefind search index size grows proportionally with advisory count, increasing initial JS payload for search pages. Mitigation: Pagefind chunked loading limits initial download to the index manifest only. Tradeoff: static site approach means no real-time advisory status updates without a rebuild; acceptable given advisory content changes infrequently.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Cloudflare _headers file over Transform Rules for CSP configuration because the file is committed to the repository and version-controlled alongside advisory content. Chose Pagefind over Algolia DocSearch to maintain zero external API dependencies. Confirmed Cloudflare Web Analytics over Plausible to stay within Cloudflare ecosystem and avoid additional vendor.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of static CVE advisory portal; addresses underspecified CSP header configuration from round 1.

profile.domain: Build Artifact Registry API for Enterprise CI/CD Pipelines

project_goal.value: Provide a multi-tenant build artifact registry API that stores, versions, and distributes compiled binaries and container image layers across enterprise CI/CD pipelines, with vulnerability scan ingestion and retention policy enforcement.
project_goal.rationale: Core platform need replacing ad-hoc S3 bucket artifact storage without governance.

target_users.value: Engineering teams consuming artifacts in CI/CD pipelines; platform engineers managing namespaces and retention policies; security teams reviewing vulnerability scan results.
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user personas identified in discovery sessions.

project_class.rationale: Backend API only; no frontend required. Consumers are CI pipelines and developer tooling.

capabilities.rationale: API-first service with scoped token auth, persistent storage, retention batch jobs, and public API surface for pipeline integration.

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant namespace isolation, large binary storage, retention policy engine, and vulnerability scan ingestion are non-trivial.

risk_level.rationale: Internal enterprise data; compromise could affect supply chain integrity but no direct PII exposure.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Build artifacts and scan results are internal engineering data; not publicly classified.

external_exposure.rationale: API accessible only to authenticated CI/CD pipeline tokens and internal engineering tooling.

access_model.value: Scoped API tokens per namespace with RBAC: publisher (upload artifacts), consumer (download artifacts), admin (manage retention policies and namespace settings).
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege token model aligns with SOC 2 access control requirements.

feature_scope.value: Artifact upload and download with checksum verification, multi-tenant namespace management, retention policy engine, vulnerability scan result ingestion from Trivy/Grype, webhook notifications, storage quota enforcement, and download manifest generation.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for an enterprise artifact registry platform.

mvp_scope.value: Artifact upload/download with SHA-256 verification, namespace creation, scoped API token issuance, and basic retention policy (TTL-based deletion). Vulnerability scan ingestion and webhooks in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP unblocks teams from ad-hoc S3 usage within 8 weeks.

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, scoped JWT tokens with 90-day rotation, artifact checksum validation on upload and download, namespace-level access isolation, audit log for all artifact operations.
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 baseline controls for an internal API handling supply-chain artifacts.

frontend_stack.rationale: No UI required; consumers are CI pipelines and CLI tools.

backend_stack.rationale: Go provides efficient binary handling, low memory footprint for high-concurrency artifact streaming, and strong standard library for HTTP and checksums.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for metadata and policy storage; S3-compatible object storage (AWS S3) for artifact binaries.
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for namespace/version metadata; object storage scales for large binary artifacts.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB; S3 for binary storage.
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with auto-scaling; S3 for durable artifact storage.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Artifact binaries retained per namespace retention policy (minimum 30 days, maximum configurable). Audit logs retained 2 years per SOC 2 requirements. Expired artifacts permanently deleted from S3 and metadata purged.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 audit log retention and artifact lifecycle management requirements.

future_scope.value: OCI registry protocol support, geographic replication to multiple AWS regions, and integration with SBOM generation.
future_scope.rationale: V2 roadmap for broader supply-chain security compliance.

constraints.value: Must support artifact files up to 5 GB; API p99 latency under 200ms for metadata operations; artifact upload/download via pre-signed S3 URLs to avoid proxying large binaries through the API tier.
constraints.rationale: Large binary artifacts cannot be buffered through API containers; pre-signed URLs are the standard pattern.

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for API latency traces; structured JSON logs; custom metrics for upload rate, download rate, storage quota utilization per namespace.
observability_baseline.rationale: Per-namespace storage metrics required for quota enforcement and capacity planning.

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; API-first development with OpenAPI 3.0 spec generated from Go handler annotations.
execution_preference.rationale: OpenAPI spec enables parallel client SDK generation during backend development.

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: access control with scoped tokens, audit log for all artifact and namespace operations, data retention enforcement, and change management for API deployments.
compliance_context.rationale: Enterprise customers require SOC 2 Type II attestation for supply-chain tooling.

project.goal: Multi-tenant build artifact registry with retention policy enforcement and vulnerability scan ingestion
project.target_users: CI/CD pipelines, platform engineers, security teams

backend.storage: S3 pre-signed URLs for binary upload/download

security.auth: scoped JWT tokens per namespace
security.audit: append-only audit_events table for all operations

data.cache: Redis 7 for token validation cache and rate limit counters
data.object_storage: AWS S3 with per-namespace bucket prefixes

reasoner_reviews.product: The artifact registry addresses a real gap for enterprise teams managing compiled binaries and container image layers outside of a proper registry. The MVP scope is tightly defined: upload/download with checksum verification and namespace management unblocks immediate adoption. Vulnerability scan ingestion and webhooks are correctly deferred to phase 2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin is well-suited here: efficient binary streaming, excellent concurrency for parallel artifact downloads, and the standard library crypto/sha256 handles checksum verification without dependencies. The pre-signed S3 URL pattern is critical for 5 GB artifacts — do not proxy binaries through the API tier. Namespace isolation via PostgreSQL tenant_id column filtering should be enforced at the database query layer, not only in application code.
reasoner_reviews.security: SOC 2 Type II requires an append-only audit log for all artifact operations. Token scoping must enforce namespace-level isolation: a token issued for namespace 'team-alpha' must not be able to read or write 'team-beta' artifacts. Checksum verification on both upload and download prevents artifact tampering in storage.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 5 GB artifact size constraint rules out any in-memory buffering approach. Pre-signed S3 URLs with 15-minute expiry and single-use enforcement via Redis nonce tracking is the correct pattern. The p99 200ms metadata latency target requires database connection pooling (pgx pgxpool) and Redis caching for frequently-accessed version manifests.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan should specify the nightly retention batch job architecture more precisely — particularly how it handles large namespaces with thousands of artifact versions without causing PostgreSQL lock contention during bulk deletions.

backend.service_design: Go service structured as domain packages: artifacts (upload/download handlers, checksum logic), namespaces (tenant management, quota enforcement), tokens (issuance, validation, rotation), retention (policy evaluation). Gin router with namespace-scoped middleware for token validation and rate limiting.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API following OpenAPI 3.0 spec. Artifact operations return pre-signed S3 URLs rather than binary data. Idempotent artifact version registration: re-uploading the same version SHA is a no-op. Webhook delivery with exponential backoff retry.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly retention job: queries artifact_versions with created_at older than retention_days, processes in batches of 500 to avoid lock contention, issues S3 DeleteObject calls, updates status to deleted, emits audit log entries. Scan result ingestion worker consuming from SQS queue.
backend.backend_risks: Pre-signed URL replay attacks if expiry window is too long. Mitigation: 15-minute expiry with Redis nonce invalidation after first use.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT tokens signed with RS256 key pair; tokens encode namespace_id, scope (publisher/consumer/admin), and expiry. Token validation cached in Redis with 5-minute TTL to reduce database reads on high-frequency CI pipeline calls.
security.authorization_model: Namespace-level RBAC enforced in Gin middleware: extract namespace_id from URL path, verify token namespace_id claim matches, check scope against required permission.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Append-only audit_events table: event_id, namespace_id, actor_token_id, action ENUM, resource_id, checksum, ip_address, timestamp. Queries against audit_events restricted to INSERT only.
security.security_risks: Token leakage via CI logs if tokens embedded in pipeline environment variables. Mitigation: document token injection via Secrets Manager.

data.entities: artifact_namespaces (id UUID PK, name, owner_team, storage_quota_bytes, created_at), artifact_versions (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, artifact_name, version_tag, sha256_checksum, size_bytes, s3_key, status ENUM active/expired/deleted, created_at), retention_policies (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, ttl_days, keep_latest_n INT, updated_at), scan_results (id UUID PK, artifact_version_id FK, scanner ENUM trivy/grype, severity_counts JSONB, findings JSONB, scanned_at), api_tokens (id UUID PK, namespace_id FK, scope ENUM publisher/consumer/admin, hashed_secret, expires_at, revoked_at nullable), audit_events (id UUID PK, namespace_id, actor_token_id, action, resource_id, ip_address, timestamp).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 via pgx pgxpool for metadata. S3 with key pattern {namespace_id}/{artifact_name}/{version_tag}/{sha256} for binary storage. Redis 7 for token validation cache and rate limit counters.
data.data_risks: Orphaned S3 objects if artifact_version row deletion fails. Mitigation: deletion marks status=deleted first, then async S3 cleanup with idempotent retry.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: api-service task (2 vCPU, 4 GB, min 2 tasks), retention-worker task (runs nightly via scheduled ECS task). ALB in front of api-service. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis 7.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test with race detector, go build, Docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy with health check.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with Go tracer; structured JSON logs. Custom metrics: artifact_upload_total, artifact_download_total, storage_quota_used_bytes per namespace, retention_job_duration_seconds.
devops.devops_risks: Retention batch job during peak CI hours may cause RDS CPU spikes. Mitigation: schedule at 02:00 UTC with batch size limits.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a multi-tenant artifact registry API in Go. Key decisions: pre-signed S3 URLs for all binary transfers to avoid proxying 5 GB artifacts through API containers; namespace-level RBAC enforced in Gin middleware; append-only audit_events table for SOC 2 compliance; nightly retention job using batched deletions with pg_sleep to prevent RDS lock contention.
target_output.title: Build Artifact Registry API Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin-based multi-tenant artifact registry API on AWS ECS Fargate that stores, versions, and distributes compiled binaries and container image layers for enterprise CI/CD pipelines. Artifact binaries are stored in S3 with pre-signed URL transfers; metadata, retention policies, and vulnerability scan results are persisted in PostgreSQL 16. Scoped JWT tokens enforce namespace-level RBAC and all operations are captured in an append-only audit log for SOC 2 Type II compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless Go API service runs on ECS Fargate behind an ALB, handling artifact metadata operations with p99 under 200ms by leveraging pgx pgxpool for PostgreSQL connections and Redis token validation caching. Binary uploads and downloads are handled exclusively via pre-signed S3 URLs generated by the API — the API never proxies artifact bytes. Namespace isolation is enforced at both the JWT claim level (middleware rejects cross-namespace tokens) and the database query level (all queries filter by namespace_id). A separate ECS scheduled task executes the nightly retention job, processing expired artifact_versions in batches of 500 with inter-batch sleep to prevent RDS lock contention. Vulnerability scan results from Trivy/Grype are ingested asynchronously via SQS.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. Web framework: Gin with custom namespace-auth middleware. Database driver: pgx v5 with pgxpool (max 25 connections per instance). Cache: Redis 7 via go-redis v9 for token validation cache and sliding-window rate limiting. Object storage: AWS S3 SDK v2 with pre-signed URL generation. Auth: RS256 JWT via golang-jwt/jwt. Queue: AWS SQS for async scan result ingestion. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, S3, ECR, Secrets Manager.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Namespace Management: create and configure multi-tenant namespaces with storage quota enforcement. Artifact Upload: generate pre-signed S3 PUT URL, register artifact_version metadata with SHA-256 checksum; reject re-registration of existing version as no-op. Artifact Download: validate token scope, verify artifact status=active, generate pre-signed S3 GET URL with 15-minute expiry and Redis nonce invalidation. Retention Policy Engine: per-namespace TTL and keep-latest-N rules evaluated nightly. Vulnerability Scan Ingestion: async SQS consumer ingests Trivy/Grype webhook payloads and updates scan_results. Token Management: issue, rotate, and revoke scoped API tokens per namespace.
target_output.system_components: ALB routing HTTPS traffic to ECS Fargate api-service tasks (min 2, max 10, scaling on CPU 60%). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary for writes, read replica for audit log queries. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for token validation cache and rate limit state. S3 bucket with per-namespace prefix isolation. SQS standard queue for async scan result ingestion. ECS scheduled task running retention-worker binary nightly. AWS Secrets Manager for database credentials and JWT signing keys.
target_output.workflows: Artifact Upload: CI pipeline authenticates with a publisher-scoped JWT token, calls POST /v1/namespaces/{namespace_id}/artifacts/{name}/versions with version metadata; API validates token namespace claim, checks storage quota, generates pre-signed S3 PUT URL (15-min expiry), returns URL and upload_id. CI pipeline uploads binary directly to S3, then calls POST /v1/uploads/{upload_id}/complete with the received ETag. API verifies SHA-256 checksum against registered value, marks artifact_version status=active, writes audit_events entry. Retention Enforcement: ECS scheduled task at 02:00 UTC queries artifact_versions where created_at is older than ttl_days and status=active in batches of 500. For each batch: issues S3 DeleteObject calls, updates status=deleted, writes bulk audit_events INSERT, sleeps 500ms before next batch.
target_output.data_model: artifact_namespaces (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, owner_team VARCHAR, storage_quota_bytes BIGINT, used_bytes BIGINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). artifact_versions (id UUID PK, namespace_id UUID FK, artifact_name VARCHAR, version_tag VARCHAR, sha256_checksum CHAR(64), size_bytes BIGINT, s3_key VARCHAR, status ENUM active/expired/deleted, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(namespace_id, artifact_name, version_tag)). retention_policies (id UUID PK, namespace_id UUID FK UNIQUE, ttl_days INT, keep_latest_n INT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_results (id UUID PK, artifact_version_id UUID FK, scanner ENUM trivy/grype, severity_counts JSONB, findings JSONB, scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). api_tokens (id UUID PK, namespace_id UUID FK, scope ENUM publisher/consumer/admin, hashed_secret VARCHAR, expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). audit_events (id UUID PK, namespace_id UUID, actor_token_id UUID, action VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, checksum VARCHAR nullable, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API under /v1. POST /v1/namespaces (create namespace, admin only). POST /v1/namespaces/{ns_id}/tokens (issue scoped token). POST /v1/namespaces/{ns_id}/artifacts/{name}/versions (initiate upload, returns pre-signed S3 PUT URL). POST /v1/uploads/{upload_id}/complete (confirm upload, triggers checksum verification). GET /v1/namespaces/{ns_id}/artifacts/{name}/versions/{tag} (get version metadata and pre-signed S3 GET URL). DELETE /v1/namespaces/{ns_id}/artifacts/{name}/versions/{tag} (soft-delete). GET /v1/namespaces/{ns_id}/artifacts/{name}/versions (list versions with status filter). POST /v1/webhooks/scan-results (scan result ingestion, HMAC-verified). GET /v1/namespaces/{ns_id}/audit-log (paginated audit events, admin scope only).
target_output.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 Type II controls: append-only audit_events table (INSERT-only privileges for api-service DB role), scoped JWT tokens with RS256 signing stored in Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation, namespace-level RBAC enforced in Gin middleware before any handler executes. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB. Artifact integrity: SHA-256 checksum verified on upload completion. Pre-signed S3 URL nonce tracking: Redis stores upload_id nonce with 20-minute TTL; completion endpoint invalidates nonce. Rate limiting: 100 upload requests/minute per namespace token via Redis sliding window. HMAC-SHA256 signature validation on scan result webhooks.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI pipeline: golangci-lint (staticcheck, errcheck, gosec), go test ./... -race (80% coverage gate), go build, docker build multi-stage (golang:1.22-alpine builder, distroless/static final), push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Database migrations via golang-migrate run as ECS one-off task before deploy. Secrets Manager secrets injected as environment variables via ECS task definition secretsManagerSecrets. Environments: dev (single Fargate task, RDS t3.medium), staging (2 tasks, RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ), production (min 2 max 10 tasks, RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ, Redis cluster).
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with Go tracer instrumented on all Gin handlers; spans include namespace_id and artifact_name tags. Structured JSON logs (zerolog) with correlation IDs. Custom Datadog metrics: artifact_upload_total (counter, tagged by namespace), artifact_download_total, storage_quota_used_bytes (gauge per namespace), retention_job_batches_processed, scan_ingestion_queue_depth. PagerDuty alert: API error rate >2% in 5-minute window, retention job failure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800-1200/month at moderate load: Fargate 2 tasks baseline ~$120/month, RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ ~$400/month, ElastiCache r6g.large ~$200/month, S3 5 TB storage ~$115/month. ECS auto-scaling on CPU 60% target handles pipeline burst traffic. Retention policies are critical for S3 cost control.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): namespace CRUD, scoped JWT token issuance and validation, artifact upload/download via pre-signed S3 URLs with SHA-256 verification, basic TTL retention policy engine, append-only audit_events table, OpenAPI 3.0 spec, ECS Fargate deployment with GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (4 weeks): keep-latest-N retention policy, vulnerability scan result ingestion via SQS, outbound webhook delivery, storage quota enforcement. Phase 3 (4 weeks): download manifest generation for multi-artifact release bundles, Datadog APM instrumentation, per-namespace metrics dashboard, rate limiting per token.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with go vet, golangci-lint (staticcheck, errcheck, gosec enabled). All database queries use pgx named parameters; no string concatenation in SQL. 80% test coverage enforced in CI. Unit tests for retention policy evaluation; integration tests against dockerized PostgreSQL and Redis using testcontainers-go. Error responses follow RFC 7807 Problem Details format. Structured logging via zerolog only.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: pre-signed S3 URL replay if nonce invalidation fails (Redis outage). Mitigation: 15-minute URL expiry limits replay window; Redis cluster mode reduces single-point failure risk. Risk: retention batch job lock contention on artifact_versions during peak CI hours. Mitigation: 02:00 UTC scheduling and 500ms inter-batch sleep with pg_advisory_lock to prevent concurrent retention runs. Tradeoff: async scan ingestion via SQS means vulnerability data is eventually consistent (minutes delay); acceptable given scan jobs run after artifact upload completes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected pre-signed S3 URLs over artifact proxy streaming to meet the 5 GB artifact constraint. Chose PostgreSQL application-layer namespace_id filtering over RLS policies to simplify query debugging. Confirmed SQS over direct Trivy/Grype webhook synchronous processing to decouple scan latency from artifact upload response time.

metadata.notes: Round 1 first-pass for multi-tenant artifact registry API; plan quality weak per manifest spec.

profile.domain: Real-Time Telemetry Ingestion Pipeline for Kubernetes Microservices

project_goal.value: Ingest distributed trace spans, metrics, and structured logs from Kubernetes-hosted microservices, normalize them into an OTLP-compatible event stream, and deliver to ClickHouse with exactly-once semantics and backpressure handling.
project_goal.rationale: Replaces fragmented per-service logging with a unified observability pipeline.

target_users.value: Platform engineering teams operating the Kubernetes cluster; SREs querying telemetry for incident response; observability tool consumers reading from ClickHouse.
target_users.rationale: Three internal consumer personas identified in platform roadmap.

project_class.rationale: Core purpose is high-throughput data ingestion, normalization, and delivery.

capabilities.rationale: Real-time ingestion with exactly-once batch checkpointing, OTLP protocol integration, and ClickHouse delivery.

complexity_level.rationale: Hundreds of agents, high-throughput fan-in, exactly-once delivery semantics, and schema evolution under live traffic are high-scale concerns.

risk_level.rationale: Internal observability data; loss impacts incident response capability but no external data exposure.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Telemetry data is internal engineering observability data; no PII or financial data in scope.

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline operates entirely within the Kubernetes cluster network; no external ingress.

access_model.value: Internal Kubernetes service accounts for agent-to-gateway communication; mTLS between pipeline components; no external user authentication.
access_model.rationale: Service mesh mTLS provides component-level authentication without external identity provider.

feature_scope.value: gRPC OTLP ingestion gateway, schema validation and normalization, Kafka fan-out to ClickHouse writer, exactly-once delivery via per-agent checkpoint manager, schema evolution with auto-provisioning of ClickHouse columns, backpressure signaling to agents.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for a production-grade observability ingestion pipeline.

mvp_scope.value: gRPC OTLP gateway accepting trace spans and metrics, schema validation, Kafka fan-out, ClickHouse writer with at-least-once delivery. Exactly-once checkpointing and schema evolution in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: At-least-once delivery is sufficient for MVP observability use cases.

security_baseline.value: mTLS between all pipeline components via Istio service mesh; Kubernetes RBAC for service account permissions; no external ingress; network policies restricting agent-to-gateway traffic.
security_baseline.rationale: Internal-only pipeline; perimeter security via network policies and mTLS.

frontend_stack.value: None — data pipeline with no user interface.
frontend_stack.rationale: Pipeline is consumed programmatically; Grafana dashboards are external consumers not part of this system.

backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides memory-safe, zero-copy buffer handling essential for high-throughput span ingestion; Tokio enables efficient async gRPC server.

data_platform.value: ClickHouse for normalized telemetry storage; Kafka for inter-component event streaming; Redis for checkpoint state.
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse columnar storage optimizes telemetry aggregation queries; Kafka provides durable buffering and replay for exactly-once semantics.

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot with Kubernetes Deployments and StatefulSets for stateful components.
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot manages node provisioning; StatefulSets for Kafka and ClickHouse provide stable network identities.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Telemetry data retained 30 days in ClickHouse hot storage; archived to GCS cold storage for 90 days; deleted after 90 days. Checkpoint state retained indefinitely in Redis.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 30-day hot retention supports incident postmortem workflows; 90-day archive for capacity planning.

future_scope.value: Tail-based trace sampling, anomaly detection on metric series, and multi-cluster federation for cross-cluster observability.
future_scope.rationale: V2 roadmap to reduce storage costs and add proactive alerting.

constraints.value: Must handle 100k spans/second at peak; end-to-end latency from agent emit to ClickHouse availability under 5 seconds; zero data loss on gateway pod restart.
constraints.rationale: Latency and throughput requirements derived from cluster observability SLA.

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline self-telemetry; metrics for ingestion throughput, Kafka consumer lag, ClickHouse write latency, and checkpoint offset staleness.
observability_baseline.rationale: Pipeline must be self-observable to detect ingestion bottlenecks and delivery failures.

execution_preference.value: Continuous delivery via GitLab CI with Helm chart deployments; canary rollout for gateway upgrades to avoid ingestion gaps.
execution_preference.rationale: Canary rollout minimizes risk of ingestion disruption during gateway upgrades.

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: audit log for pipeline configuration changes; data retention enforcement at 90-day boundary; access controls via Kubernetes RBAC for pipeline component service accounts.
compliance_context.rationale: Internal platform SOC 2 scope includes observability infrastructure configuration changes.

project.goal: High-throughput OTLP telemetry ingestion with exactly-once delivery to ClickHouse
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, full exactly-once in 16 weeks

backend.protocol: gRPC with tonic for OTLP ingestion
backend.kafka: rdkafka Rust client for Kafka producer/consumer

security.auth: mTLS via Istio service mesh
security.network: Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricting agent egress to gateway only

data.primary: ClickHouse columnar store for normalized telemetry
data.streaming: Kafka 3.x via Confluent Cloud on GCP
data.checkpoint: Redis 7 for per-agent ingestion offset state

constraints.durability: zero data loss on gateway restart

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline directly solves the fragmented observability problem across hundreds of Kubernetes microservices. The phased approach (at-least-once MVP, then exactly-once) is pragmatic. Schema evolution is a critical differentiator that prevents manual ClickHouse column management as microservice teams add new span attributes.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust with Tokio is the right choice: zero-copy buffer handling via bytes::Bytes prevents memory pressure under 100k span/second load; tonic gRPC server handles OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest natively. The checkpoint manager must use Kafka consumer group offsets as the source of truth. The schema evolution job must acquire a ClickHouse DDL lock before ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN to prevent concurrent schema mutations.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS via Istio provides strong component authentication. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy must restrict agent pods to egress only to the gateway service ClusterIP on port 4317 (gRPC). Pipeline configuration changes must be captured in a pipeline_config_audit table for SOC 2.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 100k spans/second at 5-second end-to-end latency requires Kafka with 64 partitions and ClickHouse async_insert with buffer_flush_max_rows=50000. The gateway pod restart zero-loss requirement is met by Kafka producer acks=all and idempotent producer configuration. Round 3 should verify that schema evolution DDL operations are atomic and non-blocking.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The previous plan resolved the ClickHouse write amplification issue by switching to async_insert. Round 3 should harden the schema evolution concurrency story and clarify the backpressure signaling mechanism.

backend.service_design: Three Rust binary services: ingestion-gateway (gRPC OTLP server, schema validation, Kafka producer), checkpoint-manager (tracks per-agent offsets in Redis, handles agent reconnection replay), schema-evolution-worker (detects new OTLP attribute keys, issues ClickHouse ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, notifies downstream query consumers).
backend.api_patterns: gRPC service implementing opentelemetry-proto ExportTraceServiceRequest, ExportMetricsServiceRequest, ExportLogsServiceRequest. Backpressure via gRPC flow control: gateway returns RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED status when Kafka producer queue exceeds 80% capacity, signaling agents to apply exponential backoff.
backend.failure_handling: Gateway pod restart: Kafka idempotent producer prevents duplicate publishes. ClickHouse write failure: writer buffers failed batches in a Redis retry queue with exponential backoff up to 5 retries. Schema evolution DDL uses ON CLUSTER syntax with DDL queue to serialize mutations.
backend.scaling_notes: Gateway scales horizontally 3-10 pods based on Kafka producer queue depth HPA custom metric. Each gateway pod handles ~15k spans/second.

security.auth_design: Istio service mesh enforces mTLS for all inter-component gRPC calls. Agent pods use Kubernetes service accounts with projected ServiceAccountToken.
security.authorization_model: Kubernetes RBAC: gateway ServiceAccount has permissions on agent ServiceAccounts for token validation; schema-evolution-worker ServiceAccount has access to ClickHouse admin credentials in Secret.
security.security_risks: Schema evolution worker requires ClickHouse admin credentials; worker runs in dedicated namespace with strict NetworkPolicy; credentials rotated monthly via External Secrets Operator.

data.entities: trace_spans (trace_id String, span_id String, parent_span_id Nullable(String), service_name LowCardinality(String), operation_name String, start_time DateTime64(9), duration_ns UInt64, status_code LowCardinality(String), attributes Map(String, String)) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(start_time) ORDER BY (service_name, trace_id, start_time) TTL start_time + INTERVAL 30 DAY. metric_series (metric_name LowCardinality(String), service_name LowCardinality(String), timestamp DateTime64(9), value Float64, labels Map(String, String)) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree. log_events (timestamp DateTime64(9), service_name LowCardinality(String), severity_text LowCardinality(String), body String, trace_id Nullable(String), attributes Map(String, String)) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree. ingestion_checkpoints stored as Redis hashes per agent_id with per-partition Kafka offsets. schema_definitions (attribute_key, data_type, first_seen_at, provisioned_at) in PostgreSQL sidecar.
data.storage_design: ClickHouse 6-node cluster on GKE with ReplicatedMergeTree and ZooKeeper. 64-partition Kafka topics for spans/metrics/logs. Redis cluster for checkpoint state. GCS bucket for ClickHouse cold storage tiering.
data.consistency_notes: Exactly-once delivery: Kafka idempotent producer on gateway plus transactional consumer on ClickHouse writer. Checkpoint offsets committed to Redis only after ClickHouse batch INSERT acknowledgment. Schema evolution DDL uses ON CLUSTER syntax with distributed_ddl_task_timeout to serialize across all nodes.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot: ingestion-gateway Deployment (3-10 pods HPA), clickhouse-writer StatefulSet (2 pods), checkpoint-manager Deployment (2 pods active-passive), schema-evolution-worker CronJob (every 15 min). ClickHouse 6-node cluster via clickhouse-operator. Kafka via Confluent Cloud on GCP (6 brokers, replication factor 3).
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: cargo clippy (deny warnings), cargo test, cargo build --release, Docker multi-stage build (rust:1.76-slim builder, distroless final), Helm chart lint and diff, canary deploy (10% traffic) with 5-minute observation window, full rollout on metric health.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping all pipeline component /metrics endpoints. Grafana dashboards: ingestion throughput, Kafka consumer lag per partition, ClickHouse insert latency p50/p99, checkpoint offset staleness, schema evolution DDL queue depth.
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous chart revision; gateway canary abort on Kafka producer error rate >1%. ClickHouse schema rollback: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN only if column is empty.

ISS-001.title: ClickHouse write amplification under sync INSERT defaults
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan used synchronous INSERT per batch causing write amplification at 100k spans/second. Resolved in round 2 by switching to async_insert with buffer_flush_max_rows=50000.

history.note: Auditor identified synchronous INSERT causing merge pressure.
Switched to async_insert; load test confirmed throughput achieved.

ISS-002.title: Schema evolution DDL concurrency safety not addressed
ISS-002.detail: The schema evolution worker issues ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN without addressing concurrent DDL mutations across ClickHouse cluster nodes, creating race conditions that could cause column not found errors.

history.note: Critic raised schema evolution concurrency during round 2 review.

ISS-003.title: Backpressure mechanism to agents not specified
ISS-003.detail: Plan references backpressure signaling but does not specify the gRPC flow control mechanism or the Kafka producer queue depth threshold.

history.note: Architect reviewer noted missing backpressure specification.

focus_issues.title: Schema evolution DDL concurrency safety not addressed
focus_issues.detail: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN without DDL serialization across cluster nodes can cause column not found errors on concurrent inserts.

focus_issues.title: Backpressure mechanism to agents not specified
focus_issues.detail: gRPC flow control thresholds and Kafka queue depth trigger not documented.

latest_recommendations: Specify ClickHouse ON CLUSTER DDL serialization mechanism for schema evolution to prevent concurrent column mutations.
Document the exact gRPC RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED trigger condition and backpressure threshold.
