previous_plan.title: OTLP Telemetry Ingestion Pipeline Architecture — Revision 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Rust/Tokio gRPC ingestion gateway fan-outing normalized OTLP telemetry to ClickHouse via Kafka with at-least-once delivery resolved to exactly-once via per-agent Redis checkpoint offsets. Async ClickHouse inserts with 50k-row flush threshold handle 100k spans/second throughput.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Rust ingestion gateway receives OTLP spans/metrics/logs over gRPC, validates schema, and produces to 64-partition Kafka topics. ClickHouse async_insert writer consumes from Kafka and flushes at 50k rows or 2s. Redis stores per-agent checkpoint offsets for exactly-once replay.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: mTLS via Istio enforces component authentication. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy restricts agent egress to gateway ClusterIP on port 4317.
previous_plan.workflows: Schema evolution worker detects new attribute keys, issues ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN — concurrency handling not yet specified.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-002 (schema evolution DDL concurrency) and ISS-003 (backpressure specification). For ISS-002: ClickHouse ON CLUSTER DDL with distributed_ddl_task_timeout and a Redis SET NX EX distributed lock serializes schema-evolution-worker invocations. For ISS-003: gRPC backpressure via RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED triggered at 80% of rdkafka queue.buffering.max.messages (80k/100k), with agent exponential backoff starting at 500ms.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified ClickHouse ON CLUSTER DDL serialization: schema-evolution-worker acquires a Redis distributed lock (SET schema_evolution_lock NX EX 300) before issuing any ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ON CLUSTER statements. DDL uses distributed_ddl_task_timeout=60 to wait for all cluster nodes to confirm column addition before releasing the lock. Writer pods detect new attribute keys in OTLP data and defer rows with unknown columns to a Redis retry queue until schema-evolution-worker provisions the column and releases the lock.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Schema evolution DDL is serialized across schema-evolution-worker invocations and ClickHouse cluster nodes, eliminating column not found errors under concurrent inserts of spans with new attribute keys.

fix_report.action_taken: Documented exact backpressure mechanism: gateway monitors rdkafka internal producer queue depth via librdkafka statistics callback every 100ms. When queue_depth / queue.buffering.max.messages > 0.80 (threshold: 80k of 100k messages), gateway returns gRPC status RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED on subsequent Export requests. Agents implement exponential backoff starting at 500ms, doubling up to 30s max. When queue depth drops below 60% (recovery threshold), gateway resumes accepting requests.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Agents receive explicit backpressure signal before the gateway Kafka producer queue saturates, preventing span loss under burst ingestion loads.

target_output.title: OTLP Telemetry Ingestion Pipeline Architecture — Revision 3
target_output.executive_summary: A Rust/Tokio gRPC ingestion gateway that accepts distributed trace spans, metrics, and structured logs from Kubernetes microservices over OTLP, normalizes them, and delivers to ClickHouse via 64-partition Kafka topics with exactly-once semantics. Round 3 hardens the schema evolution DDL concurrency model with ON CLUSTER serialization and a Redis distributed lock, and specifies the gRPC backpressure threshold (80% rdkafka queue depth → RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED) with agent exponential backoff recovery.
target_output.architecture_overview: Ingestion gateway (3-10 Rust/Tokio pods) receives OTLP Export requests over gRPC/mTLS, validates span schema against schema_definitions registry, and publishes to Kafka topics (otlp-spans, otlp-metrics, otlp-logs) with 64 partitions each. Backpressure: gateway monitors rdkafka producer queue depth via statistics callback every 100ms; when depth exceeds 80% of queue.buffering.max.messages (80k/100k), returns gRPC RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED to trigger agent exponential backoff (500ms base, doubling to 30s max). Recovery threshold at 60%. ClickHouse async_insert writer pods consume from Kafka and flush at buffer_flush_max_rows=50000 or 2 seconds. Checkpoint manager persists per-agent Kafka partition offsets in Redis only after ClickHouse batch INSERT acknowledgment, enabling exactly-once replay on reconnect. Schema evolution worker acquires a Redis distributed lock before issuing ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ON CLUSTER to serialize DDL across all ClickHouse nodes.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Rust 1.76 with Tokio async runtime. gRPC: tonic 0.11 with opentelemetry-proto generated bindings. Kafka client: rdkafka (librdkafka bindings) with idempotent producer (acks=all) and transactional consumer. ClickHouse client: clickhouse-rs with async_insert enabled. Redis: redis-rs with cluster mode for distributed lock and checkpoint state. Service mesh: Istio 1.21 for mTLS. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Confluent Cloud Kafka on GCP (6 brokers, RF 3), ClickHouse 6-node cluster via clickhouse-operator, Redis cluster 3 nodes. CI/CD: GitLab CI with Helm canary rollouts. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: OTLP Ingestion Gateway: accepts trace spans, metrics, and logs over gRPC; validates OTLP schema; applies backpressure via RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED when Kafka queue exceeds 80% capacity. Kafka Fan-out: normalized events published to typed Kafka topics with 64-partition assignment by trace_id hash. ClickHouse Async Writer: consumes Kafka partitions in batches; async_insert flushes at 50k rows or 2s; exactly-once commit on ACK. Checkpoint Manager: tracks per-agent Kafka partition offsets in Redis; replays missed events from last committed offset on agent reconnect with deduplication. Schema Evolution Worker: runs every 15 minutes via CronJob; acquires Redis distributed lock; issues ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN ON CLUSTER; notifies consumers via Redis pub/sub.
target_output.system_components: Ingestion Gateway Deployment (3-10 pods, HPA on Kafka producer queue depth ratio): Tokio gRPC server on port 4317; rdkafka producer with idempotent mode; statistics callback polling queue depth every 100ms; backpressure state machine (accepting/shedding/recovering). ClickHouse Writer StatefulSet (2 pods, each assigned 32 Kafka partitions): rdkafka consumer group; clickhouse-rs async_insert batch accumulator; Redis checkpoint commit after ClickHouse ACK. Checkpoint Manager Deployment (2 pods active-passive). Schema Evolution Worker CronJob (every 15 min): queries schema_definitions; acquires Redis lock; issues ClickHouse ON CLUSTER DDL; publishes schema_updated to Redis pub/sub. ClickHouse 6-node ReplicatedMergeTree cluster with ZooKeeper. Kafka 6-broker Confluent Cloud cluster. Redis 3-node cluster.
target_output.workflows: OTLP Span Ingestion with Backpressure: OpenTelemetry agent calls ExportTraceServiceRequest to gateway. Gateway validates span fields. If rdkafka queue depth >80%: returns RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED; agent applies exponential backoff (500ms base, doubling to 30s max). If queue depth <80%: gateway serializes span to Kafka message keyed by trace_id, publishes with acks=all. ClickHouse writer consumes batch from Kafka, accumulates rows until flush threshold, issues async INSERT INTO trace_spans. On ClickHouse ACK: checkpoint manager commits Kafka consumer group offset to Redis hash. Schema Evolution: Schema evolution worker wakes via CronJob. Queries schema_definitions WHERE provisioned_at IS NULL. If unprovisioned keys exist: acquires Redis distributed lock (SET schema_evolution_lock NX EX 300). Issues ALTER TABLE trace_spans ON CLUSTER ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS with distributed_ddl_task_timeout=60. On all-nodes confirmation: UPDATE schema_definitions SET provisioned_at=NOW(). Releases lock. Publishes schema_updated to Redis pub/sub. Writer pods subscribe and refresh column list before next insert batch.
target_output.data_model: trace_spans (trace_id String, span_id String, parent_span_id Nullable(String), service_name LowCardinality(String), operation_name String, start_time DateTime64(9) CODEC(DoubleDelta), duration_ns UInt64 CODEC(T64), status_code LowCardinality(String), attributes Map(String, String), resource_attributes Map(String, String)) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(start_time) ORDER BY (service_name, trace_id, start_time) TTL start_time + INTERVAL 30 DAY. metric_series (metric_name LowCardinality(String), service_name LowCardinality(String), timestamp DateTime64(9), value Float64, labels Map(String, String)) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree. log_events (timestamp DateTime64(9), service_name LowCardinality(String), severity_text LowCardinality(String), body String, trace_id Nullable(String), attributes Map(String, String)) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree. ingestion_checkpoints stored as Redis hashes: key=agent:{agent_id}, fields per partition offset. schema_definitions (attribute_key VARCHAR PK, data_type VARCHAR, first_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ, provisioned_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable) in PostgreSQL sidecar.
target_output.api_design: Internal gRPC API: opentelemetry.proto.collector.trace.v1.TraceService/Export, opentelemetry.proto.collector.metrics.v1.MetricsService/Export, opentelemetry.proto.collector.logs.v1.LogsService/Export. All return success or RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. Internal HTTP: GET /metrics (Prometheus scrape, port 9090), GET /health/ready (checks Kafka producer connectivity), GET /health/live (liveness probe). Checkpoint Manager gRPC: internal CheckpointService/GetAgentOffset and CommitOffset.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Istio mTLS: all inter-component communication encrypted and mutually authenticated via Istio PeerAuthentication STRICT mode. Kubernetes NetworkPolicy: agent pods allowed egress only to gateway ClusterIP on TCP 4317. Schema evolution worker runs in isolated namespace with NetworkPolicy allowing only ClickHouse admin port egress. Redis distributed lock for schema DDL: SET schema_evolution_lock NX EX 300 ensures serialized DDL execution preventing concurrent ALTER TABLE. SOC 2 controls: pipeline_config_audit PostgreSQL table records all Kafka topic configuration changes, ClickHouse retention policy changes, and schema evolution DDL events with actor service account and timestamp.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test --release, cargo build --release, Docker multi-stage (rust:1.76-bullseye builder to distroless/cc:latest final image ~15 MB), Helm chart lint, helm diff against staging, canary deploy (10% of gateway pods) with 5-minute Prometheus observation window (error rate <0.1%, kafka_producer_queue_depth_ratio <0.5), full rollout on pass or automatic rollback on fail. Environments: dev (single-broker Kafka, single-node ClickHouse), staging (3-broker Kafka, 3-node ClickHouse, Redis cluster), production (6-broker Confluent Cloud, 6-node ClickHouse, 3-node Redis cluster). ClickHouse schema migrations via schema-evolution-worker CronJob with distributed lock.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics scraped from all pipeline components every 15s. Key metrics: gateway_spans_ingested_total (counter by service_name), gateway_kafka_queue_depth_ratio (gauge, alert >0.85), gateway_resource_exhausted_total (counter tracking backpressure events), clickhouse_writer_insert_batch_latency_seconds (histogram, p99 alert >3s), kafka_consumer_lag (gauge per partition, alert if lag >50k messages for >2 min), checkpoint_offset_staleness_seconds (gauge per agent, alert >30s), schema_evolution_ddl_duration_seconds (histogram). Grafana dashboards: Ingestion Overview, Backpressure Events, ClickHouse Write Health, Schema Evolution History.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GKE Autopilot scales with pod resource requests: 3-10 gateway pods at 1 vCPU/2 GB each. Confluent Cloud 6-broker cluster ~$600/month at moderate throughput. ClickHouse 6-node cluster on GKE n2-standard-8 ~$1200/month. Redis cluster 3 nodes ~$150/month. Total estimated $2200-2800/month at 100k spans/s sustained. Gateway pods scale horizontally on kafka_queue_depth_ratio metric.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Rust ingestion gateway with OTLP gRPC server, Kafka producer (idempotent, acks=all), schema validation against static allowlist; ClickHouse writer with async_insert; basic Prometheus metrics; GitLab CI pipeline; GKE deployment via Helm. At-least-once delivery only. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Redis checkpoint manager for per-agent offset tracking; exactly-once delivery via checkpoint commit on ClickHouse ACK; agent reconnection replay; schema evolution worker with Redis distributed lock and ON CLUSTER DDL; Redis pub/sub schema update notification. Phase 3 (4 weeks): gRPC backpressure RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED with rdkafka queue depth monitoring; Grafana dashboards; GCS tiered storage for ClickHouse cold data beyond 30 days.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust edition 2021, clippy deny(warnings). All rdkafka configuration values documented in config/ TOML files, not hardcoded. Integration tests use testcontainers-rs with real Kafka and ClickHouse containers. Kafka producer tests validate idempotent delivery under simulated broker failures. Schema evolution tests inject new OTLP attribute keys and verify ClickHouse column provisioning with concurrent writer inserts. No unwrap() calls in production paths.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis distributed lock for schema evolution held for up to 300s may block writer pods processing spans with new attribute keys, causing Redis retry queue growth. Mitigation: schema-evolution-worker CronJob frequency increased to every 5 minutes if retry queue exceeds 10k entries. Risk: ClickHouse ON CLUSTER DDL with distributed_ddl_task_timeout=60 may fail if a cluster node is temporarily unreachable. Mitigation: ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent; next CronJob invocation retries. Tradeoff: Redis pub/sub schema update notification is fire-and-forget; writer pods that miss the message use the retry queue.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved ISS-002: Schema evolution uses Redis distributed lock plus ClickHouse ON CLUSTER DDL with distributed_ddl_task_timeout=60 to serialize mutations across cluster nodes. Resolved ISS-003: Backpressure triggered at 80% rdkafka internal queue depth (80k/100k messages); recovery threshold 60%; agent exponential backoff 500ms to 30s max. Confirmed async_insert buffer_flush_max_rows=50000 from round 2 remains unchanged.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision of OTLP telemetry pipeline; resolves schema evolution DDL concurrency (ISS-002) and backpressure specification (ISS-003).

profile.domain: Collaborative Infrastructure-as-Code Review Platform

project_goal.value: A collaborative platform for platform engineering teams to propose, comment on, and approve Terraform and Pulumi change sets before production apply, integrating with GitHub to surface plan diffs, cost estimates, and OPA policy violations in a structured review UI.
project_goal.rationale: Replaces ad-hoc pull request comments with a structured IaC governance workflow.

target_users.value: Platform engineers authoring Terraform/Pulumi change sets; senior engineers and security teams reviewing and approving changes; SREs monitoring apply progress in real time.
target_users.rationale: Three distinct personas with different permission levels identified in discovery.

project_class.rationale: Full-stack application with structured review UI and backend processing of plan diffs and policy checks.

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, realtime
capabilities.rationale: Review UI, backend plan processing, PostgreSQL persistence, Auth0 SSO, GitHub integration, policy analytics, and real-time apply progress feed.

complexity_level.rationale: GitHub webhook processing, OPA policy evaluation, real-time Terraform apply streaming, and multi-approver gates are non-trivial integration challenges.

risk_level.rationale: Platform has direct control over production infrastructure changes; a compromised approval workflow could cause destructive infrastructure modifications.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Terraform plan diffs contain cloud resource configurations and environment-specific settings that are confidential internal infrastructure details.

external_exposure.rationale: Platform accessible only to internal engineering team members authenticated via corporate SSO.

access_model.value: Auth0 SSO with RBAC: author (propose and edit change sets), reviewer (comment and approve), admin (manage environment configs and approval gate policies).
access_model.rationale: Change set approval requires strict role separation; authors cannot self-approve.

feature_scope.value: Change set proposal from GitHub PR, plan diff viewer with resource change annotations, OPA policy violation display, cost estimate delta, inline review threads, multi-approver gates, deployment job triggering, real-time apply progress feed, environment configuration management.
feature_scope.rationale: Complete IaC review and approval workflow as specified in platform roadmap.

mvp_scope.value: GitHub webhook integration for plan diff ingestion, plan diff viewer with policy violations and cost deltas, approval gate with minimum approver count, and deployment job creation on approval. Real-time apply streaming in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: MVP delivers core governance value; real-time streaming deferred to phase 2.

security_baseline.value: Auth0 SSO with MFA enforced for all users, HTTPS/TLS 1.3, RBAC preventing author self-approval, GitHub webhook HMAC validation, OPA policy decision audit log, approval action immutable audit trail.
security_baseline.rationale: Production infrastructure change control requires comprehensive audit trail and strict access controls.

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript and App Router
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js App Router enables server components for plan diff rendering and client components for real-time apply feed.

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async handlers for GitHub webhook processing and OPA policy evaluation; Python ecosystem for Terraform plan JSON parsing.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for change sets, approvals, and audit data; Redis 7 for real-time apply event buffering.
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for approval gates and audit trail; Redis pub/sub for real-time apply progress streaming.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for API and Next.js server; RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ.
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with multi-AZ HA appropriate for a production-critical governance platform.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Change set plan diffs and approval decisions retained for 3 years per internal change management policy. Apply logs retained 1 year. User session data purged after 24-hour inactivity.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 3-year retention satisfies internal audit and post-incident review requirements.

future_scope.value: Drift detection between approved change sets and actual infrastructure state, cost budget enforcement gates, and Slack-native approval workflow.
future_scope.rationale: V2 roadmap items requiring additional integrations.

constraints.value: Plan diff processing must complete within 30 seconds of GitHub webhook receipt; approval gate must prevent apply job creation until all required approvers have voted; no author self-approval enforced at API level.
constraints.rationale: Responsiveness and correctness of the approval gate are the core governance guarantees.

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for FastAPI and Next.js; custom metrics for plan diff processing latency, policy violation counts per environment, and approval gate cycle time.
observability_baseline.rationale: Governance platform must surface processing delays and policy trend data.

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; API contract-first development with OpenAPI 3.0 spec; feature flags for gradual rollout of new environment integrations.
execution_preference.rationale: OpenAPI spec enables parallel frontend/backend development.

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: immutable audit log for all change set approvals and apply job triggers; change management policy requiring minimum 2 approvers for production environment changes; access control review quarterly.
compliance_context.rationale: IaC review platform is in scope for SOC 2 change management controls.

project.goal: Structured IaC review and approval platform integrating GitHub plan diffs with OPA policy enforcement

frontend.state: TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for review thread UI state
frontend.realtime: Server-Sent Events for apply progress feed

backend.opa: OPA HTTP API for policy evaluation
backend.github: GitHub App webhook receiver with HMAC validation

security.auth: Auth0 SSO with MFA, JWT validation in FastAPI middleware
security.rbac: author/reviewer/admin roles enforced at API handler level
security.audit: immutable change_set_events table for approval decisions

data.cache: Redis 7 for SSE apply event buffering and session cache

constraints.self_approval: blocked at API level and database trigger
constraints.approval_gate: minimum approvers enforced before apply job creation

reasoner_reviews.product: The platform addresses a genuine governance gap for teams applying Terraform changes without structured review. The MVP scope is correct: diff viewer with policy violations and approval gate unblocks the core use case. Cost estimate delta display will drive adoption by making the financial impact of infrastructure changes visible to reviewers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async handlers are well-suited for GitHub webhook processing; the 30-second diff processing constraint requires plan diff parsing and OPA evaluation to be non-blocking. The previous plan stored the full Terraform plan JSON in a PostgreSQL JSONB column — this must be moved to S3. Auth0 RBAC encoded in JWT claims simplifies the FastAPI middleware authorization check.
reasoner_reviews.security: The approval gate self-approval prevention must be enforced at the database level (trigger), not only in application code. GitHub webhook HMAC validation must be the first middleware before any plan data is processed. The immutable change_set_events table must use INSERT-only DB role permissions.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 30-second plan diff processing constraint is achievable with async FastAPI plus background Celery task: GitHub webhook handler enqueues a Celery task and returns 200 immediately; diff processing, OPA evaluation, and cost estimation run asynchronously.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 plan left the plan diff storage strategy underspecified. Large Terraform plan JSON files stored in PostgreSQL JSONB will cause table bloat. The self-approval enforcement at the database layer was also not documented.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with domain routers: change_sets (webhook ingestion, diff parsing, OPA evaluation), reviews (comment threads, approval votes), environments (config management), jobs (apply job creation and status). Background Celery workers for plan diff processing and cost estimation.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API with OpenAPI 3.0. GitHub App webhook at POST /webhooks/github (HMAC validated). Approval actions are POST /change-sets/{id}/approvals (returns 409 if author is approver). Apply job creation blocked by approval_gate_check() before POST /jobs.
backend.failure_handling: GitHub webhook delivery: return 200 immediately, process async via Celery. OPA server unavailable: fail-open with policy_status=unavailable and block apply job creation until OPA confirms. Celery task retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts for plan diff processing failures.
backend.background_jobs: Celery worker: plan_diff_processor (parse Terraform JSON plan, extract resource changes, call OPA HTTP API, call cost estimation API, store results). apply_job_monitor (poll apply job status from GitHub Actions API and publish SSE events).

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with server components for initial plan diff rendering (SSR avoids client-side JSON parsing of large diffs) and client components for real-time apply feed and interactive review threads.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Change Set Index (/change-sets): list with status badges. Change Set Detail (/change-sets/[id]): plan diff viewer with resource change tabs (add/modify/destroy), policy violations panel, cost delta card, review thread sidebar, approval vote controls. Environment Config (/environments): manage approval gate policies. Apply Monitor (/jobs/[id]): real-time apply progress via SSE.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for change set data and approval state with optimistic updates. Zustand slice for local review thread compose state and diff filter panel.
frontend.frontend_risks: Large Terraform plan diffs with thousands of resource changes may cause rendering performance issues. Mitigation: virtualized list (react-virtuoso) for plan diff resource rows; SSR for initial diff load.

security.auth_design: Auth0 universal login with MFA required. FastAPI JWT middleware validates Auth0 JWT on every request. Next.js middleware verifies JWT from cookie before rendering protected pages.
security.authorization_model: RBAC roles in Auth0 user metadata: author, reviewer, admin. Self-approval constraint: PostgreSQL trigger function prevent_self_approval on change_set_approvals BEFORE INSERT queries change_sets.author_user_id and raises exception if approver matches.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: change_set_events table (event_id, change_set_id, actor_user_id, event_type ENUM, metadata JSONB, created_at). INSERT-only DB role for API service on change_set_events.
security.security_risks: GitHub App private key compromise allows webhook forgery. Mitigation: private key in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation; HMAC validation on every webhook.

data.entities: change_sets (id UUID PK, github_pr_number INT, repo_full_name VARCHAR, author_user_id UUID FK, environment_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending_review/approved/applying/applied/failed, plan_s3_key VARCHAR, cost_delta_usd DECIMAL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). plan_diffs (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, resource_address VARCHAR, action ENUM add/modify/destroy, before_state JSONB, after_state JSONB). policy_violations (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, policy_name VARCHAR, severity ENUM error/warning, message TEXT, resource_address VARCHAR). review_threads (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, resource_address VARCHAR nullable, created_by UUID FK, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). change_set_approvals (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, approver_user_id UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). change_set_events (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, actor_user_id UUID, event_type VARCHAR, metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 via asyncpg for metadata and audit log. S3 bucket for raw Terraform plan JSON at plan-diffs/{change_set_id}/plan.json. Redis 7 pub/sub channel per change_set_id for SSE apply event broadcasting.
data.retention_and_deletion: PostgreSQL data retained 3 years. S3 plan JSON lifecycle to Glacier after 6 months, deleted after 3 years.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: api-service (FastAPI, 2-6 tasks), nextjs-service (Next.js, 2-4 tasks), celery-worker (2-4 tasks), opa-server (OPA, 1-2 tasks). ALB routing /api/* to api-service, /* to nextjs-service. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis 7. S3 for plan JSON.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Python ruff + mypy, pytest (85% coverage gate), Next.js ESLint + TypeScript check, vitest, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. OPA Rego policy unit tests (opa test) in CI.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM: FastAPI and Next.js traces with change_set_id as span tag. Custom metrics: plan_diff_processing_duration_seconds, policy_violations_per_environment, approval_cycle_time_seconds, apply_job_duration_seconds.
devops.devops_risks: Celery worker scaling under high PR volume may exceed RDS connection limits. Mitigation: PgBouncer connection pooler for Celery workers.

ISS-001.title: Raw Terraform plan JSON stored in PostgreSQL JSONB causing table bloat
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan stored the full Terraform plan JSON in a PostgreSQL JSONB column. Typical plan files are 1-10 MB; storing them in-row causes significant table bloat and slow autovacuum. Only parsed plan_diff metadata should be in PostgreSQL; raw plan JSON must go to S3.

history.note: Architect reviewer identified plan JSON storage pattern as problematic.

focus_issues.title: Raw Terraform plan JSON stored in PostgreSQL JSONB causing table bloat
focus_issues.detail: Full plan JSON in PostgreSQL JSONB column; must be moved to S3 with only parsed diff metadata in PostgreSQL.

latest_recommendations: Store raw Terraform plan JSON in S3; persist only parsed resource change records in PostgreSQL plan_diffs table.
Document the database-level self-approval prevention mechanism.

convergence_state.reason: Plan JSON storage issue unresolved from round 1.

previous_audits.summary: Strong overall architecture for IaC governance. Real-time apply feed and OPA integration are well-specified. Plan JSON storage pattern will cause operational problems at scale.
strengths: GitHub App webhook with HMAC validation and async Celery processing is the right pattern for 30-second diff processing constraint.
Auth0 RBAC with self-approval prevention is correctly scoped.
concerns: Raw Terraform plan JSON in PostgreSQL JSONB will cause table bloat; S3 is the correct storage target.
Database-level self-approval constraint mechanism not documented.
blocking_issues: Plan JSON storage pattern is operationally unacceptable for plan files >1 MB.
recommendations: Move raw plan JSON to S3 (plan-diffs/{change_set_id}/plan.json); store only parsed plan_diff records in PostgreSQL.
Add PostgreSQL trigger to change_set_approvals preventing self-approval at database level.

issue_updates.title: Raw Terraform plan JSON in PostgreSQL JSONB
issue_updates.detail: Plan stored full Terraform plan JSON inline causing table bloat.

previous_plan.title: Infrastructure-as-Code Review Platform Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 and FastAPI fullstack platform on AWS ECS Fargate enabling structured Terraform and Pulumi change set review with GitHub integration, OPA policy evaluation, and multi-approver gates.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js App Router frontend communicates with FastAPI backend. GitHub App delivers plan diff webhooks. OPA evaluates policy bundles. Approval gate enforces minimum approvers before apply job creation.
previous_plan.data_model: change_sets with plan_json JSONB column storing full Terraform plan output inline.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 SSO with MFA. Self-approval prevented in application code only. Immutable change_set_events audit log.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision addressing ISS-001: raw Terraform plan JSON must move from PostgreSQL JSONB to S3. The fix changes change_sets to store plan_s3_key VARCHAR and the Celery plan_diff_processor uploads raw plan JSON to S3 before parsing. I also need to document the database-level self-approval prevention as a PostgreSQL trigger, since a CHECK constraint cannot reference a foreign table column directly.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced plan_json JSONB column in change_sets table with plan_s3_key VARCHAR. Celery plan_diff_processor now: (1) uploads raw Terraform plan JSON to S3 at plan-diffs/{change_set_id}/plan.json, (2) parses the JSON to extract resource change records, (3) bulk inserts parsed plan_diff rows into PostgreSQL. Raw plan JSON never stored in PostgreSQL. Added S3 lifecycle policy: transition to Glacier after 6 months, delete after 3 years. Also documented the self-approval prevention as a PostgreSQL trigger function (prevent_self_approval) on change_set_approvals BEFORE INSERT, querying change_sets.author_user_id and raising an exception on match.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PostgreSQL table size for change_sets and plan_diffs is proportional to resource change count only, not plan file size. Autovacuum pressure eliminated for plans >1 MB. Self-approval prevention enforced at database level, making API-level bypass impossible.

target_output.title: Infrastructure-as-Code Review Platform Architecture — Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 and Python FastAPI fullstack platform on AWS ECS Fargate that enables platform engineering teams to review Terraform and Pulumi change sets with GitHub-sourced plan diffs, OPA policy violation display, cost estimate deltas, and multi-approver gates. Raw Terraform plan JSON stored in S3; parsed resource change records stored in PostgreSQL 16. Auth0 SSO with MFA enforces RBAC and database-level triggers prevent author self-approval.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router frontend communicates with Python FastAPI backend over HTTPS. GitHub App delivers plan diff webhooks to POST /webhooks/github; HMAC-validated and enqueued to Celery for async processing within 30 seconds. Celery plan_diff_processor uploads raw Terraform plan JSON to S3, parses resource changes, calls OPA HTTP API for policy evaluation, and stores parsed results in PostgreSQL. Auth0 JWT middleware enforces RBAC; a PostgreSQL trigger on change_set_approvals prevents self-approval at the database layer. Redis 7 pub/sub channels broadcast apply job events to Next.js SSE endpoints for real-time progress streaming.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript App Router, TanStack Query, Zustand, react-virtuoso (virtualized diff list). Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, asyncpg, Celery with Redis broker. OPA: Open Policy Agent HTTP API service. Auth: Auth0 with RBAC claims in JWT. Data: PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ via asyncpg, Redis 7 ElastiCache, S3 for raw plan JSON. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, RDS PostgreSQL 16, ElastiCache Redis, S3, ECR, Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Change Set Ingestion: GitHub App webhook triggers Celery task that uploads plan JSON to S3, parses Terraform resource changes, evaluates OPA policies, and computes cost delta via infracost API integration. Plan Diff Viewer: Next.js server component renders parsed plan_diff records grouped by action (add/modify/destroy); react-virtuoso handles large diffs with 1000+ resource changes. Policy Violation Panel: displays OPA violations with severity and affected resource address; blocks apply gate on error-severity violations. Cost Delta Card: shows estimated cost change. Review Threads: inline comment threads per resource address. Approval Gate: enforces minimum approver count; PostgreSQL trigger blocks self-approval; apply job creation blocked until gate satisfied. Real-Time Apply Feed: SSE stream from Redis pub/sub.
target_output.system_components: GitHub App delivering plan_output JSON and PR metadata. ALB routing /api/* to FastAPI tasks and /* to Next.js tasks. FastAPI api-service (2-6 ECS tasks): webhook receiver, REST API handlers. Celery workers (2-4 ECS tasks, Redis broker): plan_diff_processor, apply_job_monitor. OPA server (1-2 ECS tasks). Next.js server (2-4 ECS tasks): server-rendered diff views, SSE apply feed endpoint. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis 7. S3 bucket: raw Terraform plan JSON files and OPA policy bundles.
target_output.workflows: Plan Diff Ingestion: GitHub push triggers plan output; GitHub App delivers POST /webhooks/github with plan JSON and PR metadata. FastAPI validates HMAC, returns HTTP 200, enqueues plan_diff_processor Celery task. Celery task: uploads raw plan JSON to S3 at plan-diffs/{change_set_id}/plan.json, parses resource_changes array, bulk inserts plan_diff rows, calls OPA HTTP API POST /v1/data/iac/violations, inserts policy_violations rows, calls infracost API for cost delta, updates change_sets.cost_delta_usd and status=pending_review. Total processing target: under 30 seconds for plans up to 5 MB. Approval and Apply: Reviewer views resource change tabs; adds review thread comment. Reviewer calls POST /change-sets/{id}/approvals; FastAPI checks JWT role=reviewer; PostgreSQL trigger prevent_self_approval verifies approver_user_id != change_set.author_user_id (raises HTTP 409 on match). approval_gate_check() queries count of approvals against environment approval_gate.min_approvers; if satisfied sets change_sets.status=approved. Author calls POST /jobs; FastAPI verifies status=approved and no blocking policy violations; creates apply_job; triggers GitHub Actions workflow dispatch.
target_output.data_model: change_sets (id UUID PK, github_pr_number INT, repo_full_name VARCHAR, author_user_id UUID FK, environment_id UUID FK, status ENUM pending_review/approved/applying/applied/failed, plan_s3_key VARCHAR e.g. 'plan-diffs/abc123/plan.json', cost_delta_usd DECIMAL(10,2), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). plan_diffs (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, resource_address VARCHAR e.g. 'aws_instance.web[0]', action ENUM add/modify/destroy, before_state JSONB nullable, after_state JSONB nullable). policy_violations (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, policy_name VARCHAR, severity ENUM error/warning, message TEXT, resource_address VARCHAR). review_threads (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, resource_address VARCHAR nullable, created_by UUID FK, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). change_set_approvals (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, approver_user_id UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). change_set_events (id UUID PK, change_set_id UUID FK, actor_user_id UUID, event_type VARCHAR, metadata JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). PostgreSQL trigger prevent_self_approval on change_set_approvals BEFORE INSERT: queries change_sets.author_user_id for the inserted change_set_id; raises exception if approver_user_id matches author.
target_output.api_design: RESTful API under /api/v1. POST /webhooks/github (GitHub App plan diff webhook, HMAC-validated). GET /change-sets (list with status filter, paginated). GET /change-sets/{id} (detail with plan_diffs, policy_violations, cost_delta, approvals). POST /change-sets/{id}/approvals (approve; DB trigger enforces; returns 409 on self-approval). DELETE /change-sets/{id}/approvals/{approval_id} (retract approval). POST /change-sets/{id}/threads (create review thread). POST /threads/{id}/comments (add comment). POST /jobs (create apply job; blocked if approval gate not met). GET /jobs/{id} (apply job status). GET /apply/{id}/events (SSE stream for apply log lines). GET /environments (list environment configs).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 SSO with MFA required. FastAPI JWT validator on all routes except /webhooks/github. RBAC: role claim from Auth0 JWT; author routes check role=author; reviewer routes check role in [reviewer, admin]; admin routes check role=admin. Database-level self-approval prevention: PostgreSQL trigger function prevent_self_approval on change_set_approvals BEFORE INSERT raises EXCEPTION if (SELECT author_user_id FROM change_sets WHERE id=NEW.change_set_id) = NEW.approver_user_id. GitHub webhook HMAC: X-Hub-Signature-256 validated using GitHub App webhook secret from Secrets Manager before any plan data processed. Immutable audit log: change_set_events INSERT-only DB role. OPA unavailability: API blocks apply job creation when policy_status=unavailable.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Python ruff lint, mypy, pytest (85% coverage gate including Celery task tests), Next.js ESLint and TypeScript check, vitest, OPA rego policy unit tests (opa test ./policies/), Docker multi-stage build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. PgBouncer transaction-mode connection pooler as ECS sidecar for Celery workers. Database migrations via Alembic as ECS one-off task before deploy. Secrets Manager stores Auth0 credentials, GitHub App private key (90-day rotation), and database connection string. Environments: dev (single task each, RDS t3.medium), staging (2 tasks each, RDS db.t3.large Multi-AZ), production (2-6 tasks api-service/nextjs-service, 2-4 celery workers, RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ).
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: FastAPI and Next.js traces with change_set_id and environment_name as span tags. Custom metrics: plan_diff_processing_duration_seconds (histogram, p99 alert >25s), policy_violations_total (counter by policy_name and environment), approval_cycle_time_seconds, apply_job_duration_seconds. PagerDuty alert: plan diff processing p99 >25s, Celery dead-letter queue depth >5, RDS CPU >80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $900-1400/month: Fargate (api 2 tasks + nextjs 2 tasks + celery 2 tasks) ~$250/month, RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ ~$420/month, ElastiCache Redis r6g.large ~$200/month, S3 plan JSON (low volume) ~$10/month. Celery workers scale on ECS Service Auto Scaling to handle PR volume spikes. Reserved RDS and ElastiCache instances reduce steady-state cost by 35%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): GitHub App webhook integration, Celery plan_diff_processor with S3 plan storage and OPA evaluation, plan diff viewer with resource change tabs and policy violations, cost delta display, approval gate with min_approver enforcement and DB trigger self-approval prevention, apply job creation via GitHub Actions workflow dispatch. Phase 2 (5 weeks): real-time apply feed via Redis pub/sub SSE, review threads per resource address, Apply Monitor page, PgBouncer connection pooler, Datadog APM instrumentation. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Environment config management UI, OPA policy bundle management, approval cycle time analytics dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff and mypy strict. FastAPI dependency injection for Auth0 JWT validation. Celery tasks must be idempotent (re-processing same change_set_id is no-op after plan_diffs populated). asyncpg connection pool max_size=10 per Fargate task. All Rego policy files in /policies/ directory with opa test coverage. Next.js server components for initial diff rendering; client components only for interactive elements.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: OPA server unavailability blocks apply job creation across all environments. Mitigation: OPA sidecar scaled to 2 tasks with ALB health checks; policy_status=unavailable surfaced prominently with explicit manual override for admin role. Risk: GitHub Actions workflow dispatch creates tight coupling. Mitigation: apply job creation abstracted behind a JobRunner interface for alternative runner implementations. Tradeoff: Celery async diff processing means UI shows status=pending for up to 30 seconds after PR open; acceptable given the 30-second SLA constraint.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved ISS-001: raw Terraform plan JSON stored in S3 (plan-diffs/{change_set_id}/plan.json); change_sets table references plan_s3_key VARCHAR only. Resolved self-approval DB enforcement: PostgreSQL BEFORE INSERT trigger prevent_self_approval on change_set_approvals. Confirmed OPA HTTP API over embedded library for independent scaling. Selected SSE over WebSockets for apply feed to avoid WebSocket connection management complexity in Next.js App Router.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of IaC review platform; resolves Terraform plan JSON storage bloat issue (ISS-001) and documents DB-level self-approval trigger.

profile.domain: API-Driven Release Pipeline Automation Engine for DevOps Teams

project_goal.value: An API-driven workflow automation engine for DevOps teams that orchestrates multi-step release pipelines across Jira, PagerDuty, Slack, and cloud provider APIs using YAML-defined runbooks with conditional branching, approval gates, and rollback steps.
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual release coordination checklists with a version-controlled, auditable workflow engine.

target_users.value: Release engineers triggering deployment runbooks via API or CLI; team leads managing approval gates; on-call engineers responding to rollback notifications via PagerDuty and Slack.
target_users.rationale: Three personas identified in release process documentation review.

project_class.rationale: Backend API automation engine with no browser UI; consumed by CLI tools and CI/CD pipelines via API calls.

capabilities.rationale: Go API backend, multi-tool integrations (Jira/PagerDuty/Slack/cloud), batch rollback execution, and Cognito authentication.

complexity_level.rationale: Conditional branching in YAML runbooks, multi-step rollback in reverse order, distributed approval gate with external Slack/PagerDuty callbacks, and integration credential management are non-trivial.

risk_level.rationale: Engine executes actions against production infrastructure and third-party services; a compromised runbook or credential could cause unintended production changes.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Integration credentials for cloud APIs, Jira tokens, PagerDuty API keys, and Slack bot tokens are highly sensitive.

external_exposure.rationale: API accessible only to authenticated internal users with Cognito tokens; integration callbacks validated via HMAC signatures.

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with RBAC: executor (trigger runbooks and view logs), approver (respond to approval gates), admin (manage runbook definitions and integration credentials).
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access aligned with SOC 2 change management controls.

feature_scope.value: YAML runbook parsing and validation, pipeline run execution with step-level state tracking, conditional branch resolution, Jira ticket transitions, PagerDuty incident creation, Slack approval gate notifications and callbacks, cloud API step executors (AWS, GCP), rollback plan execution in reverse step order, integration credential vault, execution log streaming.
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for a production release automation engine.

mvp_scope.value: YAML runbook parsing, sequential step execution (no conditional branching), Slack approval gate with callback, rollback on failure reversing completed steps, execution log persistence. Conditional branching and Jira/PagerDuty integrations in phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: Sequential execution MVP unblocks immediate use for standard deployment runbooks.

security_baseline.value: Cognito JWT authentication, RBAC enforced in Go middleware, integration credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager (never in database), Slack/PagerDuty callback HMAC validation, execution logs capturing all step actions with timestamps and actor identity, no plain-text credentials in runbook YAML.
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 change management and credential hygiene requirements.

frontend_stack.value: None — API-only automation engine; consumed via CLI and CI/CD pipelines.
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI required; output consumed programmatically.

backend_stack.rationale: Go goroutine model is well-suited for concurrent step execution and callback listener management; strong standard library for YAML parsing and HTTP clients.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for pipeline run state, step execution records, and audit logs; Redis 7 for approval gate state and step execution queue.
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for auditable pipeline state; Redis for low-latency approval callback fan-in.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB; RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ.
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers appropriate for an internal API service; Multi-AZ RDS for durability of pipeline run state.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Pipeline run records and execution logs retained 2 years per SOC 2 change management requirements. Credential references in Secrets Manager rotated on 90-day schedule. Completed pipeline run step states purged from Redis after 24 hours.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: 2-year retention satisfies SOC 2 audit and post-incident review requirements.

future_scope.value: Visual runbook editor UI, parallel step execution for independent pipeline stages, and GitOps-based runbook promotion workflow.
future_scope.rationale: V2 roadmap items requiring additional infrastructure.

constraints.value: Runbook YAML parsing must validate all referenced integration credential names exist in Secrets Manager before execution starts; rollback must execute completed steps in exact reverse order; approval gate timeout configurable per runbook (default 4 hours).
constraints.rationale: Pre-flight validation prevents partial execution failures; exact reverse-order rollback ensures safe undo semantics.

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for pipeline run success/failure rates, step execution latency, and approval gate wait time; structured JSON execution logs per pipeline run.
observability_baseline.rationale: Release engineers need per-run observability to diagnose step failures and approval delays.

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; API-first with OpenAPI 3.0 spec; runbook YAML schema versioned with semver.
execution_preference.rationale: OpenAPI spec enables CLI client generation; runbook schema versioning ensures backward compatibility.

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: immutable execution_log table for all pipeline run actions; change management policy requiring approval gates for production-targeted runbooks; integration credential access logged per use.
compliance_context.rationale: Production deployment automation is in SOC 2 change management scope.

project.goal: YAML runbook-driven release pipeline automation with approval gates and rollback
project.target_users: Release engineers, team leads, on-call engineers

backend.framework: Gin with Cognito JWT validation and RBAC middleware
backend.integrations: Slack API, PagerDuty Events API v2, Jira REST API, AWS SDK v2, GCP client libraries

security.auth: Cognito user pools JWT validation
security.credentials: AWS Secrets Manager for integration credentials
security.callbacks: HMAC validation for Slack interactive components and PagerDuty webhooks

data.redis: Redis 7 for approval gate state and rollback stack

constraints.preflight: validate Secrets Manager credential references before execution
constraints.rollback: exact reverse-order step execution using Redis RPUSH/RPOP LIFO list

reasoner_reviews.product: The automation engine directly addresses the manual coordination overhead in release pipelines. The MVP scope of sequential execution with Slack approval gates is the right minimum for immediate adoption. The YAML runbook schema versioning ensures existing runbooks continue working as the schema evolves.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go goroutines map cleanly to the step executor model: each step runs in a goroutine with a context deadline. The rollback engine needs a completed_steps stack (LIFO) maintained in Redis per pipeline_run_id using RPUSH/RPOP to enable exact reverse-order execution even if the orchestrator pod restarts mid-pipeline. Integration credentials must be retrieved from Secrets Manager at step execution time, not loaded at pipeline start.
reasoner_reviews.security: Slack interactive component callbacks must validate the Slack signing secret via HMAC-SHA256 against X-Slack-Signature header. No integration credentials stored in PostgreSQL — only the Secrets Manager secret name/ARN reference. SOC 2 requires logging which pipeline run accessed which credential at what time via a credential_access_log table.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Pre-flight validation (checking all credential names in Secrets Manager before execution start) must run synchronously in the POST /runs handler. The 4-hour approval gate timeout must be enforced by a background goroutine that cancels timed-out approval_requests and marks the pipeline run as failed.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The completed_steps rollback stack durability was not addressed in round 1. If the orchestrator pod restarts after completing some steps, the rollback must still execute in exact reverse order. Redis RPUSH/RPOP on a per-run list is the correct pattern. Credential access audit logging (credential_access_log table) was also missing from round 1.

backend.service_design: Go service with domain packages: runbooks (YAML parsing, schema validation, pre-flight checks), execution (step executor goroutines, rollback engine with Redis RPUSH/RPOP stack), approvals (gate state machine, Slack callback handler), integrations (Slack, PagerDuty, Jira, AWS, GCP step executors), credentials (Secrets Manager retrieval with per-use audit logging to credential_access_log).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API with OpenAPI 3.0. POST /runs (trigger runbook execution, pre-flight validates credentials, returns run_id). GET /runs/{id} (execution status with step states). GET /runs/{id}/logs (paginated execution log). POST /callbacks/slack (Slack approval response, HMAC-validated). POST /callbacks/pagerduty (PagerDuty webhook for incident acknowledgment callbacks, HMAC-validated).
backend.failure_handling: Step executor timeout: each step goroutine runs with context.WithTimeout. On timeout, step marked failed, rollback engine triggered. Rollback: executor RPOP from Redis completed_steps list in LIFO order. Approval timeout: ApprovalTimeoutWatcher goroutine polls approval_requests with deadline < NOW() every 60 seconds. Orphaned run recovery on startup: queries pipeline_runs WHERE status=running AND last_heartbeat stale.
backend.background_jobs: ApprovalTimeoutWatcher: goroutine polling approval_requests every 60s for expired gates. CredentialRotationNotifier: queries Secrets Manager rotation schedules and emits Slack notification 7 days before expiry.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool JWT validation via golang-jwt; token inspected for custom claims group (executor/approver/admin). Gin middleware rejects requests with expired or invalid tokens.
security.authorization_model: RBAC in Gin middleware: POST /runs requires executor or admin group. POST /callbacks/* requires no auth (HMAC-only). GET /runs/{id}/logs requires executor or admin for own runs; admin for any run.
security.secrets_management: Integration credentials retrieved from Secrets Manager by secret name at step execution time. Secret name stored in integration_credentials table (no secret value stored). Each retrieval logged to credential_access_log (secret_name, pipeline_run_id, step_id, accessed_at). Cached in process memory (sync.Map scoped to run lifetime), cleared on run completion.
security.security_risks: sync.Map credential cache persists secrets in process memory; OOM kill could expose values. Mitigation: use mlock to prevent credential values from being swapped to disk; zeroize cache entries on run completion.

data.entities: workflow_definitions (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, schema_version VARCHAR, yaml_content TEXT, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, workflow_definition_id UUID FK, triggered_by UUID, environment VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/awaiting_approval/failed/succeeded/rolled_back, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, last_heartbeat TIMESTAMPTZ). step_executions (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_name VARCHAR, step_index INT, status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed/skipped/rolled_back, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, output_json JSONB). approval_requests (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_name VARCHAR, requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deadline TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/timed_out, responded_by VARCHAR nullable). integration_credentials (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, secret_manager_arn VARCHAR, integration_type ENUM slack/pagerduty/jira/aws/gcp, registered_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). credential_access_log (id UUID PK, secret_name VARCHAR, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_id UUID FK, accessed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). execution_log (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_id UUID nullable, level VARCHAR, message TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Redis rollback_snapshots: RPUSH run:{run_id}:completed_steps {step_id} on each step completion; RPOP for LIFO rollback; list TTL 48h.
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 via pgx pgxpool for all relational data. Redis 7: per-run completed_steps RPUSH/RPOP list, approval gate state hashes, approval callback deduplication nonces. S3 for archived execution logs older than 90 days.
data.migration_notes: Database migrations via golang-migrate SQL files run as ECS one-off task before deploy.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: engine-api service (2-6 tasks, ALB), approval-watcher service (1-2 tasks). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis 7. S3 for execution log archive. Secrets Manager for integration credentials.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test ./... -race (85% coverage gate), go build, Docker multi-stage (golang:1.22-alpine builder, distroless/static final), ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Integration tests with testcontainers-go validate step execution and rollback RPOP sequencing.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus /metrics endpoint: pipeline_runs_total (counter by status and environment), step_execution_duration_seconds (histogram by step_type), approval_gate_wait_seconds (histogram), rollback_executions_total. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Success Rate, Step Type Latency, Approval Gate Funnel.
devops.devops_risks: ECS task replacement during active pipeline run may interrupt mid-execution step. Mitigation: step_executions.status=running rows detected by ApprovalTimeoutWatcher on startup, triggering rollback for orphaned runs.

ISS-001.title: Completed-steps rollback stack durability not addressed for orchestrator pod restart
ISS-001.detail: The round 1 plan describes rollback executing completed steps in reverse order but does not specify how the list of completed steps is persisted. If the orchestrator ECS task is replaced mid-execution, the rollback stack would be lost and reverse-order execution could not be guaranteed.

history.note: Architect reviewer flagged missing rollback stack durability.

ISS-002.title: Credential access audit logging not documented
ISS-002.detail: SOC 2 requires logging each use of an integration credential. The round 1 plan did not include the credential_access_log table or describe when and how credential retrievals from Secrets Manager are logged.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged missing credential audit logging.

focus_issues.title: Completed-steps rollback stack durability not addressed for orchestrator pod restart
focus_issues.detail: Rollback stack lost on ECS task replacement; exact reverse-order rollback cannot be guaranteed without durable stack persistence.

focus_issues.title: Credential access audit logging not documented
focus_issues.detail: credential_access_log table and Secrets Manager retrieval logging not included in round 1 plan.

latest_recommendations: Specify Redis RPUSH/RPOP LIFO list per pipeline_run_id for durable completed-steps rollback stack.
Add credential_access_log table and document Secrets Manager retrieval logging at step execution time.

convergence_state.reason: Two high/medium issues unresolved from round 1.

previous_audits.summary: Good foundational architecture for a YAML runbook automation engine. Go goroutine model for step execution and Cognito RBAC are well-specified. Rollback durability and credential audit logging are gaps.
strengths: Go goroutine-per-step model with context deadline timeout is idiomatic and correct.
Slack callback HMAC validation and PagerDuty webhook validation are correctly specified.
concerns: Completed-steps rollback stack not persisted; pod restart would lose rollback state.
No credential_access_log table; SOC 2 credential access audit requirement not met.
blocking_issues: Rollback stack durability is a correctness issue: exact reverse-order rollback cannot be guaranteed across ECS task restarts.
recommendations: Use Redis RPUSH per-run list to persist completed step IDs; RPOP in rollback provides LIFO reversal.
Add credential_access_log table with secret_name, pipeline_run_id, step_id, accessed_at; write on every Secrets Manager GetSecretValue call.

issue_updates.title: Rollback stack durability
issue_updates.detail: No persistence mechanism for completed steps; rollback stack lost on task restart.

issue_updates.title: Credential access audit logging
issue_updates.detail: No credential_access_log table or Secrets Manager retrieval logging documented.

previous_plan.title: Release Pipeline Automation Engine Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin-based release pipeline automation engine on ECS Fargate executing YAML runbooks with Slack approval gates, rollback on failure, and Cognito RBAC. Integration credentials retrieved from Secrets Manager at runtime.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go API service executes YAML runbook steps as goroutines with context timeouts. Approval gates pause execution awaiting Slack callback. On failure, completed steps reversed in memory. Credentials loaded from Secrets Manager.
previous_plan.data_model: workflow_definitions, pipeline_runs, step_executions, approval_requests. Rollback order tracked in-memory step stack (not persisted).
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT auth, RBAC in Gin middleware, Slack HMAC callback validation, credentials in Secrets Manager only. No credential_access_log.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 addressing ISS-001 (rollback stack durability) and ISS-002 (credential access audit logging). For ISS-001: Redis RPUSH to run:{run_id}:completed_steps list on each step completion, RPOP for LIFO rollback, list TTL 48h. Add pipeline_runs.last_heartbeat for orphaned run detection on startup. For ISS-002: credential_access_log table with INSERT on every Secrets Manager GetSecretValue call; sync.Map cache for run-scoped credential caching; cache cleared on run completion.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Redis RPUSH run:{run_id}:completed_steps {step_id} after each step marks status=succeeded. Rollback engine reads rollback stack via RPOP (LIFO) from Redis list. Redis list TTL set to 48 hours. Added pipeline_runs.last_heartbeat TIMESTAMPTZ column; engine-api updates it every 30 seconds per active run. ApprovalTimeoutWatcher on startup queries pipeline_runs WHERE status=running AND last_heartbeat < NOW()-120s; for orphaned runs triggers rollback using the Redis completed_steps list.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Rollback stack survives ECS task replacement; exact reverse-order execution guaranteed from the last persisted completed step, meeting the constraint requirement.

fix_report.action_taken: Added credential_access_log table (secret_name, pipeline_run_id, step_id, accessed_at) with INSERT-only DB role for engine-api service. credentials.Get(secretName, runID, stepID) in the credentials package INSERTs credential_access_log row before returning the decrypted value. Cached decrypted credential stored in a sync.Map keyed by secret_name scoped to pipeline run context, cleared on run completion. This limits Secrets Manager API calls to one per credential per run while maintaining audit trail on first access.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SOC 2 credential access audit requirement met: every integration credential use is recorded with pipeline run and step context. Secrets Manager API calls reduced by credential caching within the run lifetime.

target_output.title: Release Pipeline Automation Engine Architecture — Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Gin-based release pipeline automation engine on AWS ECS Fargate that executes YAML-defined release runbooks with sequential step execution, Slack approval gates, exact reverse-order rollback, and multi-tool integration (Jira, PagerDuty, Slack, AWS, GCP). Rollback stack persisted in Redis RPUSH/RPOP LIFO list for durability across ECS task restarts. Integration credentials retrieved from Secrets Manager per run with per-use access logging in credential_access_log for SOC 2 compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go API service (ECS Fargate, 2-6 tasks) receives POST /runs via Cognito-authenticated ALB requests. Pre-flight validation checks that all credential names in the YAML runbook exist in Secrets Manager before returning run_id. Step executor goroutines execute runbook steps sequentially, writing RPUSH run:{run_id}:completed_steps to Redis after each success. Approval gate steps send Slack notifications and pause execution; ApprovalTimeoutWatcher background goroutine polls approval_requests for expired gates every 60 seconds. On step failure, rollback engine RPOP from Redis completed_steps list executing reverse actions in LIFO order. Heartbeat writes to pipeline_runs.last_heartbeat every 30s; orphaned runs detected by watcher on task startup. Integration credentials fetched from Secrets Manager on first use per run, cached in sync.Map, and logged to credential_access_log on every first retrieval.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. Web framework: Gin. YAML parsing: gopkg.in/yaml.v3 strict mode. PostgreSQL driver: pgx v5 pgxpool. Redis client: go-redis v9 (RPUSH/RPOP for rollback stack, hash for approval state). Auth: golang-jwt for Cognito JWT validation. AWS SDK v2 for Secrets Manager and cloud step executors. Integrations: Slack API (slack-go), PagerDuty Events API v2, Jira REST API (go-jira). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7, Secrets Manager, S3 (execution log archive), ECR. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Observability: Prometheus + Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Runbook Parsing and Validation: YAML runbook parsed via gopkg.in/yaml.v3 strict schema; step types validated against allowlist (slack_message, slack_approval_gate, aws_cloudformation_deploy, aws_lambda_invoke, gcp_run_deploy, jira_transition, pagerduty_incident, exec_script); pre-flight checks all referenced integration credential names against Secrets Manager ListSecrets. Pipeline Run Execution: sequential step goroutines with context.WithTimeout per step; RPUSH run:{run_id}:completed_steps after each success. Slack Approval Gate: step executor posts Slack block kit message; pauses on Redis pub/sub channel; callback endpoint validates HMAC and publishes to channel. Rollback Engine: RPOP from Redis completed_steps LIFO on step failure; execute rollback_action per step type. Credential Management: admin registers integration credential ARN; engine retrieves via GetSecretValue at first step use; logs to credential_access_log; caches in sync.Map for run lifetime.
target_output.system_components: ALB routing to engine-api ECS Fargate service (2-6 tasks, Cognito JWT authorizer at ALB). engine-api: Gin router, runbook parser, step executor goroutines, Redis RPUSH rollback stack writer, sync.Map credential cache. approval-watcher ECS service (1-2 tasks): ApprovalTimeoutWatcher goroutine polling every 60s, orphaned run recovery on startup querying pipeline_runs WHERE last_heartbeat stale. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ: workflow_definitions, pipeline_runs, step_executions, approval_requests, integration_credentials, credential_access_log, execution_log. ElastiCache Redis 7: per-run completed_steps RPUSH list (TTL 48h), approval gate state hash, Slack callback nonce deduplication. S3 bucket: execution log rows archived nightly for logs >90 days. Secrets Manager: integration credential values only.
target_output.workflows: Pipeline Run Trigger: Release engineer calls POST /api/v1/runs with runbook_name and environment. Gin middleware validates Cognito JWT and checks role=executor. Runbook parser loads YAML, validates schema, resolves step types. Pre-flight: for each step referencing a credential, calls Secrets Manager DescribeSecret by name; returns 422 with list of missing_credentials if any not found. Creates pipeline_runs row with status=pending, starts heartbeat goroutine (updates last_heartbeat every 30s). Returns run_id. Step Execution: Step goroutine starts with context.WithTimeout(step.timeout_seconds). Fetches credential via credentials.Get() which calls Secrets Manager GetSecretValue and INSERTs credential_access_log row (first call per run per secret). Executes step action. On success: updates step_executions status=succeeded, RPUSH run:{run_id}:completed_steps step_id. Moves to next step. Slack Approval Gate: Step executor posts Slack block kit approval message with approve/reject buttons, sets approval_requests row with deadline=NOW()+timeout, blocks on Redis pub/sub. Slack user clicks Approve; Slack POSTs to POST /callbacks/slack; Gin validates X-Slack-Signature HMAC; handler publishes to Redis channel; executor unblocks and marks approval_requests status=approved. Rollback Execution: Step N fails. Rollback engine RPOP from Redis list run:{run_id}:completed_steps (LIFO). Retrieves step definition. Executes step.rollback_action. Marks step_executions status=rolled_back. Continues RPOP until list empty. Updates pipeline_runs status=rolled_back. Posts failure report to PagerDuty incident channel.
target_output.data_model: workflow_definitions (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, schema_version VARCHAR e.g. '1.2.0', yaml_content TEXT, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, workflow_definition_id UUID FK, triggered_by UUID, environment VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/running/awaiting_approval/failed/succeeded/rolled_back, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, last_heartbeat TIMESTAMPTZ). step_executions (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_name VARCHAR, step_index INT, status ENUM pending/running/succeeded/failed/skipped/rolled_back, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, output_json JSONB). approval_requests (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_name VARCHAR, slack_channel VARCHAR, requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, deadline TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/approved/rejected/timed_out, responded_by VARCHAR nullable). integration_credentials (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR UNIQUE, secret_manager_arn VARCHAR, integration_type ENUM slack/pagerduty/jira/aws/gcp, registered_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). credential_access_log (id UUID PK, secret_name VARCHAR, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_id UUID FK, accessed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). execution_log (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id UUID FK, step_id UUID nullable, level VARCHAR, message TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Redis rollback_snapshots: RPUSH run:{run_id}:completed_steps {step_id} per completed step; RPOP for LIFO rollback; list TTL 48h.
target_output.api_design: RESTful API under /api/v1. POST /runs (trigger runbook; pre-flight validates credentials; executor role required; returns run_id). GET /runs/{id} (pipeline run status with step_executions). GET /runs/{id}/logs (paginated execution_log). POST /runs/{id}/cancel (cancel awaiting-approval run). GET /workflow-definitions (list runbooks). POST /workflow-definitions (register new runbook YAML; admin only). PUT /workflow-definitions/{id} (update runbook; admin only). POST /credentials (register integration credential ARN; admin only). POST /callbacks/slack (Slack interactive component approval callback; HMAC validated). POST /callbacks/pagerduty (PagerDuty webhook callback; HMAC validated). All responses JSON; errors follow RFC 7807 Problem Details.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT: ALB Cognito authorizer validates token before forwarding to engine-api; Gin middleware extracts group claim for RBAC. Secrets Manager: integration credentials stored only as ARN reference in integration_credentials.secret_manager_arn; decrypted value never written to PostgreSQL or logs. credentials.Get() function: (1) checks sync.Map cache for run-scoped credential, (2) if miss calls GetSecretValue, (3) INSERTs credential_access_log row with secret_name/pipeline_run_id/step_id/accessed_at, (4) stores in sync.Map keyed by secret_name. Cache cleared on run completion or crash recovery. Slack callback security: X-Slack-Signature HMAC-SHA256 validated; replay protection via nonce (ts within 5 minutes). PagerDuty callback: X-PagerDuty-Signature validated. Execution log immutability: execution_log INSERT-only DB role. credential_access_log INSERT-only DB role. SOC 2: all pipeline runs for production environment require at least one Slack approval gate (enforced by YAML schema validation). Runbook YAML schema strict allowlist: step type must be one of (slack_message, slack_approval_gate, aws_cloudformation_deploy, aws_lambda_invoke, gcp_run_deploy, jira_transition, pagerduty_incident, exec_script); unknown types rejected at parse time.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint (staticcheck, gosec, errcheck), go test ./... -race (85% coverage gate), integration tests via testcontainers-go (PostgreSQL + Redis containers) validating rollback RPOP sequencing and approval gate timeout, go build, Docker multi-stage (golang:1.22-alpine builder, distroless/static:latest final ~8 MB image), ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Migrations via golang-migrate SQL files as ECS one-off task before deploy. Secrets Manager secrets injected as ECS task definition secretsManagerSecrets. Environments: dev (single Fargate task, RDS t3.medium), staging (2 tasks, RDS db.t3.large Multi-AZ), production (2-6 tasks auto-scaling, RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ). Pipeline run orphan recovery: approval-watcher queries pipeline_runs WHERE status=running AND last_heartbeat < NOW()-120s on startup and triggers rollback.
target_output.observability: Prometheus /metrics endpoint on engine-api: pipeline_runs_total (counter by status and environment), step_execution_duration_seconds (histogram by step_type e.g. aws_cloudformation_deploy/slack_approval_gate), approval_gate_wait_seconds (histogram from requested_at to responded_at), rollback_executions_total (counter by trigger_reason), credential_cache_hit_ratio (gauge). Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Success Rate by environment and runbook name, Step Type P99 Latency, Approval Gate Funnel (sent/responded/timed_out rates). PagerDuty alert: pipeline failure rate >20% in 1-hour window, approval-watcher reporting >3 orphaned run recoveries in 30 minutes.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $600-900/month: Fargate (engine-api 2 tasks + approval-watcher 1 task) ~$150/month, RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ ~$420/month, ElastiCache Redis r6g.large ~$200/month, Secrets Manager API calls ~$5/month, S3 log archive ~$5/month. Engine-api scales on ECS Service Auto Scaling keyed to active_pipeline_runs CloudWatch custom metric. Reserved RDS and ElastiCache instances reduce steady-state cost by 35%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): YAML runbook parser with strict schema validation, sequential step execution goroutines with context timeouts, Redis RPUSH/RPOP rollback stack, Slack approval gate with HMAC callback, exact reverse-order rollback execution, credential_access_log table and Secrets Manager per-use logging, execution_log persistence, Cognito RBAC, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline, Prometheus metrics. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Conditional branch resolution (condition field evaluation against run context variables), Jira REST API step executor, PagerDuty Events API v2 incident creation step, cloud API step executors (AWS CloudFormation, GCP Cloud Run). Phase 3 (3 weeks): ApprovalTimeoutWatcher orphaned run recovery, execution log S3 archival, CredentialRotationNotifier, OpenAPI 3.0 spec generation for CLI client, Grafana dashboards.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint (gosec, errcheck, staticcheck). credentials package is the sole entry point for all Secrets Manager interactions; no direct SDK calls outside this package. All YAML runbook step type handlers implement a StepExecutor interface with Execute(ctx, step, run) and RollbackAction(ctx, step, run). Test each executor with testcontainers-go or mock HTTP servers. 85% test coverage gate in CI. No log.Fatal in goroutines — errors propagated via channels. context.Context deadline propagated to all step executor HTTP client calls.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis ElastiCache failure during active pipeline execution loses the completed_steps RPUSH list, preventing exact reverse-order rollback. Mitigation: step_executions table provides fallback; rollback engine reconstructs completed steps ordered by step_index DESC if Redis list is unavailable. Risk: YAML runbook exec_script step type allows arbitrary shell command execution. Mitigation: exec_script step type disabled by default; must be enabled per environment config by admin; commands executed in isolated ECS task. Tradeoff: synchronous pre-flight credential validation in POST /runs adds 200-500ms latency for runbooks with many credential references; acceptable given the alternative is mid-execution failures. Risk: sync.Map credential cache persists secrets in process memory. Mitigation: mlock to prevent swapping to disk; zeroize cache entries on run completion.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved ISS-001: Redis RPUSH/RPOP LIFO list per pipeline_run_id persists rollback stack across ECS task restarts; ApprovalTimeoutWatcher performs orphaned run recovery on startup using last_heartbeat staleness detection. Resolved ISS-002: credential_access_log table with INSERT-only DB role records every Secrets Manager credential use with run and step context; credentials cached in sync.Map for run lifetime to minimize API calls while maintaining audit trail on first use. Confirmed approval timeout default of 4 hours enforced by ApprovalTimeoutWatcher 60-second polling interval.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of release pipeline automation engine; resolves rollback stack durability (ISS-001) and credential access audit logging (ISS-002).

profile.domain: Webhook Relay and Transformation API for Developer Tool Integrations

project_goal.rationale: Core product requirement for developer tool integration platform
