project.goal: Webhook relay with JSONata transformation and delivery guarantees

security.auth: HMAC-SHA256 on inbound, API key on management

reasoner_reviews.product: The webhook relay is well-scoped for MVP. JSONata as the transformation DSL is correct for declarative payload reshaping. The replay API is essential for operational debugging and must be in MVP scope.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Chi on Go 1.22 is appropriate for high-throughput inbound handling. Redis sorted sets for retry scheduling with backoff delay scores is the right data structure. The delivery worker must use atomic Redis claiming to prevent duplicate delivery on restart.
reasoner_reviews.security: HMAC-SHA256 validation must occur before any database write to prevent replay attacks. Signing secrets must never be logged. Payload size must be capped at 1MB to prevent amplification.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 10000 events per minute is achievable with Go goroutine delivery workers. pgxpool connection pooling is critical for worker throughput.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The delivery worker lacks idempotency specification. Redis Lua atomic claim is needed to prevent duplicate delivery on worker crash and restart.

backend.service_design: Modular Go service: inbound handler, JSONata transformation engine, delivery worker pool, replay service, management API
backend.api_patterns: REST management API, throughput-optimized inbound handler
backend.background_jobs: Delivery worker goroutine pool with Redis Lua atomic claim and exponential backoff retry scheduler
backend.failure_handling: Exponential backoff with jitter, dead-letter after max retries, circuit breaker for consistently failing consumers

security.auth_design: HMAC-SHA256 validation with constant-time comparison, signing secrets from Secrets Manager with 5-minute cache
security.secrets_management: Signing secrets in AWS Secrets Manager via ECS task IAM role, never in logs or database
security.audit_and_logging_controls: delivery_attempts table append-only with consumer endpoint, HTTP status, latency, outcome

data.entities: webhook_endpoints, inbound_events, transformation_rules, delivery_attempts, consumer_registrations, replay_requests, signing_secrets
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL for event metadata and delivery audit log; Redis sorted sets for retry queue with backoff delay scores
data.retention_and_deletion: inbound_events 30 days, delivery_attempts 90 days, automated cleanup batch job

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate separate task definitions for inbound API and delivery worker pool
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go test -race, go vet, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with delivery_success_rate, delivery_latency_p99, retry_queue_depth, inbound_events_per_minute

ISS-001.title: Delivery worker lacks idempotency on restart
ISS-001.detail: Worker crashes after delivering to consumer but before acknowledging Redis will cause redelivery. No atomic claim mechanism specified.

history.note: Identified in round 1 audit

focus_issues.title: Delivery worker lacks idempotency on restart
focus_issues.detail: Must use Redis Lua atomic claim to mark events in-flight before processing with crash recovery timeout.

latest_recommendations: Add Redis Lua EVALSHA atomic claim for delivery worker to prevent duplicate delivery on restart
Document exactly-once delivery semantics in API design section
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Webhook Relay and Transformation Service v1

convergence_state.reason: High severity delivery idempotency issue unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Solid webhook relay architecture with good transformation and retry design, but delivery idempotency under worker restart is insufficiently specified.
strengths: JSONata transformation engine well-specified
Redis sorted set retry scheduling appropriate
HMAC signing secret management correct
concerns: Delivery worker restart could cause duplicate deliveries
No idempotency key or in-flight claim mechanism
blocking_issues: Delivery worker idempotency on restart not addressed
recommendations: Use Redis Lua EVALSHA to atomically claim delivery events before processing with configurable in-flight timeout

previous_plan.title: Webhook Relay and Transformation Service v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Go 1.22 Chi webhook relay with JSONata transformation engine, Redis-queued delivery workers, exponential backoff retry, management REST API on ECS Fargate.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Inbound Chi handler validates HMAC signatures, writes events to PostgreSQL and Redis queue. Delivery worker pool consumes from Redis, applies JSONata rules, dispatches to consumers.
previous_plan.data_model: webhook_endpoints, inbound_events, transformation_rules, delivery_attempts, consumer_registrations, replay_requests in PostgreSQL with Redis sorted set for retry.

target_output.thinking_summary: Addressing delivery worker idempotency from round 1 audit. The fix is a Redis Lua script that atomically moves events from the delivery queue to an in-flight sorted set with a 5-minute TTL score. A reclaim goroutine re-enqueues expired in-flight events for crash recovery. This ensures duplicate delivery only occurs after the 5-minute in-flight window expiry, which is acceptable for webhook relay semantics.

fix_report.action_taken: Added Redis Lua EVALSHA script for atomic event claiming: workers atomically move event IDs from the delivery_queue list to an inflight_set sorted set with score=now+300. A reclaim goroutine scans inflight_set every 30 seconds for expired scores and re-enqueues them to delivery_queue.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Worker restarts no longer cause immediate duplicate delivery. Events claimed by crashed workers are automatically reclaimed after 5 minutes, maintaining delivery guarantees without synchronous duplicate dispatch.

target_output.title: Webhook Relay and Transformation Service v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Chi webhook relay API receiving inbound events from GitHub, Linear, and Sentry, applying configurable JSONata transformation rules, and delivering to registered internal consumer endpoints. Uses Redis Lua atomic claiming to prevent duplicate delivery on worker restart. Exponential backoff retry via Redis sorted sets. Replay API for operator-triggered reprocessing of failed deliveries by time range. Deployed on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL for event and delivery audit persistence.
target_output.architecture_overview: Inbound Chi handler validates HMAC-SHA256 signatures against per-endpoint signing secrets from Secrets Manager, writes inbound_events to PostgreSQL, and enqueues event IDs to Redis delivery_queue list. Delivery workers use a Redis Lua EVALSHA script to atomically claim events by moving them from delivery_queue to inflight_set sorted set with a 5-minute expiry score. The worker fetches the full event payload from PostgreSQL, applies the configured JSONata expression to reshape the payload, and dispatches HTTP POST to the consumer endpoint URL. On 2xx the worker removes the event from inflight_set. A reclaim goroutine scans inflight_set every 30 seconds for events with expired TTL scores and re-enqueues them to delivery_queue for reprocessing after crash.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. Router: Chi v5. JSONata: golang-jsonata library. Database: PostgreSQL 16 via pgx with pgxpool. Queue: Redis 7 with Lua EVALSHA for atomic claiming. Auth: HMAC-SHA256 inbound, API key management middleware. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with 5-minute in-memory cache. Deployment: ECS Fargate separate services for API and workers. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Endpoint registration: POST /v1/endpoints with source enum, consumer URL, signing secret ARN, JSONata rule. Inbound: POST /webhooks/:endpoint_id validates HMAC, stores event, enqueues to Redis. Delivery engine: Lua-atomic claiming workers with configurable concurrency. Retry: exponential backoff with jitter via Redis sorted set delay scores. Replay: POST /v1/endpoints/:id/replay?from=ISO&to=ISO re-enqueues all failed deliveries in window. Delivery log: GET /v1/events/:id/deliveries with attempt history, HTTP status, and latency.
target_output.system_components: Chi inbound handler (HMAC validation, event write, enqueue). JSONata engine (rule application per endpoint). Redis Lua atomic claim script (EVALSHA inflight claim). Delivery worker goroutine pool (10s HTTP timeout per delivery). Reclaim goroutine (30s inflight TTL scan and re-enqueue). Replay service (time-range re-enqueue with current rules). Management REST API (endpoint and rule CRUD). PostgreSQL store (events, delivery attempts). Datadog APM sidecar.
target_output.workflows: Inbound workflow: GitHub POSTs /webhooks/:endpoint_id with X-Hub-Signature-256; Chi fetches signing secret from cache, validates HMAC with constant-time comparison; writes inbound_events row; enqueues event_id to Redis delivery_queue; returns 202 Accepted within 50ms. Delivery workflow: worker calls Lua EVALSHA to atomically LMOVE event_id from delivery_queue to inflight_set with score now+300; fetches event payload from PostgreSQL; applies JSONata rule; POSTs transformed payload to consumer URL with 10s timeout; on 2xx removes from inflight_set and writes delivery_attempts success row; on non-2xx or timeout writes failure row and re-enqueues with exponential backoff delay score. Replay workflow: operator POSTs /v1/endpoints/:id/replay?from=2026-03-10T00:00:00Z&to=2026-03-11T00:00:00Z; service queries delivery_attempts with outcome=failed in window; re-enqueues event IDs to delivery_queue; workers process with current transformation rules.
target_output.data_model: webhook_endpoints (id UUID PK, source ENUM github/linear/sentry, consumer_url, signing_secret_arn, jsonata_rule TEXT, enabled). inbound_events (id UUID PK, endpoint_id FK, source, payload_json JSONB, received_at, dedup_hash). delivery_attempts (id UUID PK, event_id FK, attempt_number, consumer_url, transformed_payload_json JSONB, http_status, response_time_ms, outcome ENUM success/failed/timeout, attempted_at). consumer_registrations (id UUID, endpoint_id FK, consumer_url, max_retries, timeout_ms). replay_requests (id UUID, endpoint_id FK, from_ts, to_ts, triggered_by, event_count). signing_secrets (id UUID, endpoint_id FK, secret_arn, last_rotated_at).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/endpoints (register source, consumer_url, jsonata_rule). GET /v1/endpoints. DELETE /v1/endpoints/:id. POST /v1/endpoints/:id/replay?from=ISO&to=ISO. GET /v1/events/:id/deliveries. POST /webhooks/:endpoint_id (inbound webhook ingestion, 202 response). GET /v1/health. Auth: X-API-Key on management, HMAC-SHA256 on inbound. Rate limit: 10000/min per source IP.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HMAC-SHA256 uses crypto/hmac Equal for constant-time comparison preventing timing attacks. Signing secrets in Secrets Manager accessed via ECS task IAM role with 5-minute in-memory cache. Payload capped at 1MB via Chi middleware. Inbound deduplication via SHA-256 payload hash with 5-minute dedup window. delivery_attempts table append-only per SOC 2 Type II audit trail requirement. Management API keys hashed with bcrypt. TLS 1.3 on ALB.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: go test -race, go vet, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling update per service. ECS: inbound API (1-4 tasks, CPU 60% scale), delivery workers (2-8 tasks, Redis queue depth scale). RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.medium with read replica. golang-migrate in CI deploy step. Secrets Manager 90-day rotation. Environments: dev, staging, production.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces inbound handler and delivery worker with endpoint_id tag. Custom metrics: inbound_events_per_minute by source, delivery_success_rate by endpoint_id, delivery_latency_p99_ms, retry_queue_depth, inflight_events_count. Alerts: delivery_success_rate below 95% for 5 min (high), retry_queue_depth above 1000 (medium), inflight_count not decreasing for 10 min (stuck worker).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate steady state: approximately $150/month. RDS PostgreSQL: $80/month. Redis ElastiCache: $30/month. Total approximately $260/month for 10000 events/min. Scales horizontally by adding delivery worker tasks.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): Chi inbound handler with HMAC validation, PostgreSQL event storage, Redis delivery queue with Lua atomic claim, delivery worker pool with exponential backoff, management API. Phase 2 (3 weeks): JSONata transformation engine, replay API, Datadog APM. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Linear and Sentry source support, dead-letter queue, signing secret rotation workflow.
target_output.development_guidelines: Redis Lua claim script tested independently. JSONata errors caught without failing delivery attempt. HTTP client uses per-request context with 10s timeout. All PostgreSQL queries use prepared statements via pgx. go test -race required in CI. Replay flow checks existing success in delivery_attempts before re-enqueuing.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: JSONata evaluation errors on malformed rules silently dropping deliveries. Mitigation: rule validation on creation via dry-run against sample payload; evaluation errors create delivery_attempt with outcome=rule_error. Tradeoff: at-least-once delivery with Lua claim over exactly-once Kafka consumers for simpler infrastructure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Redis Lua atomic claim chosen over PostgreSQL advisory locks for delivery claiming. JSONata chosen over JQ for better Go library support. Chi over Gin for lighter memory footprint appropriate for high-throughput inbound handler.

metadata.notes: Webhook relay with JSONata transformation; revision fixes delivery worker idempotency via Redis Lua atomic claim.

project.goal: Replace shell scripts with render-validate-apply CLI

security.auth: Okta OIDC device flow

reasoner_reviews.product: The render-validate-apply pipeline mirrors how engineers think about manifest deployment. The --dry-run on render and confirmation prompt on apply are essential safety features.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PyYAML deep-merge with custom resolver is correct for overlay merging. OPA via subprocess avoids embedding a Rego interpreter. System keychain via keyring is the right choice over plaintext token files.
reasoner_reviews.security: Okta OIDC device flow is the standard for CLI authentication. Apply record must include OIDC sub for attribution. System keychain provides OS-level access control on tokens.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PyInstaller binary for macOS and Linux is required. Under 25MB is achievable with careful dependency selection. OPA under 2 seconds is realistic for 500 manifests with compiled policy bundle.

backend.service_design: Standalone Python CLI with Click, no server component
backend.business_modules: ManifestRenderer (PyYAML deep-merge), PolicyEvaluator (OPA subprocess), KubectlAdapter (diff and apply), OktaDeviceAuth (OIDC with keyring), ApplyRecorder (JSON audit files)
backend.failure_handling: OPA subprocess errors captured without crashing CLI; kubectl errors display raw stderr

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC device flow: CLI opens browser to device auth URL, polls token endpoint until engineer approves
security.secrets_management: OIDC tokens in system keychain via keyring, never written to disk as plaintext
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Apply records in ~/.kube/apply-records/{timestamp}-{env}.json with OIDC sub, file sha256 hashes, kubectl exit code

ISS-001.title: No authentication for cluster API calls
ISS-001.detail: Apply command calls kubectl with no engineer authentication. Apply records cannot include attributable identity for SOC 2 change evidence.

history.note: Identified in round 1 security audit

focus_issues.title: No authentication for cluster API calls

previous_plan.title: Kubernetes Manifest Management CLI v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python 3.12 Click CLI with PyYAML deep-merge render, OPA subprocess validate, kubectl diff apply. No authentication mechanism specified.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Click CLI with ManifestRenderer, PolicyEvaluator, KubectlAdapter modules. Local filesystem for templates and overlays.

target_output.thinking_summary: Addressing the authentication gap from round 1 by integrating Okta OIDC device flow before apply operations, with OIDC sub captured in apply records for SOC 2 change attribution. The CLI architecture is otherwise solid; this revision focuses on security and apply workflow.

fix_report.action_taken: Added OktaDeviceAuth module using requests-oauthlib OIDC device flow. Apply command checks for valid token in system keychain via keyring before proceeding; if absent or expired, initiates device flow. OIDC sub extracted from JWT and written to apply_record.json.
fix_report.expected_outcome: All apply operations preceded by Okta authentication. Apply records include OIDC sub satisfying SOC 2 change management attribution requirements.

target_output.title: Kubernetes Manifest Management CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 CLI replacing shell scripts for multi-environment Kubernetes manifest management. Engineers render environment-specific YAML from base templates with environment overlays, validate against OPA policies, and apply to clusters after Okta OIDC authentication with explicit confirmation. Apply records capture OIDC sub for SOC 2 change evidence. Distributed as a PyInstaller binary via the internal package registry.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python CLI with Click subcommands: render, validate, apply, auth, config. Render deep-merges templates/*.yaml with envs/{env}/overrides.yaml using PyYAML custom merge resolver. Validate calls OPA binary via subprocess with compiled policy bundle, parsing JSON violations. Apply verifies Okta OIDC token in system keychain (initiates device flow if expired), runs kubectl diff for Rich colored delta display, requires typed confirmation, calls kubectl apply --server-side, writes apply_record.json with OIDC sub to ~/.kube/apply-records/.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12. Click 8.x command parsing. PyYAML 6.x with custom deep-merge resolver. OPA binary via subprocess with compiled policy bundle tar. Rich for colored terminal diff display. Okta OIDC device flow via requests-oauthlib. System keychain via keyring. PyInstaller for self-contained binary distribution. GitLab package registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: render: deep-merges base and env overlays, writes to staging dir, --dry-run prints without writing. validate: OPA policy bundle evaluation reporting required_labels, resource_limits, security_context, network_policy violations. apply: OIDC token check, kubectl diff display, typed confirmation, kubectl apply --server-side, apply_record.json write. auth: login initiates device flow, logout clears keychain. config: initializes cluster context and policy bundle path.
target_output.system_components: Click entrypoint. ManifestRenderer (PyYAML deep-merge). PolicyEvaluator (OPA subprocess with policy bundle). KubectlAdapter (kubectl diff and apply). OktaDeviceAuth (requests-oauthlib device flow with keyring). ApplyRecorder (JSON audit files). PolicyBundle (required_labels.rego, resource_limits.rego, security_context.rego, network_policy.rego compiled to tar).
target_output.workflows: Render: 'manifest-cli render --env staging --output ./dist/staging' deep-merges templates and envs/staging/overrides.yaml, writes output with render_metadata.json sidecar. Validate: 'manifest-cli validate --path ./dist/staging' invokes OPA eval with policy bundle, prints Rich violations table, exits 1 on required_labels or resource_limits violations. Apply: 'manifest-cli apply --env staging' checks OIDC token via keyring, initiates device flow if expired, extracts OIDC sub from JWT, runs kubectl diff, displays Rich additions/modifications/deletions table, prompts typed confirmation, calls kubectl apply --server-side, writes ~/.kube/apply-records/{timestamp}-staging.json with timestamp, env, oidc_sub, file sha256 hashes, kubectl exit code.
target_output.data_model: manifest_templates (YAML files in templates/ by resource kind). environment_overrides (YAML patches in envs/{env}/overrides.yaml). policy_rules (OPA .rego files in policies/ compiled to policies.tar.gz). render_outputs (staged YAMLs with render_metadata.json containing file checksums). diff_reports (kubectl diff stdout for Rich rendering). apply_records (~/.kube/apply-records/{timestamp}-{env}.json: timestamp, env, oidc_sub, applied_files sha256, kubectl_exit_code).
target_output.api_design: CLI: 'manifest-cli render --env ENV [--output DIR] [--dry-run]'. 'manifest-cli validate --path DIR [--fail-on SEVERITY]'. 'manifest-cli apply --env ENV [--skip-diff]'. 'manifest-cli auth login/logout'. 'manifest-cli config init'. All support --output-format json for CI. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=violations, 2=apply rejected, 3=auth error, 4=kubectl error.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC device flow authenticates engineer before every apply. Tokens in system keychain via keyring OS access controls, never in dotfiles or environment variables. OIDC sub in apply_record.json provides SOC 2 Type II change management attribution. OPA security_context policy enforces run_as_non_root=true, capabilities.drop ALL, readOnlyRootFilesystem=true. Production apply blocks --yes flag requiring interactive confirmation. Apply records retained 12 months.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI on version tag: pytest 85% branch coverage, PyInstaller build for macOS ARM64/x86_64 and Linux x86_64, upload to internal GitLab Package Registry. Install via pip with internal index URL. Version pinned via .tool-versions per infra repo. CI usage: validate command as GitLab pipeline step with --output-format json and --fail-on required_labels.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON logs to ~/.manifest-cli/logs/ with 10MB rotation. Apply: logs diff line count, confirmation, kubectl exit code, OIDC token expiry. Validate: logs OPA eval duration and violation count per policy category. Exit codes machine-readable for GitLab CI metrics.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost. Binary under 25MB. OPA evaluation under 2s for 500 resources. Render under 5s for 200 manifests. GitLab Package Registry included in plan.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (3 weeks): render with PyYAML deep-merge, validate with OPA for required_labels and resource_limits, apply with kubectl diff and confirmation, Click structure, pytest unit tests. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Okta OIDC device flow, system keychain token storage, apply records with OIDC sub, PyInstaller binary packaging. Phase 3 (2 weeks): security_context and network_policy OPA rules, internal package registry publishing, CI integration docs.
target_output.development_guidelines: Type hints required on all public functions. Click test runner for CLI integration tests with fixture manifests in tests/fixtures/. OPA policies tested with 'opa test' in CI. pytest-mock for OktaDeviceAuth and KubectlAdapter. 85% branch coverage gate. OPA rule additions require corresponding fixture test case.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Okta outage blocking cluster operations during incidents. Mitigation: cached OIDC token valid 1 hour; emergency break-glass via direct kubeconfig with incident ticket. Risk: OPA binary version drift between workstations and CI. Mitigation: vendor OPA binary at pinned version in PyInstaller build. Tradeoff: PyInstaller binary simplifies distribution at cost of larger binary and slower dependency updates.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Click chosen over Typer for team familiarity and subprocess integration. OPA subprocess over embedded Rego for policy bundle ecosystem compatibility. System keychain via keyring over ~/.kube/tokens for OS-level access control.

metadata.notes: K8s manifest CLI; revision adds Okta OIDC device flow for SOC 2 change attribution.

profile.domain: Automated Cloud Security Posture Enforcement for AWS and GCP

project_goal.value: Continuously scan AWS and GCP resources against CIS benchmark rules, auto-remediate low-risk misconfigurations, escalate high-severity findings via Slack and PagerDuty without auto-remediation
project_goal.rationale: Core security automation requirement

target_users.value: Security engineering team and cloud operations engineers
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Backend automation with no UI

capabilities.rationale: Celery workers, cloud SDK integrations, scheduled scanning

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-cloud parallel worker architecture with remediation engine

risk_level.rationale: Auto-remediation requires elevated IAM permissions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Findings contain internal cloud security posture data

external_exposure.rationale: Internal security tooling not exposed outside VPC

access_model.value: Cognito JWT for management API, IAM roles for cloud SDK access
access_model.rationale: Service-to-service auth pattern

feature_scope.value: Multi-account AWS and GCP scanning, CIS rules, auto-remediation for low severity, Slack and PagerDuty escalation, findings database, management API
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set

mvp_scope.value: AWS scanning for 5 CIS rules, findings persistence, Slack escalation, account config API
mvp_scope.rationale: Security team pilot

security_baseline.value: Separate IAM roles for scan and remediation, Secrets Manager credentials, append-only remediation audit log
security_baseline.rationale: Minimum controls for auto-remediation tooling

frontend_stack.rationale: No UI; management via REST API

backend_stack.rationale: Celery for async parallel account scanning

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for findings and audit data, Redis 7 as Celery broker
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for queries, Redis for task queue

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with worker auto-scaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: Findings 1 year, remediation audit logs 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 and internal policy retention requirements

future_scope.value: Custom rule authoring, findings trend dashboard, Jira ticket creation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2

constraints.value: Scan 20 accounts in 30 minutes, no auto-remediation for high or critical severity
constraints.rationale: Operational and safety requirements

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with Celery task metrics, PagerDuty for operational alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate standard

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints
execution_preference.rationale: Team preference

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II, CIS Benchmarks Level 1 AWS, internal cloud security policy with audit trail for all remediation
compliance_context.rationale: Compliance requirements

project.goal: Automate CIS enforcement across AWS and GCP

security.auth: Cognito JWT management, IAM AssumeRole cloud SDKs

reasoner_reviews.product: The auto-remediation safety boundary (low/medium only, never high/critical) must be enforced architecturally at two layers: rule configuration and worker code. Slack and PagerDuty escalation is essential for security team adoption.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery worker types for scan, remediation, and escalation allow independent scaling. The remediation worker must fetch before_state before any cloud API call for audit reconstruction.
reasoner_reviews.security: IAM role separation between read-only scanning and write-permission remediation is critical. Remediation audit log must be append-only at database ACL level, not just application level.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 30 minutes for 20 accounts requires 2 worker tasks minimum per account. Monthly PostgreSQL partitioning on findings.detected_at is needed for query performance at scale.

backend.service_design: Celery scan_worker, remediation_worker, escalation_worker plus FastAPI management API
backend.background_jobs: Celery Beat per-account hourly cron, remediation polling open low/medium findings, escalation via LISTEN/NOTIFY
backend.failure_handling: Celery retry on transient SDK errors, circuit breaker for cloud account access issues

security.auth_design: Separate IAM roles: CloudSecurityScanRole (read-only) and CloudSecurityRemediateRole (specific write permissions) via STS AssumeRole
security.secrets_management: Cloud credentials in Secrets Manager, accessed via ECS task IAM role, 30-day rotation
security.audit_and_logging_controls: remediation_actions append-only at database ACL; before_state and after_state captured per action

data.entities: scan_targets, benchmark_rules, findings, remediation_actions, escalation_records, cloud_account_configs
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with monthly partitioning on findings.detected_at, Redis 7 for Celery

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: FastAPI (1-3), scan workers (2-10 HPA queue depth), remediation (1-3), escalation (1-2)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest 80% coverage, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with findings_detected_total, auto_remediation_success_rate, scan_duration_seconds

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing cloud security posture enforcement with strict auto-remediation safety. Critical design: double enforcement of no-auto-remediation-for-high/critical at rule configuration (auto_remediate_eligible) and worker code (severity allowlist). PostgreSQL append-only ACL on remediation_actions provides tamper-evident audit trail for SOC 2.
target_output.title: Cloud Security Posture Enforcement Tool
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Celery-based automation tool scanning AWS and GCP resources against CIS benchmark rules, auto-remediating low-severity misconfigurations with before/after state captured in an append-only audit log, and escalating high/critical findings via Slack and PagerDuty. Auto-remediation is architecturally blocked for high and critical severity findings. FastAPI management API for account and rule configuration. ECS Fargate deployment with PostgreSQL and Redis.
target_output.architecture_overview: Celery Beat schedules hourly scan tasks per registered cloud account. Scan workers assume IAM roles or use GCP service accounts, enumerate resources via boto3 or google-cloud SDKs, evaluate each against active CIS benchmark rules from PostgreSQL, and write findings. Remediation workers poll for low/medium auto-remediable findings, capture before_state via SDK describe calls, execute remediation API calls, and write append-only remediation_actions rows with before and after state. Escalation workers receive high/critical finding notifications via PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY and post to Slack and PagerDuty. FastAPI management API handles account registration, rule configuration, findings queries, and compliance reports. The auto-remediation safety boundary is enforced at both the benchmark_rules.auto_remediate_eligible column and in remediation worker code via severity allowlist.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12. Celery 5.x with Redis 7 broker. boto3 for AWS. google-cloud-asset and google-cloud-iam for GCP. PostgreSQL 16 via SQLAlchemy with Alembic migrations. FastAPI with Cognito JWT middleware. Slack Bolt SDK. PagerDuty Events API v2. Datadog APM. AWS Secrets Manager. ECS Fargate.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Account registration: AWS with IAM role ARN or GCP with service account credentials in Secrets Manager. Rule management: enable/disable CIS rules, toggle auto_remediate_eligible. Scan scheduling: Celery Beat hourly per account. Findings lifecycle: open/auto_remediated/escalated/manually_resolved. Auto-remediation: S3 public access block, IAM access key deletion (inactive 90+ days), security group 0.0.0.0/0 revocation on ports 22 and 3389. Escalation: Slack posting and PagerDuty incident creation for high/critical findings with CIS rule ID and resource ARN.
target_output.system_components: Celery Beat scheduler. Scan workers (2-10 ECS tasks, queue depth auto-scaling). PostgreSQL findings store (scan_targets, benchmark_rules, findings, remediation_actions, escalation_records). Remediation workers (1-3 ECS tasks). Escalation workers (1-2 ECS tasks). FastAPI management API (1-3 ECS tasks). Redis 7 broker and result store.
target_output.workflows: Scan: Celery Beat fires scan_account task hourly; worker calls STS AssumeRole for AWS or loads GCP service account; enumerates S3 buckets, IAM users, security groups; evaluates each against active rules; writes findings rows. Remediation: worker queries findings where status=open AND auto_remediate_eligible=true AND severity IN ('low','medium'); captures before_state via SDK describe; executes remediation call; writes remediation_actions with before/after state; marks finding auto_remediated; code explicitly checks severity and skips high/critical regardless of rule config. Escalation: LISTEN/NOTIFY fires on high/critical finding INSERT; worker posts Slack message to security-alerts with CIS rule ID, resource ARN, account ID; creates PagerDuty incident with dedup_key=finding_id; writes escalation_record.
target_output.data_model: scan_targets (id UUID, cloud_provider ENUM aws/gcp, account_id, role_arn, enabled, last_scan_at). benchmark_rules (id, rule_id e.g. CIS-1.4, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, auto_remediate_eligible BOOLEAN, cloud_provider). findings (id UUID PK, scan_target_id FK, rule_id FK, resource_arn, severity, status ENUM open/auto_remediated/escalated/manually_resolved, first_detected_at, resource_metadata_json JSONB). remediation_actions (id UUID, finding_id FK, before_state_json JSONB, after_state_json JSONB, executed_at, executed_by ENUM auto/human, success BOOLEAN). escalation_records (id UUID, finding_id FK, pagerduty_incident_id, escalated_at). cloud_account_configs (id, scan_target_id FK, credentials_secret_arn).
target_output.api_design: POST /accounts (register cloud account). GET /accounts/:id/findings?status=open&severity=high. GET /rules. PATCH /rules/:rule_id (toggle enabled, auto_remediate_eligible). POST /accounts/:id/scans (manual trigger). GET /findings/:id/history. GET /reports/summary?period=7d. POST /exceptions (resource ARN opt-out). All require Cognito Bearer JWT.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Separate IAM roles for scan (read-only) and remediation (specific write permissions) with aws:ResourceAccount condition scoping. Remediation credentials in separate Secrets Manager secret inaccessible to scan workers. PostgreSQL remediation_actions role has INSERT only, no UPDATE or DELETE, enforcing append-only at database ACL layer. Double safety enforcement: auto_remediate_eligible=false for high/critical rules AND worker code severity allowlist check. SOC 2 Type II immutable audit trail for all scan and remediation activity. Cognito MFA on management API.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: pytest 80% coverage, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. ECS services: FastAPI (1-3), scan workers (2-10 CloudWatch queue depth auto-scale), remediation (1-3), escalation (1-2). Alembic migration as ECS one-off in CI deploy. Secrets Manager 30-day rotation. Monthly PostgreSQL partitions via maintenance job. Rollback: redeploy previous ECR image.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces all Celery tasks with scan_target_id and rule_id tags. Metrics: findings_detected_total by severity/account, auto_remediation_success_rate, escalation_rate, scan_duration_seconds. Alerts: scan failure >5% in 10 min (PagerDuty operational), remediation failures >3 in 5 min (Slack warning).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate steady state 20 accounts: approximately $200/month. RDS db.t3.medium: $80. Redis cache.t3.micro: $20. Total ~$350/month. Scales to 100 accounts by adding scan worker capacity.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): AWS scanning for 5 CIS rules (S3 public block, IAM unused keys, security group open ingress, root MFA, CloudTrail), findings persistence, Slack escalation, FastAPI account config API. Phase 2 (3 weeks): GCP scanning, full CIS AWS Level 1, auto-remediation with append-only audit log, PagerDuty integration. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Datadog APM, compliance report API, resource exception management, ECS auto-scaling, SOC 2 audit export.
target_output.development_guidelines: Remediation functions capture before_state before any cloud mutation. Worker code has explicit severity allowlist check independent of rule configuration. Rule evaluation functions are pure and tested with mocked boto3 responses. pytest-mock for all SDK calls. Integration tests against LocalStack for AWS.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: remediation IAM role compromise broader blast radius than scan role. Mitigation: separate Secrets Manager secrets per role, CloudTrail alerting on anomalous remediation API calls. Risk: false positive auto-remediation on intentionally permissive resources. Mitigation: resource ARN exception list per account for opt-out. Tradeoff: Celery over Step Functions for simpler local dev and GCP portability.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Celery chosen over Step Functions to support both AWS and GCP without cloud coupling. PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for rich cross-account findings queries. Redis for both Celery broker and result backend.

metadata.notes: Cloud security posture enforcement; double safety enforcement for auto-remediation boundary.

project.goal: Auto-generate changelog from GitHub releases

reasoner_reviews.product: Release-event-triggered builds are the right approach. The conventional commit parser for category filtering adds significant value over flat release notes. RSS feed is required for developer adoption.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro Content Collections with Zod schema validation is the correct approach for type-safe release data. Pagefind post-build indexer is the right choice for static search without external service.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers in _headers file blocking external scripts prevent XSS risks. Cloudflare Web Analytics is cookieless eliminating GDPR cookie consent complexity.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 90-second build target is achievable with incremental GitHub API fetching caching release JSON from previous builds.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented complete TypeScript release fetcher using octokit/rest that calls GitHub REST API on release published event, parses conventional commit prefixes, writes typed Astro content collection entries, and caches release JSON to reduce API calls on rebuilds.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Changelog entries published automatically within 90 seconds of GitHub release publish event, no manual frontmatter creation required.

fix_report.expected_outcome: Standards-compliant RSS feed available at /feed.xml for developer tool integration and feed reader subscriptions.

target_output.title: Auto-Generated Changelog and Release Notes Site
target_output.executive_summary: A static changelog and release notes site built with Astro 4.x and TypeScript, deployed to Cloudflare Pages. A GitHub Actions workflow triggered on release publish events fetches release data via the GitHub REST API using octokit, parses conventional commit prefixes into changelog categories, builds the Astro site, generates a Pagefind search index, and deploys within 90 seconds. RSS feed generated at build time via @astrojs/rss. No server-side runtime required.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static site with zero server-side runtime. GitHub Actions workflow fires on release published event. A TypeScript fetch script using octokit/rest calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases and GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits?sha={tag}, parses conventional commit prefixes (feat, fix, perf, docs, BREAKING_CHANGE) into changelog_categories, and writes Astro content collection JSON files to src/content/releases/. Astro builds static HTML for all pages from the content collection. @astrojs/rss generates /feed.xml. Pagefind post-build step indexes HTML for client-side search. cloudflare/pages-action deploys to Cloudflare Pages edge.
target_output.technology_stack: Astro 4.x with TypeScript. Tailwind CSS 3.x. @astrojs/rss for feed generation. octokit/rest for GitHub REST API calls in CI build scripts. Zod for content collection schema validation. Pagefind for static search index generation. Cloudflare Pages for edge delivery. GitHub Actions with release event trigger. Cloudflare Web Analytics cookieless pixel.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Release index page listing all releases in reverse chronological order with version tag, date, and commit count. Per-release detail pages at /changelog/{version} with conventional commit breakdown by category. Category filter tabs (Features, Bug Fixes, Performance, Breaking Changes) using client-side JavaScript. Version browse navigation with previous/next links. RSS feed at /feed.xml with all releases. Pagefind full-text search widget. Contributor credit section per release from commit author fields.
target_output.system_components: GitHub Actions release workflow. TypeScript octokit release fetcher (API calls, conventional commit parsing, content collection JSON generation). Astro Content Collections with Zod schemas for release_entries and changelog_categories. @astrojs/rss generator. Pagefind post-build indexer. Cloudflare Pages edge delivery with _headers CSP config.
target_output.workflows: Release publish workflow: maintainer publishes GitHub release; GitHub webhook triggers Actions workflow on release published event; TypeScript fetcher calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases (paginated with cache) and GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits?sha={tag}&per_page=100; parser categorizes commits by conventional prefix; Astro content collection entries written to src/content/releases/{version}.json; Astro builds all static HTML; Pagefind indexes generated HTML; cloudflare/pages-action deploys to edge within 90 seconds. RSS subscription: visitor navigates to /feed.xml; Cloudflare Pages serves pre-built Atom feed with all releases as items; feed reader polls and notifies subscriber of new releases.
target_output.data_model: release_entries (Astro content collection: version_tag, published_at, release_title, commit_count). changelog_categories (derived: category ENUM features/bug_fixes/performance/breaking_changes/other, entries[] with commit message). version_tags (ordered list for navigation). rss_feed_items (release_entries projected: guid=tag URL, title=release_title, pubDate=published_at). contributor_mentions (distinct author usernames per release). search_index (Pagefind-generated JSON: content fragments, URLs, titles keyed by version and category for client-side full-text search). All data shapes validated at build time via TypeScript strict mode on the fetcher script.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. GitHub REST API at build time: GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases, GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits?sha={tag}&per_page=100, authenticated with GITHUB_TOKEN read-only secret. Cloudflare Pages deploy via cloudflare/pages-action with CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN secret. No runtime API endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: No user data collected. Cloudflare Web Analytics cookieless. GITHUB_TOKEN read-only, expires with job. HTTPS via Cloudflare automatic TLS. CSP in _headers: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' (Pagefind only). No forms, cookies, or auth. Pagefind runs client-side from static index files.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: on release published - checkout, install Bun, run TypeScript fetcher with GITHUB_TOKEN, bun run build (Astro), bun run pagefind, cloudflare/pages-action deploy. Build under 90 seconds. Preview deployments on PRs. Custom domain via Cloudflare DNS. Cache headers in _headers: assets max-age 31536000 immutable, HTML max-age 300. Branch protection requires CI pass before merge. Build durations tracked in GitHub Actions step summary to alert if fetcher latency grows with repository history.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics for page views and top release pages. GitHub Actions run history for build monitoring. Build duration as Actions metric. Slack notification on build failure via on-failure step. Pagefind index size tracked as build artifact.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier: 500 builds/month, unlimited bandwidth. GitHub Actions free tier. Total $0/month. Scales to millions of visitors. Large release history under 3-minute build with incremental fetching.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (2 weeks): Actions release trigger, octokit fetcher, Astro release index and detail pages, Cloudflare Pages deploy, category filter tabs. Phase 2 (1 week): @astrojs/rss feed, Pagefind search, all conventional commit prefixes, contributor mentions. Phase 3 (1 week): custom domain, CSP _headers, Cloudflare Web Analytics, PR preview deployments.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for all build scripts. Release JSON cached in build artifact to reduce API calls. Conventional commit parser tested with fixture message lists. Zod schema validates content collection at build time. No JS bundles over 10KB on index page; Pagefind loaded async.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GitHub API rate limits on full rebuild. Mitigation: cache release JSON and fetch only incremental releases. Tradeoff: static generation over server-rendered API eliminates hosting cost at cost of 60-90 second publish delay. Tradeoff: Pagefind over Algolia eliminates external dependency at cost of larger initial JS load for search.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Astro over Eleventy for TypeScript Content Collections with Zod validation. @astrojs/rss over custom template for standards compliance. Pagefind over Fuse.js for performance on large release histories.

metadata.notes: Changelog static site; revision completes GitHub API automation and RSS feed generation.

project.goal: Kong API gateway as code with drift detection

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ Kong backing store

reasoner_reviews.product: The config-as-code constraint eliminates operational drift but requires a fast emergency apply path for rate limit incidents. The drift detection 15-minute window is reasonable for detecting unauthorized changes.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Kong DB-mode is required for the kong Terraform provider to manage configuration via Admin API. Terraform Cloud workspace isolation for staging and production prevents environment contamination. OPA Conftest policies ensure all services have required plugins.
reasoner_reviews.security: Admin API must never be exposed outside VPC. JWT RS256 signing key rotation must be automated via Secrets Manager Lambda rotation function. Terraform Cloud provides immutable run history for SOC 2 change attribution.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Drift detection Lambda using EventBridge every 15 minutes is straightforward. The Lua script in Terraform provider calls is the standard approach. Emergency apply must have incident ticket reference in commit message for audit trail.

backend.service_design: No application backend; Terraform manages Kong gateway configuration
backend.api_patterns: Kong Proxy API routes defined by Terraform kong_service and kong_route resources

security.auth_design: JWT RS256 plugin on all routes serving external traffic; Consumer credentials via kong_consumer_jwt_auth; Admin API security group VPC-only
security.secrets_management: JWT signing keys in Secrets Manager 90-day rotation; Kong DB password 30-day rotation
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Terraform Cloud immutable run history; OPA Conftest CI policy checks enforcing required plugins on all services

data.entities: gateway_services, route_configs, rate_limit_policies, plugin_configurations, upstream_targets, consumer_credentials, drift_reports
data.storage_design: Terraform state in Terraform Cloud; Kong config in PostgreSQL RDS; drift_reports in DynamoDB

devops.deployment_topology: Kong ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks behind ALB; RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ; Redis ElastiCache for rate limiting
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: terraform fmt, validate, plan -json, OPA Conftest, PR comment, apply on main merge for staging; manual workflow_dispatch for production
devops.observability_stack: Datadog Kong Prometheus exporter sidecar: kong_http_requests_total, kong_latency_ms_bucket, kong_bandwidth_bytes

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing Kong API gateway IaC with Terraform. Key decisions: DB-mode Kong for provider compatibility, OPA Conftest in CI enforcing required plugin presence on every service, Go Lambda for drift detection comparing live Admin API state against Terraform state, Terraform Cloud workspaces per environment for state isolation and audit trail.
target_output.title: Kong API Gateway Infrastructure as Code
target_output.executive_summary: A Terraform 1.8 infrastructure project deploying Kong 3.x on ECS Fargate and managing all gateway configuration declaratively using the kong Terraform provider. JWT auth offload, rate limiting, and microservice routing defined as code. GitHub Actions CI enforces OPA Conftest policies before any apply. A Go Lambda running every 15 minutes detects unauthorized Kong Admin API changes and alerts via Slack and GitHub Issues. Terraform Cloud manages state with per-environment workspaces.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Terraform configuration layers: infrastructure (ECS Fargate Kong tasks, RDS PostgreSQL backing store, ALB, security groups, VPC) and gateway configuration (kong provider resources for services, routes, plugins, consumers). Kong runs in DB-mode backed by PostgreSQL 16, enabling the kong Terraform provider to manage configuration via the Admin API. GitHub Actions CI runs OPA Conftest policies enforcing that every service has rate_limit and jwt plugins before any apply. A Go Lambda scheduled by EventBridge every 15 minutes calls the Kong Admin API, compares response against current Terraform state from S3, and raises Slack alerts and GitHub Issues for unauthorized configuration drift.
target_output.technology_stack: Terraform 1.8. hashicorp/aws provider 5.x. kong/kong provider 9.x. Kong 3.x DB-mode on ECS Fargate. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Redis 7 ElastiCache for rate limiting. GitHub Actions CI. Terraform Cloud state backend. OPA Conftest 0.45 for Terraform plan policy checks. Go 1.22 drift detection Lambda. Datadog with Kong Prometheus exporter sidecar. AWS Secrets Manager.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Service registry: kong_service resources for each upstream microservice. Route management: kong_route with path patterns and method matching. Rate limiting: kong_plugin rate-limit-advanced with Redis counter storage for accuracy across Kong instances. JWT auth offload: kong_plugin jwt with RS256 public key verification. Consumer credentials: kong_consumer and kong_consumer_jwt_auth per API client. Drift detection: Lambda alerting on unauthorized Admin API changes via Slack and GitHub Issues.
target_output.system_components: Kong ECS service (2-4 Fargate tasks behind ALB). Kong Admin API internal ALB (VPC-only). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. Redis 7 ElastiCache. GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Terraform Cloud (state, run history). Drift detection Lambda (Go, EventBridge 15-min schedule, DynamoDB drift_reports). Datadog Prometheus sidecar per Kong task.
target_output.workflows: Service onboarding: platform engineer adds kong_service, kong_route, and plugin Terraform resources in PR; CI runs OPA Conftest checking for required rate_limit and jwt plugins; reviewer approves; merge triggers automatic staging apply; separate PR with manual workflow_dispatch for production with 2 reviewer approvals. Drift detection: Lambda calls GET /services, /routes, /plugins on Kong Admin API every 15 minutes; compares against Terraform state JSON from S3; detects resources present in Admin API but absent from state; posts Slack alert to #platform-alerts and creates GitHub Issue with resource diff. Emergency rate limit update: SRE modifies plugin config in Terraform, creates PR tagged incident, on-call approves, apply runs with incident ticket in commit message logged to Terraform Cloud.
target_output.data_model: gateway_services (Terraform state: kong_service id, name, url, timeouts). route_configs (Terraform state: kong_route id, service_id, paths[], methods[]). rate_limit_policies (plugin config JSON: minute/hour limits, policy ENUM local/redis/cluster). plugin_configurations (Terraform state: id, service_id or route_id, plugin name, config JSONB). consumer_credentials (kong_consumer_jwt_auth: key, RS256 algorithm, rsa_public_key from Secrets Manager). drift_reports (DynamoDB: resource_type, resource_id, detected_at, delta_json, resolved_at).
target_output.api_design: No external API created. Kong Proxy serves downstream traffic via Terraform-defined routes (e.g., GET /api/v1/orders/* proxied to orders-service.internal:8080). JWT plugin validates Bearer RS256 tokens before proxying. Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Remaining-Minute, X-RateLimit-Limit-Minute. Kong Admin API (internal only): consumed by Terraform provider during plan/apply and drift detector Lambda.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Kong Admin API accessible only within VPC via security group explicit ingress. JWT RS256 keys in Secrets Manager with 90-day automated rotation via Lambda function. Kong DB password on 30-day rotation. SOC 2 Type II: Terraform Cloud immutable run history with IAM user attribution. OPA Conftest: every kong_service must have rate_limit plugin, every external route must have jwt plugin. Production applies require 2 reviewer approvals via branch protection.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: terraform fmt -check, validate, plan -json, OPA Conftest, post plan as PR comment. Staging: automatic apply on main merge. Production: manual workflow_dispatch with 2 approvals. Kong version upgrade: pin image tag, test in staging with kong migrations up one-off ECS task before service update. ECS rolling update minimum 100% healthy. SSL verify=on for Kong to RDS connection with CA from Secrets Manager.
target_output.observability: Datadog receives Kong proxy metrics from Prometheus sidecar: kong_http_requests_total by service and status class, kong_latency_ms_bucket P50/P95/P99, kong_bandwidth_bytes. Alerts: upstream 5xx >2% for 5 min (high), P99 >500ms (medium), rate limit hits >200/min single consumer (info). Drift Lambda publishes drift_incidents_detected CloudWatch metric.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Kong ECS Fargate 2 tasks: ~$80/month. RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ: ~$150/month. Redis cache.t3.micro: ~$20/month. Drift Lambda: <$1/month. Total ~$260/month. Scales to 8 Kong tasks for high traffic. Redis required for accurate rate limiting across instances.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (3 weeks): Terraform Kong ECS Fargate, RDS, ALB, rate_limit and jwt plugins for 3 pilot microservices, GitHub Actions plan/apply with Terraform Cloud. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Drift detection Lambda, Slack/GitHub Issues alerting, OPA Conftest policies, Terraform Cloud workspace isolation for staging/production. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Datadog Prometheus integration, consumer credential management, emergency apply pipeline, full microservices migration to Kong-proxied traffic.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Kong config via Terraform exclusively; Admin API and konga UI changes prohibited. Terraform modules for patterns: authenticated_service (service+route+jwt+rate_limit), public_service (service+route+rate_limit). All modules validated with OPA Conftest in CI. Drift Lambda tested with recorded Admin API fixtures. No manual Admin API changes in any environment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: kong Terraform provider version lag behind Kong API changes. Mitigation: pin to exact patch version, test upgrades in feature branch. Risk: Kong DB migration failure during upgrade. Mitigation: test in staging with production data snapshot. Tradeoff: DB-mode over DB-less for provider compatibility. Tradeoff: Terraform Cloud over self-hosted state for built-in audit trail.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: DB-mode Kong chosen for kong Terraform provider compatibility. OPA Conftest over Sentinel for open-source portability. Drift detection as Lambda rather than in-cluster CronJob to detect unauthorized changes from any source.

metadata.notes: Kong gateway IaC with Terraform; drift detection Lambda and OPA Conftest policy enforcement.

target_output.title: Secrets Management SaaS Compliance Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 App Router marketing landing page on Vercel Edge for a developer secrets management SaaS. Features SOC 2 Type II compliance badge, GDPR data residency section, trust center with lead-gated compliance document downloads via HubSpot CRM integration, and a filterable integration showcase from a static JSON manifest. Cookieless Vercel Analytics. No server-side database; leads in HubSpot, compliance PDFs in S3.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router generates all marketing content as static HTML at build time from MDX content files and a static integrations JSON manifest. Two Vercel serverless functions handle lead capture: POST /api/trust-center/download validates form, posts to HubSpot, returns S3 pre-signed URL; POST /api/leads/subscribe handles newsletter CTA. Trust center documents stored in a private S3 bucket with no public access. Integration showcase filter uses client-side React state over JSON data pre-loaded at page render. Vercel Edge Network serves all pages globally with automatic HTTPS.
target_output.technology_stack: Next.js 14 App Router with TypeScript. Tailwind CSS 3.x. MDX for trust center and badge content. Static JSON for 40+ integration listings. Vercel Functions for lead capture and pre-signed URL generation. Zod for form validation. HubSpot Forms API v3 for CRM. AWS SDK v3 S3 for pre-signed URLs. Vercel Analytics cookieless pixel. GitHub Actions for CI type check and lint.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero section with trial CTA. SOC 2 Type II badge with certification date and auditor. Trust center section with download cards for SOC 2 report, penetration test summary, GDPR DPA, each requiring lead form completion. GDPR data residency section with EU/US region comparison table. Integration showcase with filterable grid of 40+ developer tools from static JSON. Social proof with enterprise logos and compliance testimonials. Newsletter signup CTA.
target_output.system_components: Next.js App Router static pages. MDX content layer for badge copy and trust center descriptions. Static integrations.json (40+ entries with category, logo, docs URL). Vercel Function /api/trust-center/download (Zod validation, HubSpot Forms API, S3 getSignedUrl). Vercel Function /api/leads/subscribe (newsletter HubSpot submission). Private S3 bucket for compliance PDFs. React lead capture modal component. Vercel Analytics script.
target_output.workflows: Download workflow: visitor clicks Download SOC 2 Report; React modal renders lead form; visitor submits first_name, last_name, company, work_email; client calls POST /api/trust-center/download; Vercel Function validates with Zod, posts to HubSpot Forms API with source property trust_center_soc2, calls S3 getSignedUrl with 15-minute expiry, returns JSON with download_url; browser downloads PDF via pre-signed URL. Integration filter: all integrations JSON pre-loaded into React state; visitor types search or clicks category chip; React filters displayed cards client-side; no API calls.

All forms validated server-side via Next.js Route Handler before HubSpot API submission.

POST /api/leads/subscribe (body: email; HubSpot newsletter). HubSpot Forms API: POST https://api.hsforms.com/submissions/v3/integration/submit/{portalId}/{formGuid}. AWS SDK v3 S3 getSignedUrl with 15-min expiry. No public GET endpoints; all content static.

S3 compliance documents private bucket; downloads only via 15-min pre-signed URLs. HTTPS via Vercel auto-TLS. CSP headers in next.config.js: script-src self and Vercel Analytics domain only. No cookies; Vercel Analytics cookieless. HubSpot API credentials in Vercel environment variables, never in client bundle.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: TypeScript check, ESLint, Next.js build verify. Vercel auto-deploys: preview on PR, production on main merge. Environment variables in Vercel settings: HUBSPOT_PORTAL_ID, HUBSPOT_FORM_GUID_TRUST, HUBSPOT_FORM_GUID_NEWSLETTER, AWS_S3_BUCKET, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY. S3 key scoped to s3:GetObject on compliance-documents/* only. Custom domain via Cloudflare DNS CNAME. Vercel Edge Config stores feature flags for beta trust center document visibility. Cloudflare WAF rate-limits the HubSpot proxy Route Handler to 20 requests/minute per IP to prevent form spam on high-intent compliance download flows.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics for page views and top sections. Vercel Function logs capture form submission outcomes. Vercel Analytics custom events: start_trial_hero, view_trust_center, download_document with document_type. HubSpot CRM for trust center conversion funnel and download volume. Vercel Slack notifications for production build failures.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro $20/month. S3 PDFs under $1/month. HubSpot free tier. Total $20-25/month. Scales to millions of page views on Vercel Edge. Serverless functions auto-scale for download spikes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (2 weeks): Static pages with hero, compliance badges, trust center placeholders, integration showcase from JSON. Phase 2 (2 weeks): Lead capture modal, HubSpot integration, S3 pre-signed URL generation, integration category filter. Phase 3 (1 week): Vercel Analytics events, GDPR consent checkbox, mobile responsive polish, Open Graph meta tags.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. All Vercel Functions use Zod for request body validation before any processing. No database access. Integration JSON updated via PR for versioned governance. Compliance document S3 keys in typed constants module. cva for Tailwind variant-based component styling.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: HubSpot API outage blocks trust center downloads during sales cycles. Mitigation: Function falls back to returning S3 URL directly if HubSpot returns non-2xx, logging missed lead for manual CRM entry. Tradeoff: static JSON integration manifest over headless CMS keeps content in Git at cost of PR required for new integrations. Tradeoff: Vercel Functions over dedicated API for minimal infrastructure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: HubSpot over Salesforce for simpler Forms API. S3 pre-signed URLs over CloudFront signed URLs for simpler setup. Vercel Analytics over GA4 to eliminate cookie consent banner for compliance-focused audience.

ISS-001.title: ArgoCD Applications created imperatively without selfHeal
ISS-001.detail: Current ArgoCD Applications are created via kubectl apply and do not have auto-sync or selfHeal enabled, meaning manual cluster changes persist and accumulate as drift.

history.note: ApplicationSets added but selfHeal still disabled

ISS-002.title: Argo Rollouts canary analysis has no sample count threshold
ISS-002.detail: AnalysisTemplate Prometheus queries may return no data during off-peak hours, causing false canary aborts on insufficient samples.

ISS-003.title: ArgoCD Application configs not in GitOps monorepo
ISS-003.detail: ArgoCD application configurations were managed imperatively; now stored in argocd/ directory in monorepo.

focus_issues.title: ApplicationSets missing selfHeal=true and prune=true
focus_issues.detail: Must enable automated.selfHeal and automated.prune in all ApplicationSet templates.

focus_issues.title: Canary analysis missing sample count threshold
focus_issues.detail: Add count >= 100 success condition to AnalysisTemplate to prevent false aborts on low traffic.

target_output.thinking_summary: Resolving the two remaining issues: enabling selfHeal=true and prune=true in all ApplicationSet templates to auto-revert manual cluster changes, and adding count >= 100 minimum sample threshold to AnalysisTemplate to prevent false canary aborts during low-traffic windows. These are targeted changes to the ApplicationSet manifests and AnalysisTemplate resources.

fix_report.action_taken: Enabled automated.selfHeal=true and automated.prune=true in all ApplicationSet Application templates in argocd/appsets/. ArgoCD now reverts any manual kubectl changes within the 3-minute sync interval and prunes resources not in the GitOps repo.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Manual cluster changes automatically reverted within 3 minutes. Configuration drift no longer accumulates between nightly remediation runs.

fix_report.action_taken: Added count >= 100 minimum sample constraint to all AnalysisTemplate Prometheus success conditions. Analysis now requires at least 100 total requests in the 10-minute window before evaluating success/failure criteria, preventing false aborts during off-peak low-traffic deployments.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Canary promotions proceed correctly during off-peak hours. False aborts due to insufficient Prometheus samples eliminated.

target_output.title: GitOps Kubernetes Migration with ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts
target_output.executive_summary: A GitOps infrastructure migration converting a legacy manually-managed GKE cluster to a fully declarative ArgoCD-driven model. ApplicationSets with selfHeal=true and prune=true auto-revert unauthorized manual changes within 3 minutes. Argo Rollouts canary deployments with Prometheus AnalysisTemplates requiring 100+ sample count before evaluation. GitOps monorepo with Helm 3 charts plus Kustomize overlays. Sealed Secrets for encrypted Git storage. Okta OIDC for ArgoCD SSO with group-based RBAC. ArgoCD self-manages its own configurations via app-of-apps.
target_output.architecture_overview: GitOps monorepo with three directories: apps/ (Helm charts per service with base values.yaml), envs/ (Kustomize overlays per environment), argocd/ (ApplicationSet and Application manifests including ArgoCD self-management via app-of-apps root Application). ArgoCD on GKE Autopilot watches the monorepo, renders Helm+Kustomize manifests, and syncs to target namespaces with auto-sync automated.selfHeal=true and automated.prune=true ensuring any out-of-tree resources are removed. ApplicationSet Git directory generator auto-discovers service charts. Argo Rollouts manages canary deployments via Istio VirtualService weight adjustment with Prometheus AnalysisTemplate gates requiring count >= 100 samples before success/failure evaluation.
target_output.technology_stack: ArgoCD 2.10 with ApplicationSet controller. Helm 3.14 with Kustomize 5.x. Argo Rollouts 1.7. Istio 1.21 for traffic splitting. GKE Autopilot. GitHub GitOps monorepo. Okta OIDC for ArgoCD SSO. GitHub Actions for PR validation. Grafana 10 with Prometheus. Sealed Secrets with GCP KMS backup.
target_output.functional_feature_map: ApplicationSet patterns: Git directory generator auto-discovers service Helm charts and creates Application objects with auto-sync, selfHeal=true, prune=true. Canary deployments: Argo Rollouts canary strategy (10%/30%/50%/100%) with AnalysisTemplate gates requiring 100+ samples. Sealed Secrets: kubeseal encryption for all in-cluster secrets. Drift prevention: selfHeal reverts manual kubectl changes within 3 minutes. RBAC: Okta group to ArgoCD role mapping (developers=read, platform=sync, SRE=admin). Nightly CronJob for remediation audit logging.
target_output.system_components: ArgoCD application controller and API server on GKE Autopilot. ApplicationSet controller. Argo Rollouts controller and analysis runner. Istio control plane for traffic weight management. Sealed Secrets controller. GitHub Actions CI (helm lint, kustomize build, kubeval, argocd app diff). Nightly drift audit CronJob. Grafana and Prometheus. Okta OIDC application.
target_output.workflows: Engineer merge: engineer merges Helm chart change; Actions CI runs helm lint --strict, kustomize build, kubeval; ArgoCD detects commit within 3 minutes; ApplicationSet renders manifests; auto-sync with selfHeal=true applies changes and removes any out-of-tree resources. Production canary: engineer updates envs/prod image tag; ArgoCD syncs Rollout; Argo Rollouts shifts 10% Istio weight; AnalysisRun queries Prometheus for success_rate and P99 latency requiring count >= 100 requests over 10-minute window; if metrics pass and count met, promotes to 30%/50%/100%; if metrics fail or insufficient samples at timeout, aborts and rolls back. Drift prevention: any direct kubectl apply to production is automatically reverted by ArgoCD selfHeal within 3 minutes; nightly CronJob runs argocd app list for OutOfSync apps and logs count to audit file.
target_output.data_model: application_sets (ArgoCD ApplicationSet manifests in argocd/appsets/ with Git directory generator). sync_policies (Application spec: automated.prune=true, automated.selfHeal=true, syncOptions=[CreateNamespace=true]). rollout_strategies (Argo Rollouts Rollout spec: canary steps[], analysis.templates[] with count >= 100 success condition). environment_overlays (Kustomize kustomization.yaml per env with image tag patch). progressive_delivery_configs (AnalysisTemplate with Prometheus queries and count >= 100 constraint). gitops_repositories (ArgoCD repository with GitHub App credentials).
target_output.api_design: No external API. ArgoCD API server (internal): GitHub Actions argocd app diff for plan preview, nightly CronJob argocd app list and sync. GitHub push webhook triggers immediate ArgoCD sync. Prometheus API queried by Argo Rollouts AnalysisRun for success rate and P99 latency with count constraint. Okta OIDC for ArgoCD SSO with groups claim for RBAC.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Sealed Secrets encrypts all secrets before Git commit using cluster public key; private key backed up to GCP KMS. ArgoCD RBAC: developers read-only, platform sync, SRE admin. Okta MFA enforced via OIDC. SOC 2 Type II: every ArgoCD sync logged with OIDC user, application, Git SHA, outcome. selfHeal=true reverts unauthorized kubectl changes within 3 minutes. Namespace RBAC via ArgoCD project restrictions. Admission webhook blocks direct kubectl apply to production namespaces.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bootstrap: Terraform installs ArgoCD on GKE Autopilot; root app-of-apps Application points to argocd/ directory; ArgoCD self-manages all subsequent ApplicationSet changes. PR validation: GitHub Actions helm lint --strict, kustomize build, kubeval, argocd app diff --local as PR comment. Production promotion: merge envs/prod image tag PR; ArgoCD auto-syncs within 3 minutes; Argo Rollouts canary starts. Rollback: git revert in production overlay; ArgoCD syncs previous image within 3 minutes.
target_output.observability: Grafana: ArgoCD sync status heatmap by service and environment, Argo Rollouts canary promotion history with success/abort outcomes, nightly drift audit OutOfSync count trending. Alerts: application OutOfSync >15 minutes despite selfHeal (critical - potential admission webhook failure), Rollout abort in production (high), canary analysis insufficient samples repeated 3+ times for same service (medium). ArgoCD UI shows real-time resource diffs.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ArgoCD and Argo Rollouts on GKE Autopilot: ~$50-80/month. Grafana and Prometheus: ~$100/month. ApplicationSet directory generator scales to 200+ services without manual Application creation. Canary temporarily doubles pod count during analysis window (~10-15% compute increase per rollout).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (4 weeks): ArgoCD bootstrap via Terraform, app-of-apps for self-management, 5 pilot services with Helm+Kustomize, ApplicationSet with selfHeal=true and prune=true, Okta OIDC SSO. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Argo Rollouts canary with AnalysisTemplate (count >= 100 threshold), Sealed Secrets for all secrets, GitHub Actions PR validation pipeline. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Full service migration, nightly drift audit CronJob, Grafana dashboards, GitOps runbooks for team onboarding.
target_output.development_guidelines: All cluster changes via monorepo PR; direct kubectl to production blocked by admission webhook. Image tag is the only frequently-changed field in production Kustomize overlays. Sealed Secrets regenerated via kubeseal when adding new secrets; no plaintext values in Git. argocd app diff for plan preview on all production overlay PRs. ApplicationSet selfHeal=true ensures all Applications inherit auto-revert behavior.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GitOps monorepo merge conflicts when multiple teams update same service overlay. Mitigation: CODEOWNERS restricting production overlay changes to service owners. Risk: AnalysisTemplate count threshold may delay canary on low-traffic services even during peak hours. Mitigation: per-service AnalysisTemplate override with appropriate count threshold based on service traffic volume. Tradeoff: Helm+Kustomize over pure Kustomize for better base chart value reuse. Tradeoff: ArgoCD over Flux for ApplicationSet and Okta SSO maturity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ArgoCD over Flux for ApplicationSet generator and Okta OIDC SSO maturity. Istio over Nginx Ingress for canary traffic splitting precision. Sealed Secrets over External Secrets Operator for simpler bootstrapping without external secret store dependency.

metadata.notes: GitOps K8s migration; revision 3 enables selfHeal in ApplicationSets and adds sample count threshold to canary AnalysisTemplates.

profile.domain: FedRAMP Privileged Access Management Mobile App for DevOps Engineers

mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for FedRAMP pilot

security_baseline.rationale: FedRAMP Moderate baseline controls

privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FedRAMP retention requirement

future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap

constraints.rationale: FedRAMP authorization boundary constraints

observability_baseline.rationale: FedRAMP AU-12 audit record generation requirement
