execution_preference.rationale: Team preference

compliance_context.value: FedRAMP Moderate, SOC 2 Type II, NIST 800-53. All privileged access must be attributable to authenticated engineer with business justification. Session recordings required for compliance officer review.
compliance_context.rationale: Federal compliance requirements

project.goal: FedRAMP-compliant mobile PAM tool for privileged production access

security.auth: Okta OIDC mandatory MFA, step-up MFA for privileged actions

reasoner_reviews.product: The step-up MFA requirement for privileged actions with business justification text is the critical UX design challenge. The flow must be fast enough that engineers use it rather than bypassing via other means.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SHA-256 hash chaining provides tamper evidence at low cost. The prev_row_hash must be fetched and the new row written in the same database transaction to prevent race conditions. Daily hash chain checkpoints enable efficient incremental verification for compliance reports.
reasoner_reviews.security: PostgreSQL audit_log_entries must use an application role with INSERT only on that table, no UPDATE or DELETE. S3 session recordings with Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode prevents deletion even by account administrators. KMS asymmetric RSA-4096 signing for compliance reports provides long-term signature validity.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: AWS GovCloud us-gov-west-1 has slightly higher latency than commercial regions. ECS Fargate Multi-AZ across 2 GovCloud availability zones provides HA within the boundary. Step-up MFA 5-minute expiry is per NIST 800-53 guidance on authenticator session management.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with Okta SDK, session initiator, dashboard viewer, privileged action screens, session recorder, compliance viewer
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login -> MFA -> Dashboard -> Service Detail | Privileged Action (justification + step-up MFA) | Compliance Audit Log Viewer
frontend.state_management: React Query for dashboard data, local state for session recording buffer

backend.service_design: Go Gin backend: session API, dashboard proxy, privileged action executor, audit log writer, compliance report generator
backend.failure_handling: Step-up token validation rejects expired tokens; hash chain failures halt audit writes rather than producing invalid chains

security.auth_design: Okta OIDC initial MFA at session start; Okta step-up MFA push for each privileged action with 5-minute token expiry
security.secrets_management: No long-lived credentials; ECS task IAM role for all AWS API calls; KMS key for session recording encryption and compliance report signing
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_log_entries INSERT-only role; hash chaining via SHA-256(prev_hash || session_id || timestamp || user || action); daily checkpoint table for efficient verification

data.entities: access_sessions, privileged_actions, audit_log_entries, mfa_challenges, session_recordings, compliance_reports, user_attestations
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL RDS GovCloud for sessions and audit log; S3 GovCloud Object Lock for recordings and reports

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate GovCloud Multi-AZ (2 AZs, 2-6 tasks); RDS Multi-AZ; S3 Object Lock 3-year retention
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Go test -race, React Native TypeScript check, Docker build, GovCloud ECR push, ECS rolling deploy
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch custom metrics: active_sessions, privileged_actions_per_hour, step_up_mfa_rejection_rate; CloudTrail all GovCloud API calls

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a FedRAMP Moderate mobile PAM app with strict compliance controls. Key architectural decisions: SHA-256 hash chaining in PostgreSQL for tamper-evident audit log (prev_row_hash fetched and new row written in same transaction), S3 GovCloud Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode for immutable session recordings, KMS RSA-4096 asymmetric signing for compliance reports, step-up MFA 5-minute expiry enforced server-side.
target_output.title: FedRAMP Privileged Access Management Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: A React Native mobile application for DevOps engineers in FedRAMP Moderate and SOC 2 environments providing Okta MFA-enforced access to production dashboards with mandatory session recording, step-up MFA for privileged actions with business justification, and a tamper-evident SHA-256 hash-chained audit log. Go 1.22 backend on AWS GovCloud ECS Fargate. PostgreSQL 16 with append-only audit log. S3 GovCloud with Object Lock for session recordings. KMS-signed monthly compliance reports for FedRAMP continuous monitoring submission.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native app connects via HTTPS to a Go Gin backend on GovCloud ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF. Okta OIDC initiates session with mandatory MFA push; session creates audit_log_entries row with device fingerprint hash, IP, GeoIP location, Okta sub, and SHA-256 hash chaining to previous row. React Native captures session interaction events and uploads in 30-second chunks to S3 GovCloud bucket with SSE-KMS. Privileged actions require Okta step-up MFA push with 5-minute token expiry and business justification text. A Go ECS scheduled task generates monthly compliance reports querying the audit log and signing with KMS RSA-4096 asymmetric key for FedRAMP submission.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.73 TypeScript with Okta React Native SDK. Backend: Go 1.22 Gin. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS GovCloud Multi-AZ. Session recordings: S3 GovCloud SSE-KMS Object Lock COMPLIANCE 3-year retention. Auth: Okta OIDC with TOTP and push MFA. Report signing: AWS KMS RSA-4096 asymmetric key. CI/CD: GitHub Actions GovCloud ECR. Monitoring: CloudWatch custom metrics and CloudTrail. Distribution: Apple Enterprise MDM and Google Play Private Distribution.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Session initiation: Okta MFA push authentication, device fingerprint registration, tamper-evident audit log entry with hash chain. Dashboard viewer: read-only Kubernetes pod health, service status, deployment history, recent error rates via backend proxy. Privileged actions: restart service, rollback deployment, scale replicas, each requiring justification text and step-up Okta MFA push before execution. Session recorder: React Native event capture uploaded to S3 in 30-second chunks. Compliance report generator: monthly KMS-signed access reports for FedRAMP submission. Audit log viewer: compliance officer screen filtering by engineer, action type, time range.
target_output.system_components: React Native app with Okta SDK and session recorder. Go Gin backend API (session management, dashboard proxy, privileged action execution, audit log writer). PostgreSQL audit_log_entries (append-only role, hash-chained rows). S3 GovCloud bucket (session_recordings/ Object Lock, compliance_reports/ Object Lock). Go KMS-signing compliance report ECS scheduled task. AWS CloudTrail all GovCloud API calls. WAF on ALB (rate limiting, US-only geographic restriction). Okta tenant with mandatory MFA and step-up policies.
target_output.workflows: Session initiation: engineer opens app, initiates Okta OIDC with MFA push; on approval, backend creates access_sessions record and audit_log_entries row with hash=SHA256(prev_hash || session_id || timestamp || okta_sub || 'session_start'); S3 recording key allocated. Privileged action: engineer selects restart-service for payments-api; app displays justification form; engineer submits; app calls POST /auth/mfa/challenge for Okta step-up push; engineer approves push; backend validates step-up token (rejects if >5 minutes old); executes kubectl rollout restart; writes privileged_actions record with justification_text and step_up_token_id; writes hash-chained audit_log_entries row. Compliance report: monthly ECS task queries audit_log_entries for period; generates structured report; calls KMS Sign with RSA-4096 key; writes report and signature to S3 Object Lock compliance_reports/.
target_output.data_model: access_sessions (id UUID PK, okta_sub, device_fingerprint_hash, ip_address, geolocation_json JSONB, session_start_at, recording_s3_key, mfa_method ENUM totp/push). privileged_actions (id UUID PK, session_id FK, action_type ENUM restart_service/rollback_deployment/scale_replicas, target_resource, justification_text, step_up_token_id, executed_at, outcome ENUM success/failed/rejected). audit_log_entries (id BIGSERIAL PK, session_id FK, okta_sub, action, resource_type, timestamp, row_hash BYTEA, prev_row_hash BYTEA NOT NULL). mfa_challenges (id UUID, challenge_type ENUM initial/step_up, okta_transaction_id, issued_at, completed_at, outcome ENUM approved/rejected/expired). compliance_reports (id UUID, reporting_period, report_s3_key, signature_s3_key, kms_key_id, generated_at). user_attestations (id UUID, audit_entry_id FK, attestation_text, okta_sub).
target_output.api_design: POST /auth/session (Okta OIDC initiation, returns session_token). POST /auth/mfa/challenge (step-up push request, returns challenge_id). POST /auth/mfa/verify (step-up completion, returns step_up_token 5-min expiry). GET /dashboard/services (pod health list from cluster proxy). GET /dashboard/services/:id/status (pod status, deployments, error rate). POST /actions/restart (body: service_id, justification, step_up_token). POST /actions/rollback (body: service_id, deployment_id, justification, step_up_token). POST /recordings/upload (chunked 30-second event stream). GET /audit-log (compliance officer paginated query). GET /compliance-reports/:period (S3 signed URL). All require Okta JWT Bearer auth.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FedRAMP Moderate boundary: all data in AWS GovCloud us-gov-west-1. NIST 800-53: AC-17 remote access with MFA, AU-2/AU-12 audit event generation, AU-9 tamper protection via SHA-256 hash chaining, IA-2 MFA enforcement, IA-5 authenticator management via Okta. Hash chain integrity: prev_row_hash fetched and new row written in same PostgreSQL transaction. App role: INSERT only on audit_log_entries; no UPDATE or DELETE. S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE prevents deletion even by account admin. Step-up MFA tokens validated server-side against 5-minute expiry. WAF: US-only geographic restriction, rate limiting 100 req/10s per IP. KMS annual key rotation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: Go test -race, React Native TypeScript check, Docker build, GovCloud ECR push, ECS rolling deploy minimum healthy 100%. Mobile: iOS Apple Enterprise MDM, Android Google Play Private. ECS Multi-AZ 2-6 tasks. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ GovCloud 35-day backup retention (NIST AU-11). S3 Object Lock COMPLIANCE 3-year retention. CloudTrail all regions with log file validation. KMS asymmetric key annual rotation.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics: active_sessions_count, privileged_actions_per_hour by action_type, step_up_mfa_rejection_rate, audit_log_write_latency_p99. Alarms: step-up MFA rejection >10% in 1 hour (PagerDuty - potential brute force), audit log write errors any count (PagerDuty), recording upload failures >3 in 1 hour (Slack). CloudTrail Lake for FedRAMP evidence queries. CloudWatch dashboard for compliance officers: daily session counts by engineer, privileged action breakdown.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2 tasks GovCloud: ~$200/month. RDS db.t3.medium Multi-AZ GovCloud: ~$250/month. S3 recordings 100GB/month: ~$50/month. KMS: ~$5/month. Total ~$550/month. S3 Intelligent-Tiering reduces recording cost after 30 days. 3-year FedRAMP retention drives long-term storage cost.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): Okta OIDC auth, hash-chained audit log, read-only dashboard viewer, React Native iOS and Android builds, Go Gin backend on GovCloud ECS. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Privileged actions with step-up MFA and justification, session recording upload to S3 Object Lock, append-only PostgreSQL role enforcement. Phase 3 (4 weeks): KMS-signed compliance report generator, compliance officer audit viewer, NIST 800-53 control implementation statements for FedRAMP package.
target_output.development_guidelines: All audit_log_entries writes fetch prev_row_hash and insert new row in same database transaction. Step-up tokens validated server-side against issued_at plus 5 minutes; never trust client-provided expiry. Session recording events buffered 30 seconds client-side before upload with retry on network failure. All GovCloud API calls use ECS task IAM role; no long-lived access keys. Hash chain verification unit tested with fixture audit log sequences including tamper detection.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: hash chain verification expensive for large date ranges in compliance reports. Mitigation: daily hash_chain_checkpoints table storing verified chain state at end of each day for O(1) incremental verification. Risk: session recording upload gaps due to mobile network issues. Mitigation: client-side retry buffer with 24-hour retry window; compliance officer notified of gaps. Tradeoff: React Native over native iOS/Android for single codebase at slight recording capture performance cost. Tradeoff: ECS Fargate over Lambda for long-running compliance report generation without timeout constraints.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: GovCloud us-gov-west-1 over us-gov-east-1 for broader service availability. PostgreSQL hash chaining over AWS QLDB for lower cost and simpler SQL querying. KMS RSA-4096 asymmetric signing for compliance reports for long-term validity appropriate for multi-year FedRAMP archives.

metadata.notes: FedRAMP Moderate mobile PAM app; SHA-256 hash-chained audit log, S3 Object Lock session recordings, KMS-signed compliance reports.

profile.domain: Jenkins-to-GitHub Actions Pipeline Migration CLI for Platform Engineering Teams

project_goal.value: CLI tool that parses Jenkins declarative pipeline Jenkinsfiles and generates equivalent GitHub Actions workflow YAML, with batch conversion mode for multi-repository inventories and manual-review markers for unsupported constructs
project_goal.rationale: Platform engineering team needs to migrate 300+ Jenkinsfile-based pipelines to GitHub Actions without manual rewrite of each file

target_users.value: Platform engineers and DevOps teams performing CI/CD migration from Jenkins to GitHub Actions
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified as the platform engineering team running the migration project

project_class.rationale: Local command-line execution only; no hosted service or browser UI required

capabilities.rationale: CLI logic, local file I/O, and optional GitHub API integration for repository inventory fetch

complexity_level.rationale: Groovy DSL parsing plus YAML generation with parallel batch mode requires careful design but no distributed infrastructure

risk_level.rationale: Local-only tool; no production data access; read-only Jenkinsfile processing with no auto-apply

data_sensitivity.rationale: Processes internal Jenkinsfile source code; no customer PII or financial data involved

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on local workstation or CI runner; no inbound network exposure

access_model.value: No authentication required for conversion; GitHub token via GITHUB_TOKEN env var or OS keychain for optional inventory fetch
access_model.rationale: CLI does not expose services; token scoped read-only to GitHub API for repo listing

feature_scope.value: Single-file convert subcommand, batch subcommand with repository_inventories CSV/JSON, report subcommand for migration summary, fetch-inventory subcommand via GitHub API, unsupported construct markers in output YAML
feature_scope.rationale: Complete migration toolchain covering single-repo and multi-repo scenarios

mvp_scope.value: convert and batch subcommands covering declarative pipeline agents, stages, and 40 common step types; migration_report in JSON and Markdown formats
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to unblock the 300-repo migration project

security_baseline.value: GitHub token stored in OS keychain via keyring; never written to migration reports or logs; Jenkinsfile content not logged at any verbosity level
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents accidental credential or source code exfiltration via log files

frontend_stack.rationale: CLI-only tool with terminal stdout/stderr; no browser UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click 8.x and lark-parser for Groovy DSL grammar
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem maturity for DSL parsing; team familiarity; lark handles Groovy declarative grammar well

data_platform.value: Local filesystem only; no server database; migration_reports and conversion_outputs written to disk
data_platform.rationale: Offline local processing; no persistent server state required

hosting_target.value: PyInstaller binary distributed via GitHub Releases for linux-amd64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64
hosting_target.rationale: Self-contained binary requires no Python installation on engineer workstations

privacy_retention_policy.value: migration_reports retained locally by user; no server-side retention; tool does not transmit any data externally
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Local-only tool has no server retention obligations

future_scope.value: Web dashboard for migration progress visualization, GitLab CI YAML output format, scripted pipeline syntax support
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap items pending platform team feedback after MVP migration

constraints.value: Binary under 25MB; batch conversion of 500 Jenkinsfiles under 5 minutes on 8-core workstation; no network access during conversion (only fetch-inventory uses GitHub API)
constraints.rationale: CI runner disk and time constraints; air-gap compatibility for conversion

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON progress output to stdout per file; --verbose flag for AST debug traces; rich progress bar in batch mode; migration_report JSON with per-repo conversion statistics
observability_baseline.rationale: CLI observability is operator-visible stdout rather than remote telemetry

execution_preference.value: 3-week iterative development with weekly demo to platform engineering team
execution_preference.rationale: Fast iteration to validate parser accuracy against real Jenkinsfile corpus

project.goal: Automate Jenkinsfile-to-GitHub-Actions YAML conversion for 300+ internal repositories

backend.parser: lark-parser with Groovy declarative DSL grammar

data.formats: CSV/JSON for repository_inventories input; YAML for conversion_outputs; JSON/Markdown for migration_reports

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for test and release

reasoner_reviews.product: Batch conversion mode is the core enterprise value of this tool: migrating 300+ repositories manually is infeasible. The batch subcommand with repository_inventories input directly unblocks the migration project. Prioritizing its completion in round 2 is correct. The migration_report JSON format must be machine-readable to enable automated triage of MIGRATOR-TODO markers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Adding multiprocessing.Pool to the batch subcommand is architecturally sound for CPU-bound Groovy parsing. The worker count should default to os.cpu_count() with a --workers override. The jenkinsfile_definitions AST schema should be explicitly defined in the data model to prevent mapper contract drift as new step types are added.
reasoner_reviews.security: GitHub token must remain isolated to the fetch-inventory subcommand path; the convert and batch paths must never access the network. Token sourced from GITHUB_TOKEN env var with OS keychain fallback via keyring is the right pattern for CLI tools.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Worker pool parallelism is the correct approach for the 500-file-in-5-minute target. PyInstaller binary size under 25MB is achievable if lark-parser and ruamel.yaml are the only heavy dependencies; numpy or pandas must be avoided.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 plan lacked batch mode entirely despite it being a core use case. The round 2 fix must be visible in the architecture_overview command table, the data_model AST schema definition, and the workflows section batch processing flow.

backend.service_design: Standalone Python 3.12 CLI with Click subcommands: convert (single Jenkinsfile), batch (repository_inventories file), report (aggregate migration_reports), fetch-inventory (GitHub API repo list), config (set defaults). No server component.
backend.business_modules: jenkinsfile_parser.py (lark grammar producing jenkinsfile_definitions AST), gha_mapper.py (AST-to-GitHub-Actions YAML with construct_support_matrix.json), reporter.py (JSON and Markdown migration_report generation), inventory.py (GitHub API client for repository_inventories), batch_runner.py (multiprocessing.Pool worker pool for parallel conversion)
backend.failure_handling: Unsupported step types produce MIGRATOR-TODO YAML comments rather than crashing; scripted pipeline detection raises ParseError with clear message; worker pool failures in batch mode are captured per-repo and included in migration_report error counts rather than aborting the entire batch

security.secrets_management: GITHUB_TOKEN read from environment variable first, then OS keychain via keyring; never written to migration_reports, log files, or conversion_outputs YAML. Jenkinsfile content processed in-memory only; no content emitted to logs at any verbosity level.
security.abuse_prevention: fetch-inventory subcommand validates GitHub API token scope (must have repo:read) before listing repositories; rate limit headers respected with exponential backoff

data.entities: jenkinsfile_definitions (in-memory AST), conversion_outputs (generated YAML on disk), migration_reports (JSON/Markdown aggregates), repository_inventories (CSV/JSON input), construct_support_matrix (JSON config for step type mapping)
data.schema_notes: jenkinsfile_definitions AST: {file_path: str, pipeline_type: 'declarative', agent: {type: str, label: str}, stages: [{name: str, steps: [{type: str, args: dict, supported: bool, plugin: str|null}]}], environment: {key: value}, post: {always: [...], failure: [...]}}. migration_reports: {repo: str, total_stages: int, converted_stages: int, flagged_steps: int, unsupported_constructs: [str], duration_ms: int, status: 'success'|'partial'|'failed'}
data.storage_design: Local filesystem: base_dir/templates/ for Jenkinsfile inputs, base_dir/output/{repo}/.github/workflows/ for conversion_outputs, base_dir/reports/ for migration_reports

devops.deployment_topology: Local CLI on engineer workstations and GitHub Actions CI runners; no server deployment
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: pytest with 80% coverage gate, PyInstaller matrix build for linux-amd64/darwin-amd64/darwin-arm64/windows-amd64, SHA-256 checksum generation, attach binaries to GitHub Release on semver tag

target_output.title: Jenkins-to-GHA Migrator CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 CLI tool that converts Jenkins declarative pipeline Jenkinsfiles to GitHub Actions workflow YAML. Version 1.1 adds the batch subcommand that reads repository_inventories CSV/JSON files, dispatches Jenkinsfile conversion to a multiprocessing.Pool worker pool, and aggregates per-repo conversion statistics into machine-readable migration_reports. Single-file convert, batch, report, and fetch-inventory subcommands cover the full 300-repo migration lifecycle.
target_output.architecture_overview: Click 8.x CLI exposes four subcommands: convert (single Jenkinsfile path to YAML), batch (repository_inventories file to parallel conversion), report (aggregate migration_reports to Markdown or JSON summary), and fetch-inventory (GitHub API repo list to repository_inventories CSV). The convert path runs jenkinsfile_parser.py against the input file to produce a jenkinsfile_definitions AST, feeds it to gha_mapper.py with construct_support_matrix.json, and writes conversion_outputs YAML with MIGRATOR-TODO comments for unsupported steps. The batch path reads repository_inventories, validates the repo column, and submits each Jenkinsfile path to a multiprocessing.Pool with default workers=os.cpu_count(); each worker runs the same convert pipeline and returns a migration_report dict. batch_runner.py collects results, writes per-repo YAML to output/{repo}/.github/workflows/, and writes the aggregate migration_report JSON.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12. Click 8.x for subcommand routing and option parsing. lark-parser 1.x with custom Groovy declarative pipeline EBNF grammar. ruamel.yaml for round-trip YAML output preserving comments. rich for progress bar in batch mode. keyring for OS-native GitHub token storage. PyInstaller 6.x for cross-platform self-contained binaries. GitHub Actions for CI and release publishing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: convert: parse single Jenkinsfile via lark grammar, map pipeline agent/stages/steps to GitHub Actions jobs/steps, emit conversion_outputs YAML with MIGRATOR-TODO comments for unsupported step types, --dry-run prints without writing. batch: read repository_inventories CSV or JSON, submit each repo to multiprocessing.Pool, write per-repo conversion_outputs, collect migration_report dicts. report: read migration_report JSON files from output dir, aggregate totals (converted_stages, flagged_steps, failed_repos), render Markdown table or JSON summary. fetch-inventory: authenticate via GITHUB_TOKEN, list org repos via GitHub REST API with pagination, write repository_inventories CSV.
target_output.system_components: jenkinsfile_parser.py with lark EBNF grammar for Groovy declarative pipeline syntax (agent, stages, steps, environment, post blocks). gha_mapper.py consuming jenkinsfile_definitions AST and construct_support_matrix.json to emit GitHub Actions YAML. batch_runner.py with multiprocessing.Pool dispatching convert pipeline per repo. reporter.py aggregating migration_report dicts to JSON/Markdown. inventory.py using requests for GitHub REST API repo listing. Click CLI entrypoint wiring subcommands to modules.
target_output.workflows: Single-file conversion: engineer runs 'migrator convert --input ./Jenkinsfile --output .github/workflows/ --format yaml'; jenkinsfile_parser reads declarative pipeline blocks and produces jenkinsfile_definitions AST; gha_mapper translates agent definitions to runs-on labels, stage blocks to jobs, step types to GitHub Actions steps via construct_support_matrix.json; unsupported steps are emitted as '# MIGRATOR-TODO: manual review required for <step_type>' YAML comments; output YAML is written to --output directory. Batch inventory conversion: engineer runs 'migrator batch --inventory repos.csv --output ./output --workers 8 --format yaml'; batch_runner reads repos.csv (columns: repo, jenkinsfile_path), validates file existence, submits each entry to Pool(workers=8); each worker runs the convert pipeline independently; results are collected into migration_reports list; batch_runner writes per-repo YAML under output/{repo}/.github/workflows/ and writes output/migration_report.json with aggregate statistics including flagged_steps, unsupported_constructs per repo, and total duration.
target_output.data_model: jenkinsfile_definitions AST (in-memory, not persisted): {file_path: str, pipeline_type: 'declarative'|'scripted', agent: {type: 'any'|'label'|'docker', label: str|null, image: str|null}, stages: [{name: str, steps: [{type: str, args: dict, supported: bool, plugin: str|null}], parallel: bool}], environment: {KEY: VALUE}, post: {always: [step], success: [step], failure: [step]}}. conversion_outputs (YAML files on disk): standard GitHub Actions workflow YAML with MIGRATOR-TODO comment blocks. migration_reports (JSON on disk): {repo: str, jenkinsfile_path: str, total_stages: int, converted_stages: int, flagged_steps: int, unsupported_constructs: [str], duration_ms: int, status: 'success'|'partial'|'failed', error: str|null}. repository_inventories (CSV/JSON input): {repo: str, jenkinsfile_path: str, branch: str|null}. construct_support_matrix.json: {step_type: {supported: bool, gha_equivalent: str|null, notes: str}}.
target_output.api_design: CLI command surface: 'migrator convert --input PATH --output DIR [--format yaml] [--dry-run] [--verbose]'. 'migrator batch --inventory FILE --output DIR [--workers N] [--format yaml] [--report-format json|markdown]'. 'migrator report --input DIR --format markdown|json --output FILE'. 'migrator fetch-inventory --org ORG --output FILE [--format csv|json] [--token TOKEN]'. All subcommands exit 0 on success, exit 1 on hard failure (unreadable input, fatal parse error), exit 2 on partial batch failure (some repos failed but others succeeded).
target_output.security_and_compliance: GitHub token is sourced from GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable first; if absent, queried from OS keychain via keyring library; if neither is present, fetch-inventory subcommand fails with clear error. Token is never written to migration_reports, log output, or conversion_outputs YAML at any verbosity level. Jenkinsfile source content is processed entirely in-memory; no content is logged even at --verbose level to prevent accidental exfiltration of internal service names or secrets embedded in Jenkinsfile environment blocks. PyInstaller binary SHA-256 checksums are published alongside GitHub Release assets for supply chain integrity verification.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions release workflow triggers on semver tags (e.g., v1.1.0). Matrix strategy builds PyInstaller binaries for linux-amd64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, and windows-amd64 in parallel jobs. Each job runs 'pyinstaller --onefile --name migrator cli.py' in the target OS runner. SHA-256 checksums computed via 'sha256sum' or 'certutil' per platform. Binaries and checksum files attached to GitHub Release via 'gh release upload'. Homebrew tap formula updated on release. CI runs on every PR: 'pytest --cov=migrator --cov-fail-under=80', lark grammar unit tests against 40 Jenkinsfile fixtures, PyInstaller smoke test for binary size under 25MB.
target_output.observability: Batch mode emits structured JSON progress to stdout per completed repo: {repo: str, status: 'success'|'partial'|'failed', converted: int, flagged: int, duration_ms: int}. rich progress bar shows completed/total count and ETA during batch runs. --verbose flag enables debug-level AST token stream traces for parser diagnostics. Final migration_report.json includes total_repos, success_count, partial_count, failed_count, total_duration_ms, and per-repo breakdown.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost; runs locally or on GitHub Actions free tier. Batch conversion scales linearly with --workers up to os.cpu_count(); 500 Jenkinsfiles processed in under 5 minutes on an 8-core workstation based on 500ms per-file average measured on declarative pipeline corpus. PyInstaller binary build is free on GitHub Actions Ubuntu runners.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-3): jenkinsfile_parser.py with lark EBNF grammar covering declarative pipeline agents, stages, steps, environment, and post blocks; 40 Jenkinsfile fixture corpus for parser unit tests; gha_mapper.py with initial construct_support_matrix.json covering 40 common Jenkins step types. Phase 2 (weeks 4-6): batch_runner.py with multiprocessing.Pool; repository_inventories CSV/JSON input and migration_report output; reporter.py for JSON and Markdown summaries; fetch-inventory subcommand via GitHub API. Phase 3 (weeks 7-8): PyInstaller release workflow for four platforms; Homebrew tap; construct_support_matrix.json expanded to 60+ step types based on real Jenkinsfile corpus analysis.
target_output.development_guidelines: jenkinsfile_parser.py and gha_mapper.py must have independent test suites with >80% line coverage enforced in CI. New Jenkins step type mappings are added to construct_support_matrix.json with a corresponding Jenkinsfile fixture test case. Jenkinsfile content must never appear in any log output regardless of --verbose level. All subprocess calls forbidden in convert and batch paths; parser is pure Python with lark grammar.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Groovy DSL parsing coverage is the primary risk: Jenkins shared libraries and scripted pipeline syntax (node/stage/sh blocks outside declarative structure) cannot be parsed by the declarative lark grammar. Mitigation: detect scripted syntax by presence of 'node {' or 'pipeline' absence in first 10 lines and emit full MIGRATOR-TODO output rather than partial conversion. PyInstaller binary size risk if transitive dependencies include native extensions; mitigation is '--exclude-module' for numpy and pandas which must never be added as dependencies. Multiprocessing on Windows uses spawn instead of fork, requiring all shared state to be picklable; construct_support_matrix is loaded once per worker to avoid repeated disk I/O.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Shared library import steps (via groovyScript or load()) are detected by pattern matching and emitted as MIGRATOR-TODO unsupported_construct_markers rather than attempted inline expansion. Scripted pipeline syntax is explicitly out of scope for v1.x and will be documented in the README with detection behavior. Repository inventory format supports both CSV (repo,jenkinsfile_path,branch) and JSON array for flexibility in downstream automation integration.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision for Jenkins-to-GHA migrator CLI. Addresses ISS-001 (batch conversion mode absent) and ISS-002 (AST schema underspecified). Plan is weak quality: all mandatory sections present but several are thinner than ideal, particularly observability and cost_and_scaling.

profile.domain: SLSA Level 3 Supply Chain Compliance Automation Platform for Internal Build Pipelines

project_goal.value: Automation platform that enforces SLSA Level 3 provenance requirements across all internal build pipelines: attesting build artifacts via Cloud KMS signatures, verifying attestations at deployment gates, and generating CycloneDX SBOM reports for all production components
project_goal.rationale: Security audit identified supply chain compromise risk from unverified artifacts reaching production; SLSA Level 3 compliance required by board security mandate

target_users.value: Security engineering team manages attestation_policies; platform engineers integrate pipeline plugins; release managers monitor deployment gate decisions and break-glass exceptions
target_users.rationale: Three distinct personas with different interaction modes identified in stakeholder discovery

project_class.rationale: Backend automation service with CI/CD plugin integrations, batch SBOM generation jobs, and deployment gate enforcement; no end-user browser UI

capabilities.rationale: Attestation REST API, pipeline plugin integrations for GitHub Actions/Jenkins/Tekton, and weekly SBOM batch worker

complexity_level.rationale: Cloud KMS signing architecture, Workload Identity Federation, fail-closed gate check, multi-pipeline plugin SDK, and compliance audit trail require careful design

risk_level.rationale: Gate service controls production deployments; KMS key mismanagement or gate logic errors can block all releases or allow compromised artifacts

data_sensitivity.rationale: build_attestations, slsa_provenance_records, and sbom_reports contain internal component inventories and build infrastructure topology

external_exposure.rationale: Attestation API and gate service accessible only from internal VPC; pipeline plugins use Workload Identity Federation with no external key distribution

access_model.value: Workload Identity Federation for pipeline plugins (no long-lived service account keys); internal service accounts for gate service and SBOM worker; security team uses service account JSON restricted to attestation_policies CRUD
access_model.rationale: WIF eliminates credential rotation burden; separate roles enforce least-privilege between attestation write and policy management

feature_scope.value: POST /v1/attestations for build attestation storage with KMS signing, POST /v1/gates/check for deployment gate verification, GET /v1/attestations/{digest} for provenance retrieval, weekly CycloneDX SBOM batch job, attestation_policies CRUD, pipeline plugin SDK for GitHub Actions/Jenkins/Tekton, deployment_gates audit log
feature_scope.rationale: Complete SLSA Level 3 enforcement lifecycle from build attestation through deployment gate to SBOM audit

mvp_scope.value: Attestation API with KMS signing, gate check service for GitHub Actions pipelines only, PostgreSQL persistence, deployment_gates audit log; SBOM worker and Jenkins/Tekton plugins deferred to phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: GitHub Actions is 80% of internal pipeline volume; phased rollout prevents deployment disruption during attestation collection period

security_baseline.value: Cloud KMS for all signing and verification (private key material never in application memory); INSERT-only PostgreSQL grants on build_attestations and deployment_gates; WIF for pipeline authentication; fail-closed gate check (deny on KMS timeout or service unavailability); break-glass procedure with PagerDuty alert
security_baseline.rationale: SLSA Level 3 requires non-forgeable provenance requiring HSM-backed signing; fail-closed is mandatory security posture for supply chain enforcement

frontend_stack.rationale: No browser UI; management via REST API and CLI plugin SDK only

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin for attestation API and gate service; Go CronJob for SBOM batch worker
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low-latency gate check response required for deployment pipeline SLO; strong GCP SDK support; memory safety for KMS operations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for build_attestations, slsa_provenance_records, sbom_reports catalog, attestation_policies, deployment_gates, signing_keys, pipeline_integrations; Cloud Storage for sbom-reports artifacts
data_platform.rationale: Relational model required for attestation policy joins and audit queries; Cloud SQL managed HA with automated failover

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1 with HPA for attestation-api and gate-service; Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with HA standby; Cloud KMS multi-region key ring
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot eliminates node management; multi-region KMS ensures signing availability across GCP regional incidents

privacy_retention_policy.value: build_attestations and deployment_gates: 7-year retention with PostgreSQL partitioning by year and Cloud SQL automated backup; sbom_reports GCS: STANDARD 90 days, NEARLINE 90-365 days, COLDLINE 1-3 years, deleted at 3 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: SOC 2 Type II and internal security policy require 7-year provenance audit trail; SBOM reports are operational artifacts with shorter compliance window

future_scope.value: SLSA Level 4 support with hermetic build verification, Jira ticket creation for policy violations, self-service attestation_policy management UI, cross-organization provenance federation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 roadmap pending SLSA Level 3 adoption across all pipelines

constraints.value: Gate check endpoint must respond within 2 seconds P99; attestation API must handle 500 attestations/hour at peak build load; no long-lived service account JSON keys in pipeline integrations
constraints.rationale: 2s gate check limit prevents deployment pipeline timeout; 500/hour covers peak build burst during release windows; WIF eliminates key rotation risk

observability_baseline.value: Prometheus metrics on /metrics for attestation_generation_duration_seconds, gate_check_duration_seconds, gate_check_result_total; Grafana dashboards; PagerDuty alerts for P99 gate check >1.8s and error rate >1%
observability_baseline.rationale: Gate check latency SLO requires sub-second alerting; attestation throughput metrics needed for capacity planning

execution_preference.value: 4-week sprints with phased rollout: attestation collection mode first (gate enforces allow-with-warning), then enforcement mode after 90% pipeline coverage
execution_preference.rationale: Phased rollout prevents deployment disruptions during onboarding; 90% coverage threshold ensures no critical pipeline is missed before gate enforcement

compliance_context.value: SLSA Level 3 provenance requirements (CNCF specification v1.0), NIST SP 800-218 SSDF, SOC 2 Type II change management controls requiring attributable build provenance for all production deployments
compliance_context.rationale: Board security mandate requires SLSA Level 3 compliance; SOC 2 audit requires provenance records for all production artifact deployments

project.goal: Enforce SLSA Level 3 provenance across internal build pipelines via attestation storage, gate check, and SBOM generation
project.target_users: Security engineering, platform engineers, release managers

backend.signing: Cloud KMS for SLSA predicate v1.0 signature generation and verification

security.auth: Workload Identity Federation for pipeline plugins; service accounts for internal services
security.signing: Cloud KMS HSM-backed keys; private key material never in application memory
security.gate_behavior: fail-closed on KMS timeout or service unavailability

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with HA

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with go test -race, golangci-lint, gosec

constraints.auth_model: no long-lived service account JSON keys

reasoner_reviews.product: SLSA Enforcer addresses a critical supply chain security gap: without provenance attestation, any artifact in the delivery pipeline could be substituted by a compromised build step. The phased rollout strategy collecting attestations before enforcing gates is the right approach to avoid deployment disruptions. The SBOM batch worker is a strong differentiator for SOC 2 audit readiness since it produces the component inventory auditors require without manual effort.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Correctly separating the attestation generation path (async, CI plugin) from the gate check path (synchronous, deployment blocker) is the key architectural decision. Cloud KMS for signing is mandatory for SLSA Level 3 non-forgeable provenance; the 50-100ms KMS verify latency is well within the 2s gate check SLO. The 1,500ms KMS timeout with fail-closed behavior is the correct tradeoff over fail-open for a supply chain enforcement system.
reasoner_reviews.security: WIF for pipeline authentication eliminates the long-lived credential rotation problem. INSERT-only PostgreSQL grants on build_attestations and deployment_gates enforce immutability at the database layer. Break-glass procedure with PagerDuty escalation is required for operational situations where gate enforcement would block a critical security patch deployment.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GKE Autopilot HPA scaling from 3 to 20 replicas handles 500 attestations/hour peak. Cloud SQL HA ensures gate check database availability. Multi-region KMS key ring addresses regional incident risk for the 2s gate check SLO.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The SBOM worker dependency resolution is the highest implementation risk: mapping artifact digests to full dependency trees requires accurate provenance chain data from all build pipeline integrations. Phase 1 must validate the provenance data quality before committing to SBOM accuracy guarantees.

backend.service_design: Three independent Go services: attestation-api (Gin, stores build_attestations and slsa_provenance_records, calls KMS sign), gate-service (Gin, synchronous gate check with KMS verify, writes deployment_gates), sbom-worker (CronJob, generates CycloneDX SBOM from provenance chain)
backend.api_patterns: REST API with versioned endpoints under /v1; all responses include request_id header for distributed tracing correlation; gate check endpoint designed for sub-second P99 with KMS verify as the primary latency driver
backend.background_jobs: sbom-worker CronJob runs weekly at 02:00 UTC: queries build_attestations for production artifacts deployed in the past week, resolves dependency graph from slsa_provenance_records, generates CycloneDX 1.4 XML and JSON, uploads to Cloud Storage sbom-reports/{date}/{artifact_name}.cdx.json, updates sbom_reports catalog table
backend.failure_handling: Gate service: KMS timeout (1,500ms) returns deny with reason 'kms_timeout' (fail-closed); Cloud SQL connection pool exhaustion returns deny; all deny decisions written to deployment_gates audit table. Attestation API: KMS sign failure returns 503 with retry-after header; Cloud SQL write failure returns 503; idempotency via artifact_digest+builder_id unique constraint prevents duplicate attestations on retry.
backend.scaling_notes: attestation-api HPA on CPU 70% and custom metric attestation_queue_depth; gate-service HPA on CPU 60% to prioritize low-latency responses; both services use Cloud SQL connection pooler (pgBouncer sidecar) to prevent connection exhaustion at 20 replicas

security.auth_design: Pipeline plugins exchange OIDC token from WIF token endpoint for short-lived GCP access token scoped to attestation API service account. Gate service uses dedicated GCP service account with roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyDecrypterViewer. SBOM worker uses separate service account with read-only Cloud SQL access and Cloud Storage write access to sbom-reports bucket.
security.secrets_management: Cloud KMS key ring 'slsa-signing' with regional keys per pipeline_integration; key references stored in signing_keys table (KMS resource name only, no key material). Database credentials in Google Secret Manager, mounted via External Secrets Operator as Kubernetes Secrets. No secrets in environment variables or ConfigMaps.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: deployment_gates table is INSERT-only (no UPDATE/DELETE grants to slsa_app role); Cloud Audit Logs enabled for Cloud KMS sign and verify API calls; break-glass insertions to deployment_gates with break_glass=true flag trigger PagerDuty alert via Cloud Monitoring notification channel
security.incident_response_notes: Gate service failure (unhealthy): deployment pipelines receive deny responses and must escalate to release manager for break-glass approval. KMS key compromise: rotate key in KMS (new signing_key_id), existing attestations with old key remain valid for historical audit; new attestations use rotated key per updated attestation_policies.

data.entities: build_attestations, slsa_provenance_records, sbom_reports, attestation_policies, deployment_gates, signing_keys, pipeline_integrations
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL us-central1 with HA standby replica. SLSA audit tables partitioned by created_at year for efficient 7-year retention management. Cloud Storage bucket sbom-reports with lifecycle policy: STANDARD to NEARLINE at 90 days, NEARLINE to COLDLINE at 365 days, delete at 3 years.
data.schema_notes: build_attestations: id UUID PK, artifact_digest CHAR(71), artifact_name TEXT, builder_id TEXT, slsa_level SMALLINT, signature BYTEA, predicate_json JSONB, signing_key_id TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), UNIQUE(artifact_digest, builder_id). deployment_gates: id UUID PK, artifact_digest CHAR(71), target_environment TEXT, policy_version INT, decision TEXT CHECK (IN 'allow','deny','break_glass'), reason TEXT, gate_check_duration_ms INT, break_glass BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). attestation_policies: id UUID PK, environment TEXT UNIQUE, required_slsa_level SMALLINT, approved_builder_ids TEXT[], policy_version SERIAL, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT.
data.retention_and_deletion: build_attestations and deployment_gates: 7-year retention via annual partitioning; old partitions archived to Cloud SQL export to Cloud Storage before DROP. sbom_reports GCS: automatic lifecycle transitions; sbom_reports catalog table rows soft-deleted when GCS object expires.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1. attestation-api Deployment: 3 replicas minimum, HPA max 20, PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable=2, pod anti-affinity across zones. gate-service Deployment: 3 replicas minimum, HPA max 10, PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable=2. sbom-worker CronJob: weekly schedule, single pod, no HPA. Cloud SQL HA primary in us-central1 with standby replica. Cloud KMS multi-region key ring covering us-central1 and us-east1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: 'go test ./... -race' for all services, golangci-lint and gosec static analysis, 'go build' for binary verification, container image build pushed to Artifact Registry, Helm chart render and kubeval validation, integration tests against Cloud SQL Auth Proxy in CI. Deploy to dev on main merge; staging deploy on release branch; production deploy via manual approval gate in GitHub Environments.
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous release revision on failed health check. Database schema migrations use golang-migrate with up/down files; rollback applies down migration before Helm rollback. gate-service rollback is safe since deployment_gates is append-only.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics exposed on :9090/metrics for each service. Grafana dashboards: attestation throughput, gate check P50/P95/P99 latency, gate decision breakdown (allow/deny/break_glass), SBOM generation success rate. Cloud Logging for structured JSON logs with trace_id correlation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a SLSA Level 3 compliance automation platform with three distinct operational paths: async attestation generation via CI plugins, synchronous fail-closed gate check, and weekly SBOM batch generation. Cloud KMS is mandatory for non-forgeable signatures; private key material must never reach application memory. The fail-closed gate check design is the highest-stakes decision: KMS timeout returns deny, not allow. WIF eliminates long-lived credential distribution risk across pipeline integrations. PostgreSQL INSERT-only grants enforce immutability at the database layer. Phased rollout (collection before enforcement) prevents deployment disruption during the onboarding period.
target_output.title: SLSA Enforcer: Supply Chain Compliance Automation Platform
target_output.executive_summary: SLSA Enforcer is a Go 1.22 automation platform on GKE Autopilot that enforces SLSA Level 3 provenance requirements across all internal build pipelines. CI pipeline plugins authenticate via Workload Identity Federation and call the attestation API to store build_attestations signed with Cloud KMS. Deployment systems call the gate-service to verify attestation signatures before promoting artifacts; the gate is fail-closed and returns deny on KMS timeout or service unavailability. A weekly CronJob generates CycloneDX SBOMs from stored provenance records for SOC 2 audit readiness. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL stores all compliance records with INSERT-only grants enforcing audit trail immutability.
target_output.architecture_overview: SLSA Enforcer is structured around three independent operational paths with distinct latency and availability profiles. The attestation generation path is asynchronous: CI pipeline plugins authenticate via Workload Identity Federation, invoke POST /v1/attestations to store build_attestations with Cloud KMS signatures, and receive attestation_id confirmation. The gate check path is synchronous and blocking: deployment systems call POST /v1/gates/check with artifact_digest and target_environment; the gate-service fetches the build_attestation, retrieves the attestation_policy for the environment, calls Cloud KMS verify_signature with a 1,500ms timeout, evaluates the SLSA level requirement, writes a deployment_gates audit record, and returns allow or deny. The SBOM generation path is scheduled: a weekly CronJob queries build_attestations for production artifacts, resolves dependency trees from slsa_provenance_records, generates CycloneDX 1.4 XML/JSON, uploads to the sbom-reports Cloud Storage bucket, and updates the sbom_reports catalog table. All three services share the same PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL instance but use separate service accounts with distinct IAM bindings.
target_output.technology_stack: Go 1.22 with Gin 1.9.x for attestation-api and gate-service. Go CronJob binary for sbom-worker. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with high-availability standby replica. Google Cloud KMS with HSM-backed key ring 'slsa-signing'. Workload Identity Federation for pipeline plugin authentication. External Secrets Operator for Secret Manager to Kubernetes Secret sync. Prometheus client_golang for metrics. golang-migrate for schema migrations. Helm 3 for Kubernetes deployment. GitHub Actions for CI/CD. Artifact Registry for container images. Cloud Storage for sbom-reports artifacts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Attestation generation: pipeline plugin calls POST /v1/attestations with artifact_digest, builder_id, slsa_level, build_inputs_hash; API generates SLSA predicate v1.0, calls Cloud KMS sign, stores build_attestation and slsa_provenance_record, returns attestation_id. Gate check: deployment system calls POST /v1/gates/check with artifact_digest and target_environment; gate-service verifies KMS signature, evaluates attestation_policy, writes deployment_gates record, returns {decision: allow|deny, reason, gate_id}. Provenance retrieval: GET /v1/attestations/{digest} returns build_attestation and slsa_provenance_record. Policy management: GET/PUT /v1/policies/{environment} for security team to manage required_slsa_level and approved_builder_ids. SBOM generation: weekly CronJob produces CycloneDX SBOM per production artifact; GET /v1/sbom/{artifact_name}/latest returns download URL. Break-glass: POST /v1/gates/break-glass creates signed exception in deployment_gates with break_glass=true and triggers PagerDuty alert.
target_output.system_components: attestation-api (Go/Gin): handles build_attestation storage, slsa_provenance_record creation, Cloud KMS sign calls, policy CRUD; 3-20 replicas with HPA. gate-service (Go/Gin): synchronous gate check with Cloud KMS verify (1,500ms timeout, fail-closed), deployment_gates audit writes; 3-10 replicas with HPA on CPU 60%. sbom-worker (Go CronJob): weekly CycloneDX generation from provenance chains, Cloud Storage upload, sbom_reports catalog update. plugin-sdk: Go module and GitHub Actions reusable action implementing WIF token exchange and POST /v1/attestations call for GitHub Actions; Jenkins shared library and Tekton Task for other pipeline types. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with pgBouncer sidecar connection pooler. Cloud KMS key ring 'slsa-signing' with per-environment signing keys. External Secrets Operator syncing Secret Manager credentials to Kubernetes Secrets.
target_output.workflows: Build attestation workflow: at CI build completion, the GitHub Actions plugin exchanges the OIDC token from the WIF token endpoint for a short-lived GCP access token scoped to the attestation-api service account; calls POST /v1/attestations with {artifact_digest: 'sha256:abc123...', artifact_name: 'api-service:v2.1.4', builder_id: 'principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/...', slsa_level: 3, build_inputs_hash: 'sha256:def456...', build_trigger: 'push/main'}; attestation-api generates SLSA predicate v1.0 JSON, calls Cloud KMS projects/PROJECT/locations/global/keyRings/slsa-signing/cryptoKeys/KEY:asymmetricSign with the predicate digest, stores build_attestation row with signature BYTEA and predicate_json JSONB, stores slsa_provenance_record with full provenance chain, returns {attestation_id: UUID, status: 'stored'}. Deployment gate workflow: release pipeline calls POST /v1/gates/check with {artifact_digest: 'sha256:abc123...', target_environment: 'production'}; gate-service queries build_attestations for the digest, fetches attestation_policy for 'production' requiring slsa_level >= 3 and builder_id in approved_builder_ids; calls Cloud KMS asymmetricVerify with stored signature and policy-approved key reference; if verify returns valid and policy passes, inserts deployment_gates row with decision='allow'; if KMS times out at 1,500ms or returns error, inserts decision='deny' with reason='kms_timeout' and returns HTTP 200 {decision: 'deny'} (fail-closed, not 503).
target_output.data_model: build_attestations: id UUID PK, artifact_digest CHAR(71) UNIQUE (sha256: prefix + 64 hex), artifact_name TEXT, builder_id TEXT (WIF principalSet URI), slsa_level SMALLINT CHECK (1-4), signature BYTEA (Cloud KMS ECDSA P-384 signature), predicate_json JSONB (SLSA predicate v1.0: buildType, builder.id, invocation, buildConfig, metadata.buildStartedOn/buildFinishedOn), signing_key_id TEXT (KMS resource name), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). UNIQUE constraint on (artifact_digest, builder_id). slsa_provenance_records: id UUID PK, attestation_id UUID FK build_attestations.id, build_inputs JSONB (array of {uri, digest} for source materials), dependencies JSONB (resolved package graph from build metadata), provenance_version TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. deployment_gates: id UUID PK, artifact_digest CHAR(71), target_environment TEXT, policy_version INT, decision TEXT CHECK (IN 'allow','deny','break_glass'), reason TEXT, gate_check_duration_ms INT, break_glass BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, checked_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(). attestation_policies: id UUID PK, environment TEXT UNIQUE, required_slsa_level SMALLINT, approved_builder_ids TEXT[], policy_version SERIAL, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_by TEXT. sbom_reports: id UUID PK, artifact_digest CHAR(71), artifact_name TEXT, cyclonedx_version TEXT, component_count INT, storage_uri TEXT (GCS path), generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ. signing_keys: id UUID PK, key_name TEXT (KMS resource name), environment TEXT, active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. pipeline_integrations: id UUID PK, name TEXT, pipeline_type TEXT CHECK (IN 'github_actions','jenkins','tekton'), wif_principal TEXT, active BOOLEAN, registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ.
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/attestations — store build_attestation; body: {artifact_digest, artifact_name, builder_id, slsa_level, build_inputs_hash, build_trigger}; auth: WIF-derived Bearer token; responds 201 with {attestation_id, status: 'stored'}. GET /v1/attestations/{digest} — retrieve build_attestation and slsa_provenance_record by artifact SHA-256 digest; responds 200 with full attestation object or 404. POST /v1/gates/check — synchronous gate check; body: {artifact_digest, target_environment}; auth: internal service account Bearer token; responds 200 with {decision: 'allow'|'deny'|'break_glass', reason, gate_id, duration_ms}; never returns 5xx (fail-closed returns allow=deny). GET /v1/policies/{environment} — retrieve attestation_policy; PUT /v1/policies/{environment} — update policy; auth: security-team service account. POST /v1/gates/break-glass — create signed exception; body: {artifact_digest, target_environment, justification, approver_id}; responds 201 with gate_id; triggers PagerDuty alert. GET /v1/sbom/{artifact_name}/latest — returns {storage_uri, component_count, generated_at}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cloud KMS manages all signing_keys; the attestation-api calls KMS asymmetricSign and the gate-service calls asymmetricVerify via Cloud KMS API; private key material is never retrieved or present in application memory, satisfying SLSA Level 3 non-forgeable provenance requirement. Workload Identity Federation replaces long-lived service account JSON keys in pipeline_integrations: each registered pipeline binds a WIF principalSet URI to a GCP service account; tokens expire in 1 hour with automatic refresh by the plugin SDK. PostgreSQL role 'slsa_app' is granted INSERT-only on build_attestations and deployment_gates and SELECT-only on attestation_policies; no UPDATE or DELETE grants; row-level security on build_attestations prevents cross-tenant reads where tenants are pipeline_integration ids. Cloud Audit Logs enabled for Cloud KMS sign and verify API calls providing a non-repudiable record of every signing operation. Break-glass insertions with break_glass=true trigger a Cloud Monitoring alert forwarded to PagerDuty with artifact_digest and approver_id for immediate security team notification. All intra-service communication within the GKE cluster uses mTLS via Istio service mesh.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster in us-central1. attestation-api Deployment: 3 replicas minimum, HPA scaling to 20 replicas on CPU 70% and custom Prometheus metric attestation_queue_depth, PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable=2, pod anti-affinity topology key kubernetes.io/hostname across zones. gate-service Deployment: 3 replicas minimum, HPA max 10 replicas on CPU 60% (lower threshold to prioritize P99 gate check latency), PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable=2. sbom-worker CronJob: schedule '0 2 * * 0' (weekly Sunday 02:00 UTC), concurrencyPolicy Forbid, single pod with Cloud SQL Auth Proxy sidecar. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: 'go test ./... -race' (all three services), golangci-lint, gosec static analysis, container image build pushed to Artifact Registry with SHA256 digest tag, Helm chart kubeval validation, integration tests against Cloud SQL Auth Proxy in CI environment. Deploy to dev on main branch merge; staging deploy on release branch; production deploy requires manual approval in GitHub Environments with two-approver requirement. Rollback: 'helm rollback slsa-enforcer {revision}'; golang-migrate down migration applied first for schema rollback.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics exposed on :9090/metrics for each service. attestation-api metrics: attestation_generation_duration_seconds histogram (p50/p95/p99 per pipeline_integration.pipeline_type), attestation_store_errors_total counter by error_type. gate-service metrics: gate_check_duration_seconds histogram (p99 SLO target 2s), gate_check_result_total counter with labels {decision=allow|deny|break_glass, environment}, kms_verify_duration_seconds histogram for isolating KMS latency contribution. sbom-worker metrics: sbom_artifacts_processed_total, sbom_generation_errors_total, sbom_batch_duration_seconds. Grafana dashboards per service with SLO burn rate panels. PagerDuty alerts: gate check P99 >1,800ms (15-minute burn rate), attestation error rate >1% (5-minute window), break-glass event (immediate). Cloud Logging for structured JSON logs with trace_id and request_id for cross-service correlation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: GCP monthly estimate at steady state: GKE Autopilot ~$1,200 (3-20 pods per service, scales to low utilization off-peak), Cloud SQL HA PostgreSQL 16 ~$400 (db-standard-4 with HA standby), Cloud KMS ~$15 (500 attestations/hour * 730 hours = 365,000 sign operations at $0.03/10k + verify operations), Cloud Storage sbom-reports ~$50 (growing ~1GB/month), Secret Manager ~$10. Total ~$1,675/month. Scaling ceiling: 500 attestations/hour requires ~3 attestation-api replicas at current Go handler performance; HPA allows burst to 20 replicas for 3,000+ attestations/hour during peak release windows without configuration changes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (weeks 1-4): Core attestation API with build_attestations and slsa_provenance_records schema, Cloud KMS signing integration, POST /v1/attestations and GET /v1/attestations/{digest} endpoints, GitHub Actions plugin SDK with WIF token exchange, PostgreSQL migrations, GKE Autopilot deployment for attestation-api only in collection mode (no gate enforcement). Phase 2 (weeks 5-8): gate-service with fail-closed KMS verify, attestation_policies CRUD, deployment_gates schema, POST /v1/gates/check endpoint, break-glass procedure and PagerDuty integration; enable gate enforcement for staging environment only. Phase 3 (weeks 9-12): sbom-worker CronJob with CycloneDX generation, Jenkins shared library plugin, Tekton Task plugin, production gate enforcement after 90% pipeline coverage threshold confirmed. Phase 4 (weeks 13-16): multi-environment policy management, Grafana dashboards, SLO alerting, SOC 2 audit evidence package generation.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Cloud KMS operations must use context-aware calls with explicit 5-second context deadline to prevent gate check latency blowout from unbounded KMS waits. PostgreSQL schema migrations use golang-migrate with sequential up/down files; no raw DDL in application code; migrations run as a separate Kubernetes Job before Deployment rollout. The 'slsa_app' database role INSERT-only constraint must be verified in integration tests by asserting that UPDATE and DELETE statements return permission denied errors. Gate check handler must never return HTTP 5xx; all error paths return HTTP 200 with decision='deny' and a reason string to ensure deployment pipelines receive a usable gate decision regardless of internal service state. Provenance predicate JSON must conform to SLSA predicate v1.0 schema; schema validation test runs on every build.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Cloud KMS latency is the primary gate check risk: verify operations typically complete in 50-100ms but GCP regional incidents can push P99 above 500ms. Mitigation: gate service KMS timeout is 1,500ms returning deny (fail-closed); this is the correct security tradeoff over fail-open even during GCP incidents. SBOM dependency resolution accuracy risk: the sbom-worker resolves dependency graphs from slsa_provenance_records which depend on build pipelines reporting accurate build_inputs; if a pipeline omits transitive dependencies from build_inputs_hash, the SBOM will be incomplete. Mitigation: SBOM reports include a completeness_score field derived from provenance chain depth. WIF adoption risk: teams accustomed to long-lived service account JSON keys may resist WIF migration. Mitigation: phased onboarding starting with GitHub Actions (WIF native support) before Jenkins and Tekton integrations. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over Spanner sacrifices global multi-region writes for simpler schema management and lower cost at the current attestation volume.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Gate check fail-closed behavior is confirmed: when the attestation registry or KMS is unavailable, the gate service returns deny to block deployment rather than failing open. This is the correct security posture for a SLSA Level 3 enforcement system where allowing an unverified artifact is worse than a deployment delay. Policy exception handling during the transition period uses break-glass with PagerDuty escalation rather than automatic allow, ensuring all exceptions are documented in the deployment_gates audit trail. SBOM generation scope is confirmed as production artifacts only (slsa_level >= 3 attestations) to keep CycloneDX component counts accurate and auditor-meaningful.

metadata.notes: Round 1 first_pass for SLSA Level 3 supply chain compliance automation. Strong quality with rich domain-specific content across all plan sections, complete seven-entity data model, Cloud KMS signing architecture, fail-closed gate check design, and phased rollout from collection mode to enforcement mode.

profile.domain: A security-hardened TypeScript SDK for interacting with internal CI/CD pipeline APIs, providing type-safe request builders, automatic token refresh, HMAC request signing, and structured error handling with retry logic. Published to a private npm registry and consumed by dozens of internal automation scripts.

project_goal.value: Publish a type-safe TypeScript SDK for internal CI/CD pipeline APIs with HMAC request signing, automatic token refresh, and structured retry logic
project_goal.rationale: Centralise CI/CD API interaction into a versioned, tested SDK to eliminate ad-hoc fetch calls across 50+ automation scripts

target_users.value: Internal platform engineering teams and automation script authors consuming CI/CD pipeline APIs
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in internal developer survey

project_class.rationale: Published npm package with exported API surface, not a hosted runtime

capabilities.rationale: SDK provides typed HTTP client and token management; no frontend or database

complexity_level.rationale: HMAC signing, token refresh, and retry policies require careful state management

risk_level.rationale: SDK handles internal API tokens and HMAC signing keys; compromise exposes CI/CD pipeline control

data_sensitivity.rationale: Tokens and signing keys are internal credentials; no customer PII involved

external_exposure.rationale: SDK is published to private Artifactory registry; not publicly available

access_model.value: Bearer JWT tokens with automatic refresh via configured refresh endpoint; HMAC signing applied per-request
access_model.rationale: CI/CD API uses JWT auth with HMAC signature for request integrity

feature_scope.value: PipelineClient with typed request builders for all pipeline API endpoints, TokenStore with automatic refresh, HMAC signer, RetryPolicy with exponential backoff and jitter, structured ErrorTypes, response validators
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set from API consumer requirements

mvp_scope.value: PipelineClient core, TokenStore with refresh, HMAC signing, basic retry with 3 attempts, typed error classes
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum set to replace ad-hoc fetch calls in 10 highest-traffic automation scripts

security_baseline.value: HMAC-SHA256 signing over method+path+timestamp, token storage abstracted behind TokenStore interface, no credentials logged
security_baseline.rationale: SOC 2 Type II requires secure credential handling and audit trail

frontend_stack.rationale: SDK is a Node/Bun-compatible package with no browser UI

backend_stack.value: TypeScript (Bun runtime for tests and build)
backend_stack.rationale: Type-safe SDK compiled to ESM/CJS with Bun test runner

data_platform.value: No persistent storage; in-memory TokenStore with pluggable backend interface
data_platform.rationale: SDK is a stateless library; consumers provide token persistence if needed

hosting_target.value: Private npm registry (Artifactory) on AWS
hosting_target.rationale: All internal packages published to Artifactory for version control and access management

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data persisted by SDK; token TTLs governed by CI/CD API issuer
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Library does not store or process personal data

future_scope.value: gRPC transport support, OpenTelemetry trace propagation, mock server for consumer integration tests
future_scope.rationale: Planned v2 features after SDK adoption stabilises

constraints.value: Must support Node 20+ and Bun 1.x, zero runtime dependencies outside Node built-ins, compiled output under 50KB gzipped
constraints.rationale: Automation scripts run in minimal CI containers; heavy SDKs cause container bloat

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON log output via pluggable logger interface; each retry attempt logged with request_id, attempt_number, and error code
observability_baseline.rationale: Consuming scripts aggregate SDK logs into their own observability pipelines

execution_preference.value: Semantic versioning with CHANGELOG, breaking changes gated behind major version bumps
execution_preference.rationale: 50+ consumers require predictable compatibility guarantees

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II: secure credential handling, no sensitive data in logs, access control for registry publishing
compliance_context.rationale: Organisation holds SOC 2 Type II certification; SDK handling internal tokens must comply

project.goal: Type-safe TypeScript SDK for CI/CD pipeline APIs with HMAC signing and token refresh
project.target_users: Internal platform teams and automation script authors

backend.runtime: TypeScript compiled to ESM and CJS

security.credentials: never logged, stored behind TokenStore interface

data.persistence: in-memory by default; pluggable TokenStore interface for Redis or file-backed storage

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: lint, type-check, test, publish to Artifactory

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK scope is correctly bounded around the CI/CD API surface. Zero runtime dependencies is an aggressive but correct constraint for container-optimised automation environments. The pluggable TokenStore interface is essential for teams that need Redis-backed token sharing across parallel CI workers.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: HMAC-SHA256 signing with clock skew tolerance must be explicitly specified; CI agents on different machines may have up to 60s clock drift. The retry policy needs to distinguish retryable (503, 429) from non-retryable (401, 403, 404) status codes. ESM/CJS dual output via bun build is the correct approach for Node 20+ consumers.
reasoner_reviews.security: Token refresh must avoid concurrent refresh races when multiple in-flight requests detect a 401 simultaneously; a refresh mutex or promise-dedup pattern is required. HMAC key rotation should be handled by the KeyStore abstraction without requiring SDK version bump.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero runtime dependencies is achievable for HMAC-SHA256 using Node crypto built-in. Bundle size under 50KB is feasible with tree-shaking via bun build. Bun test runner with coverage supports the required 90% branch coverage target.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 2 plan still lacked a concrete refresh mutex design. Two concurrent requests both hitting 401 would trigger two parallel refresh calls, potentially revoking the first-issued token before it is used.

backend.service_design: SDK exports: PipelineClient (main entry point), createClient factory, TokenStore interface, HmacSigner class, RetryPolicy class, ErrorTypes (PipelineError, AuthError, RateLimitError, NetworkError). All exported from index.ts as named exports.
backend.api_patterns: PipelineClient wraps fetch with a pipeline: validate token (TokenStore.get) -> refresh if expiring (deduplicated via Promise mutex) -> sign request (HmacSigner.sign) -> execute -> parse response -> throw typed error on failure. Request builders for GET /pipelines, POST /pipelines/{id}/trigger, GET /pipelines/{id}/runs, GET /runs/{id}/logs.
backend.failure_handling: RetryPolicy.execute wraps requests with configurable maxAttempts (default 3) and backoff: attempt * baseDelayMs + jitter. Retryable status codes: 429 (honour Retry-After header), 503, 502. Non-retryable: 400, 401, 403, 404. NetworkError (fetch throws) is always retried.
backend.scaling_notes: SDK is stateless per-instance. Consumers running in parallel CI workers should share a Redis-backed TokenStore to avoid duplicate refresh calls across workers. Default in-memory TokenStore is safe for single-process use.

security.auth_design: TokenStore interface: get(): Promise<Token>, store(token: Token): Promise<void>, invalidate(): Promise<void>. HmacSigner.sign(request): adds X-Signature header as HMAC-SHA256(method + path + unix_timestamp_seconds) using subtle.sign from Node crypto. Clock skew tolerance: timestamp checked within ±60s by server.
security.secrets_management: HmacSigner accepts key as Uint8Array to avoid accidental string serialisation. KeyStore interface allows HSM-backed key retrieval. Keys never appear in log output; logger receives sanitised request context.
security.security_risks: Refresh token race: mitigated by Promise mutex (refreshInProgress: Promise | null pattern). HMAC replay: server enforces nonce uniqueness within 2-minute window. Dependency supply chain: zero runtime deps eliminates npm supply chain attack surface.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: lint (eslint + prettier check), type-check (tsc --noEmit), test (bun test --coverage, fail under 90% branch coverage), build (bun build --target node --format esm,cjs), publish (npm publish --registry Artifactory, gated on semver tag).
devops.deployment_topology: Published package in Artifactory under @internal/pipeline-sdk. Semantic versioning enforced by release-please bot. Changelog generated from conventional commits.
devops.devops_risks: Artifactory outage blocks all CI pipelines depending on the SDK. Mitigation: cache SDK tarballs in GitHub Actions cache layer.

data.entities: Token (accessToken: string, expiresAt: number, refreshToken?: string). SigningKey (keyId: string, algorithm: 'hmac-sha256', keyBytes: Uint8Array). RetryContext (requestId: string, attempt: number, lastErrorCode: number, nextDelayMs: number).
data.schema_notes: All entities are TypeScript interfaces, not database schemas. Token expiresAt uses Unix epoch seconds for comparison with Date.now()/1000. SDK ships .d.ts declarations for full IDE type inference.
data.consistency_notes: TokenStore implementations must be atomic on store(); in-memory implementation uses synchronous Map assignment; Redis implementation uses SET NX with TTL matching token expiry.

ISS-001.title: Token refresh race condition when multiple concurrent requests hit 401
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 plans did not address the case where two in-flight requests simultaneously detect token expiry and both trigger refresh, potentially causing the first-issued token to be invalidated by the second refresh.

history.note: Identified in security review
Round 2 plan mentioned token refresh but did not specify mutex pattern

ISS-002.title: HMAC clock skew tolerance not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 omitted clock skew handling; round 2 plan addressed it with +-60s tolerance documented in HmacSigner

history.note: Identified in security review
Round 2 plan specified +-60s tolerance

ISS-003.title: Retry policy does not distinguish retryable from non-retryable HTTP status codes
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan retried all failed requests; round 2 specified retryable status codes list

history.note: Round 2 plan added retryable vs non-retryable code list

focus_issues.detail: Two in-flight requests simultaneously detecting 401 trigger duplicate refresh calls. Requires Promise mutex or deduplication pattern.

Show concrete refreshInProgress: Promise<Token> | null guard in auth_design
Confirm SDK bundle size measurement in build output

Retryable vs non-retryable status code list is complete and correct
