issue_updates.title: Token refresh race condition
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 plan mentioned refresh but did not show deduplication logic

previous_plan.title: Pipeline SDK v1 - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: TypeScript SDK with HMAC signing, token refresh, and structured retry. Round 2 added clock skew tolerance and retryable status code list but did not specify refresh deduplication.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Named exports from index.ts: PipelineClient, TokenStore interface, HmacSigner, RetryPolicy. Dual ESM/CJS output via bun build. Zero runtime dependencies.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HMAC-SHA256 with +-60s clock skew tolerance. Token refresh on 401. SOC 2 compliant logging (no credentials). Refresh race condition not addressed.
previous_plan.api_design: Request builders for GET /pipelines, POST /pipelines/{id}/trigger, GET /runs/{id}/logs. Typed error classes for auth, rate-limit, network failures.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 focuses on resolving ISS-001 (token refresh race condition) by specifying the Promise mutex pattern: TokenManager holds refreshInProgress of type Promise<Token> or null. Any request detecting an expired token checks this field first; if set, it awaits the in-flight refresh rather than triggering a new one. This is the canonical dedup pattern for async token refresh in TypeScript SDKs. Plan quality is moderate per manifest.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified Promise mutex in TokenManager: refreshInProgress field initialised to null. On 401 detection, client checks refreshInProgress; if non-null it awaits the existing promise; if null it sets refreshInProgress = this.executeRefresh() and awaits it, then nulls the field on completion or error. This ensures exactly one refresh call per expiry cycle regardless of concurrent in-flight requests.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Parallel CI workers sharing a TokenManager instance trigger at most one token refresh per expiry cycle, eliminating duplicate refresh calls and token invalidation races.

target_output.title: Internal CI/CD Pipeline SDK v1
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-dependency TypeScript SDK published to the internal Artifactory npm registry, providing typed request builders for CI/CD pipeline API endpoints, HMAC-SHA256 request signing with clock skew tolerance, automatic token refresh with Promise-based deduplication mutex, and structured error handling with configurable retry policies. Consumed by 50+ internal automation scripts and tooling projects.
target_output.architecture_overview: Package-only architecture: no hosted runtime, no database. SDK exports named symbols from index.ts compiled to dual ESM and CJS output via bun build targeting Node 20+ and Bun 1.x. Core pipeline: TokenManager (checks expiry, deduplicates refresh via Promise mutex) -> HmacSigner (HMAC-SHA256 over method+path+timestamp using Node crypto.subtle) -> fetch -> ResponseParser -> typed Error throwing. All types exported as .d.ts declarations for full IDE inference in consuming scripts.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: TypeScript 5.x in strict mode. Build: bun build targeting node, dual ESM/CJS output, bun build --metafile for bundle size verification. Test: bun test with coverage gate at 90% branch coverage. Lint: eslint with typescript-eslint, prettier. Registry: Artifactory private npm under @internal scope. CI: GitHub Actions. Runtime dependencies: zero (Node crypto built-in for HMAC, Node fetch for HTTP).
target_output.functional_feature_map: PipelineClient: main entry point with methods triggerPipeline(id, params), listPipelines(filter), getPipelineRun(runId), getRunLogs(runId, options). TokenManager: token expiry check, refresh deduplication via Promise mutex, pluggable TokenStore backend. HmacSigner: HMAC-SHA256 signature over concatenated method+path+unix_timestamp_seconds, adds X-Pipeline-Signature and X-Pipeline-Timestamp headers. RetryPolicy: configurable maxAttempts and baseDelayMs, exponential backoff with jitter, Retry-After header parsing for 429 responses. ErrorTypes: PipelineError (base), AuthError (401/403), RateLimitError (429 with retryAfterMs), NetworkError (fetch failure), ValidationError (4xx with body).
target_output.system_components: index.ts: named exports barrel. PipelineClient: orchestrates TokenManager, HmacSigner, RetryPolicy, fetch. TokenManager: refreshInProgress mutex field, TokenStore interface (get/store/invalidate), in-memory DefaultTokenStore. HmacSigner: KeyStore interface (getKey(keyId)), DefaultKeyStore (in-memory Uint8Array). RetryPolicy: execute(fn, options) wraps any async function with retry logic. ErrorTypes: typed error class hierarchy. Types: Token, SigningKey, RetryContext, PipelineRun, PipelineLog interfaces.
target_output.workflows: Token Refresh with Deduplication: Request interceptor calls TokenManager.getValidToken(). If token expires within 30s, checks refreshInProgress: if Promise exists, awaits it; if null, sets refreshInProgress = executeRefresh() and awaits. On completion nulls refreshInProgress. All concurrent callers that checked at the same time await the same single refresh Promise. HMAC Request Signing: HmacSigner.sign(method, path) calls KeyStore.getKey(keyId), builds signingString = method.toUpperCase() + path + unixTimestamp, calls crypto.subtle.sign HMAC-SHA256, encodes to hex, sets X-Pipeline-Signature and X-Pipeline-Timestamp headers.
target_output.data_model: Token interface: accessToken string, expiresAt number (Unix epoch seconds), refreshToken optional string, tokenId string. SigningKey interface: keyId string, algorithm literal 'hmac-sha256', keyBytes Uint8Array. RetryContext interface: requestId string, attempt number, lastErrorCode number, nextDelayMs number. PipelineRun interface: runId string, pipelineId string, status ENUM pending/running/success/failed/cancelled, triggeredAt string ISO8601, completedAt optional string. PipelineLog interface: runId string, lines array of LogLine (timestamp string, level ENUM info/warn/error, message string).
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/pipelines?org={orgId}&status={status} -> PipelineListResponse. POST /v1/pipelines/{id}/trigger with TriggerParams body -> PipelineRun. GET /v1/pipelines/{id}/runs?limit=20&cursor={cursor} -> RunListResponse with nextCursor. GET /v1/runs/{runId} -> PipelineRun. GET /v1/runs/{runId}/logs?since={iso8601}&limit=100 -> PipelineLog. All requests include X-Pipeline-Signature, X-Pipeline-Timestamp, Authorization: Bearer {token} headers.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HMAC-SHA256 over method+path+unix_timestamp_seconds using Node crypto.subtle.sign; no third-party crypto libraries. Clock skew tolerance: server accepts +-60s timestamp window; SDK clock is Date.now()/1000. Token refresh mutex: TokenManager.refreshInProgress field is Promise<Token> or null; ensures single refresh per expiry regardless of concurrency. Credentials never passed to logger: HmacSigner receives key as Uint8Array; TokenManager redacts token strings in structured log context. SOC 2 controls: no secrets in logs, Artifactory publish gated behind tag-based GitHub Actions workflow requiring approver review.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: on push to main runs lint (eslint + prettier --check), type-check (tsc --noEmit --strict), test (bun test --coverage, fail under 90% branch), build (bun build --target node --format esm,cjs --metafile, fail if gzipped output over 50KB). On semver tag push: publish to Artifactory via npm publish --registry with ARTIFACTORY_TOKEN secret. release-please bot generates CHANGELOG and bumps package.json version from conventional commits. Breaking changes require major version bump; enforced by release-please config.
target_output.observability: SDK does not emit metrics; consumers integrate their own. Pluggable logger interface: Logger with debug/info/warn/error methods accepting structured context object. Default: no-op logger. Each retry attempt logs: requestId, attempt, statusCode, nextDelayMs. Token refresh logs: tokenId, expiresAt, refreshTrigger (expired/expiring_soon). HMAC signing logs: keyId, path (no key bytes or token values).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting cost; package stored in existing Artifactory instance. Bundle gzipped under 50KB keeps CI container image size impact negligible. Token refresh deduplication reduces CI/CD API rate limit pressure when 100+ parallel workers share a TokenStore.
target_output.phased_implementation: v1.0 (6 weeks): PipelineClient core, TokenManager with refresh mutex, HmacSigner with clock skew, RetryPolicy with retryable codes, typed ErrorTypes, in-memory TokenStore and KeyStore, bun build dual output, Artifactory publish pipeline. v1.1 (4 weeks): Redis-backed TokenStore implementation, OpenTelemetry trace propagation headers, mock pipeline server for consumer integration testing.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode required. All exports documented with TSDoc. Branch coverage gate at 90% enforced in CI. Conventional commits enforced by commitlint. Zero runtime dependencies rule enforced by publint in CI. Semantic versioning with release-please; no force-push to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Artifactory outage blocks all CI pipelines depending on the SDK. Mitigation: cache SDK tarballs in GitHub Actions cache with restore-keys. Tradeoff: zero runtime deps over node-fetch or axios limits redirect handling to native fetch behaviour; documented as known limitation. Risk: HMAC key rotation requires consumers to update KeyStore implementation; KeyStore interface versioned as separate export to allow in-place update without breaking changes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Promise mutex pattern chosen over lock library to maintain zero runtime deps. In-memory DefaultTokenStore selected for v1.0; Redis implementation deferred to v1.1 when multi-worker sharing requirement is confirmed. ESM+CJS dual output confirmed as required by Node 20 and older bundler consumers.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision resolving token refresh race condition ISS-001 for internal CI/CD TypeScript SDK with HMAC signing.
